title: JDA Worldwide hiring Web Developer in Indianapolis, IN | LinkedIn
aboutCompany: JDA Worldwide is a leading advertising and marketing services company dedicated to creating innovative campaigns that drive results for clients across various industries. With a focus on strategic planning and creative execution, we strive to help businesses achieve their marketing objectives with excellence. Connect with us on LinkedIn to stay updated on our latest projects and successes.
jobDescription:
Web Developer (Webflow & No-Code) – JDA Worldwide

​

Join JDA Worldwide, a leading full-service marketing agency since 2003, serving innovative brands nationwide. Based in Indianapolis, IN, our team specializes in creative, digital, brand strategy, paid media, and public relations. We’re passionate about crafting standout digital experiences and supporting our clients’ growth. If you thrive in a collaborative, low-ego, high-talent environment, we want to meet you.

​

About the Role

​

We’re looking for a multi-skilled Web Developer to help elevate our agency and client websites. You’ll play a hands-on role in front-end and back-end web development, leading the charge on new builds, maintenance, and optimization. You’ll also get the chance to work with a range of platforms, including WordPress, Shopify, and no-code tools like Webflow, to bring creative ideas to life.

​

This is an on-site role in Indianapolis, IN — perfect for someone seeking to grow their skill set in a supportive, marketing-driven team. (Some flexibility for hybrid/remote creative jobs may be possible for outstanding candidates.)

​

Key Responsibilities

​

  • Develop, maintain, and enhance dynamic websites and web applications for JDA and our clients
  • Build functional prototypes to gather and implement client feedback early and often
  • Deliver high-performance, standards-compliant solutions across WordPress, Shopify, and Webflow
  • Collaborate with designers and strategists to create seamless UX/UI with a focus on user-path mapping
  • Troubleshoot bugs, optimize web performance, and update existing client sites
  • Ensure projects are delivered on time and within budget
  • Stay on top of web trends, emerging tech, and no-code innovations to infuse projects with fresh ideas
  • Apply object-oriented programming (OOP) practices and weigh technical approaches for budget and timelines

​

What You Bring

​

  • Advanced experience with WordPress (custom plugin and theme development)
  • Proficient in Webflow and other no-code web development platforms
  • Strong knowledge of PHP, HTML5, CSS3/SCSS, JavaScript, and jQuery
  • Experience with Shopify, including custom theme development
  • Ability to implement modern UX/UI concepts and responsive web design best practices
  • Skilled in Figma for collaboration with designers
  • Familiar with React and current web animation techniques
  • Adept at optimizing sites for SEO and accessibility (WCAG, ADA compliance)
  • Understanding of Git and collaborative development workflows, preferably with Git Flow
  • Commitment to cross-browser compatibility and web performance optimization

​

Bonus Points

​

  • Hands-on experience with Hubspot or other CRM integrations
  • Knowledge of MySQL/PostgreSQL and database-driven web applications
  • Awareness of web security standards and Unix command line tools
  • Experience with C#, ASP.NET, and macOS workflow
  • Passion for blending code with creative storytelling in marketing contexts

​

Essential Qualities

​

  • Exceptional attention to detail
  • Drive for excellence and continuous improvement
  • Confident communicator and collaborator
  • Team-first mindset with a positive attitude
  • Motivation to grow and achieve ambitious goals

​

Tech & Tools

​

PHP, HTML5, CSS3, SCSS, JavaScript, jQuery, Webflow, WordPress, Shopify, Figma, React, Git, Hubspot, Sublime Text, VS Code, macOS, Windows, MySQL, C#, ASP.NET

​

Why JDA Worldwide?

​

  • Work with a collaborative, talented, and low-ego team
  • Partner with national brands on inspiring marketing and creative projects
  • Access professional growth opportunities in digital, no-code, and remote creative jobs
  • Flexible, supportive work environment in Indianapolis

​

Ready to help shape the future of web experiences with us? Apply today and bring your creativity, technical skills, and passion for no-code and Webflow development to JDA Worldwide.

title: Cleo hiring Digital Designer, Email & Landing Pages in New York, NY | LinkedIn
aboutCompany: Cleo is a cutting-edge financial services company leveraging AI technology to revolutionize how individuals manage their finances. With a focus on simplifying money management, Cleo empowers users to make informed decisions and reach their financial goals.
jobDescription:
Digital Designer, Email & Landing Pages  

Cleo | New York, NY | Hybrid (Flexible Remote)

​

Join Cleo and help redefine how people relate to money. As a profitable fintech unicorn with over $200M in annual revenue and rapid, global growth, we’re building a future where everyone has access to a smart, approachable financial advisor—right from their pocket. If you’re passionate about creative problem-solving and want to leave a positive mark at scale, Cleo could be the perfect place for you.

​

About the Role

​

We’re seeking a hands-on Digital Designer focused on emails and landing pages who thrives across the full design process—from concept to build. You’ll breathe life into Cleo’s bold brand, crafting high-impact digital campaigns, emails, and landing experiences for our growth and lifecycle teams. This role combines strategic vision with deep expertise in design systems, no-code tools like Webflow, and a keen attention to detail.

​

This hybrid opportunity is ideal for a creative designer who loves building in no-code platforms, enjoys collaborating with cross-functional teams, and embraces the fast pace of remote creative jobs.

​

What You’ll Do

​

  • Design and develop visually compelling emails, landing pages, microsites, and digital experiences from initial concept to final handoff, always upholding creative quality and performance.
  • Champion creative direction and strategy for briefs—turning a blank canvas into standout, brand-forward assets that drive engagement and conversion.
  • Create modular, scalable design systems for CRM and web that enable rapid iteration and seamless execution across touchpoints.
  • Build and test landing pages using Webflow, Framer, HubSpot, or custom code. Basic understanding of HTML/CSS, responsive design, and browser compatibility is essential.
  • Leverage user data and analytics to iterate on layout, UX, and conversions, keeping the customer journey at the heart of everything you design.
  • Collaborate closely with developers, copywriters, and brand designers to deliver cohesive digital experiences, while leading the visual execution of your projects.
  • Support a variety of digital and brand initiatives as needed, with email and landing pages as your main focus.
  • Manage your timelines and workload with autonomy in a high-performing, creative team.

​

What We’re Looking For

​

  • 3–5 years of end-to-end digital design experience, with a strong portfolio of web and email design—ideally in fast-moving tech or consumer environments.
  • Proficiency in Figma for design, prototyping, and component library management, plus working knowledge of Adobe Creative Suite.
  • Experience building and iterating sites or pages using Webflow, Framer, or similar no-code tools—JavaScript or headless CMS a plus.
  • Understanding of email marketing best practices and familiarity with platforms like Customer.io, Beefree, or similar.
  • Strong foundation in UX/UI, performance funnels, responsive mobile-first design, and accessibility.
  • A holistic, brand-first approach to performance creative—balancing stunning visuals with clear messaging and effective user flows.
  • Collaborative spirit with the confidence to articulate and defend your design decisions.
  • Bonus: Experience with animation, CMS integrations, or CRO tools like Hotjar, VWO, or Optimizely.

​

Why You’ll Love Working Here

​

  • Competitive compensation (base + equity) with bi-annual reviews and a public progression framework.
  • Opportunity to grow at one of the fastest-scaling fintech startups, backed by top VCs like Balderton and EQT Ventures.
  • Clear career development path and total support as you try new things and take ownership.
  • Flexible work approach—work where and how you perform best. Hybrid options in London; remote roles supported worldwide with occasional in-person team meetups (travel costs covered).
  • Fast-paced, remote-friendly creative environment with high standards and meaningful impact.

​

Other Benefits

​

  • Company-wide performance reviews every 6 months
  • Generous pay increases and equity top-ups for strong performance
  • 25 days annual leave plus public holidays (and an extra day each year up to 30 days)
  • Enhanced parental leave and 1 month paid sabbatical after 4 years
  • Private medical, dental, and life insurance
  • 6% employer-matched pension in the UK
  • OpenAI subscription paid by Cleo
  • Online mental health support and workplace nursery scheme
  • Regular socials (virtual and in-person)
  • And much more

​

We believe in building a diverse, inclusive team and strongly encourage applicants from all backgrounds—including people of color, LGBTQ+, disabled and neurodivergent individuals, parents, carers, and people from lower socio-economic communities. If there’s anything we can do to support or accommodate your needs during the application process, please just let us know.

​

Compensation

​

  • United States: $84,000 – $103,000 gross annually
  • For other locations, compensation will be discussed individually, based on qualifications and experience

​

Apply today and help us shape a new era of personal finance—one that’s inclusive, accessible, and powered by creative technology like Webflow and no-code innovation.

​

If you’re an iOS user in the UK or Europe, explore Cleo with our TestFlight app: https://testflight.apple.com/join/fDG9hWng

​

Looking for remote creative jobs where you can make real impact? Cleo is the place for you.

title: Copywriter
aboutCompany: Wonderist Agency is a creative marketing firm specializing in innovative branding strategies and compelling storytelling. Our team of experts helps businesses connect with their audience through engaging content and visually striking design. Visit us at www.wonderistagency.com to see how we can bring your brand to life.
jobDescription:
Strategic Copywriter – Creative Content for Webflow & No-Code Marketing

​

Join Wonderist Agency as a strategic Copywriter and shape high-impact marketing content for brands nationwide. We’re a creative powerhouse supporting over 1,000 dental clients with original branding, dynamic photo and video shoots, and flawless website experiences—never cookie-cutter, always bespoke. Recent work? Check out www.valordentaltx.com for a taste of our approach.

​

Based in sunny San Diego, our 70+ person team thrives on collaboration, creative energy, and meaningful connections—even in a hybrid and remote work environment. Beyond our awards (San Diego’s Best Places to Work, Inc. 5000, Fastest Growing Companies), our culture is the real win: supportive, innovative, and always pushing boundaries in no-code, Webflow, and digital marketing.

​

We need a Copywriter who’s passionate about creative content, skilled with no-code tools, and eager to integrate AI-driven workflows. If you enjoy taking the lead with clients, pitching big ideas, and owning results—this is a remote creative job where your talent will stand out.

​

What You’ll Do

​

  • Lead content strategy from kickoff by discovering client needs, refining their brand voice, and shaping web and marketing content that connects and converts.
  • Collaborate closely with designers and brand strategists to generate standout practice names, branding ideas, and memorable taglines.
  • Build comprehensive content strategies tied to SEO, user experience, and business goals—using sitemaps, wireframes, and conversion tactics.
  • Craft original webpage content for client sites (often built in Webflow), blending AI-generated drafts with your creative expertise.
  • Take ownership of edits, QA, and proofreading; ensure every word meets our—and our clients’—quality standards.
  • Partner with our SEO team to deliver monthly content that optimizes site performance and search visibility.
  • Write compelling copy for direct mailers, campaigns, and other collateral that fuel client growth.
  • Contribute to and refine AI-driven workflows, always looking for smarter, faster ways to deliver results in the no-code marketing space.

​

What You Bring

​

  • 2+ years’ experience in copywriting, content strategy, or digital marketing—agency background preferred.
  • Bachelor’s degree in journalism, writing, marketing, advertising, or a related field.
  • Strong handle on SEO fundamentals and a knack for user-first content.
  • Hands-on skills with AI content creation tools (like ChatGPT, Gemini, or Claude) and interest in weaving them into creative processes.
  • Comfortable in a remote or hybrid environment, with strong communication skills for client calls and presentations.
  • Experience or familiarity with Webflow and no-code website building a big plus.
  • Adept with Google Workspace (Gmail, Docs, Sheets) and common project management/chat tools (Slack, Monday.com).
  • Bonus: Familiarity with the dental industry, knowledge of Relume, and a passion for process improvement.

​

Why Wonderist?

​

  • Award-winning culture built on trust, creativity, and genuine collaboration.
  • Flexible, hybrid, and remote creative jobs—work from anywhere in the US.
  • Regular team events in health, development, social good, and fun.
  • Work with passionate marketers and industry innovators who value your voice.
  • Opportunities to grow your skills in no-code, Webflow, AI, and more.

​

Ready to craft content that makes an impact—and have fun doing it? Apply with your résumé and a cover letter that shares your favorite grammar rule or writing pet peeve. Help us shape the future of creative marketing at Wonderist!

title: JDA Worldwide hiring Web Developer in Indianapolis, IN | LinkedIn
aboutCompany: JDA Worldwide is a leading advertising and marketing services agency dedicated to helping brands make a lasting impact in the market. With a strong focus on creativity and strategy, we collaborate with clients to deliver innovative solutions that drive business growth. jobDescription:
Web Developer at JDA Worldwide

​

Join our award-winning marketing agency, JDA Worldwide, where we craft powerful brand, creative, and digital solutions for leading and emerging brands nationwide. Based in Indianapolis and part of Prolific’s growth network, we foster a low-ego, passionate team culture and have been recognized as a top employer in Indiana.

​

We’re seeking a multi-disciplined web developer ready to own front and back-end development for a variety of high-traffic client and agency sites. This role is ideal for someone passionate about the latest in no-code and Webflow technologies, excited about remote creative jobs, and skilled in modern marketing and technical best practices.

​

Responsibilities

​

  • Lead the development, maintenance, and optimization of our agency and client websites, delivering fast, user-centric, and visually engaging experiences
  • Build functional prototypes for client feedback and turn ideas into live solutions
  • Implement both front-end (HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript, jQuery, React) and back-end (PHP, WordPress) web development projects
  • Update and enhance existing sites, troubleshooting issues as needed
  • Ensure all sites meet accessibility and SEO standards
  • Stay ahead of industry shifts, supporting adoption of new tools like Webflow and no-code solutions
  • Advise on project scope, time, and budget considerations to implement the best technical approach

​

Requirements

​

  • Advanced experience with WordPress (custom themes and plugins) and PHP
  • Strong skills in Shopify theme development
  • Proficiency with Figma, semantic HTML5, CSS3/SCSS, JavaScript, and jQuery
  • Comfortable with React and current web animation techniques
  • Deep understanding of responsive web design, browser compatibility, and performance optimization
  • Ability to build SEO-friendly websites with accessibility in mind (WCAG 2.2, Section 508)
  • Familiarity with Git version control (ideally Git Flow workflow)
  • Experience with modern web and design best practices

​

Bonus Skills

​

  • Experience with HubSpot and relationship database systems (MySQL/PostgreSQL)
  • Understanding of web security fundamentals
  • Familiarity with UNIX command line, macOS, and C#/ASP.NET environments
  • Hands-on knowledge of Webflow and other no-code platforms
  • Experience creating accessible web applications

​

What You’ll Thrive With

​

  • A team that values detail-obsessed, excellence-driven individuals
  • Support for pursuing remote creative job opportunities and flexible work arrangements
  • A collaborative, motivated atmosphere where high achievers succeed together

​

Tech Stack

​

  • PHP, HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript, jQuery, React, Figma, WordPress, Shopify, Webflow
  • SEO and web accessibility tools (WCAG)
  • Git, HubSpot, macOS, Windows, iOS, Android, and cloud-based workflows

​

At JDA, you’ll get to work with industry-leading brands, leverage the latest in no-code and Webflow design, and shape digital experiences that reach thousands every day. If you’re confident, collaborative, and committed to creative excellence, we’d love to meet you. Apply today to take your web development career to the next level!

title: Remote Webflow Developer (Contract) – No-Code & Marketing
jobDescription:
Join HoneyBook as a Remote Webflow Developer (Contract)

​

HoneyBook is the leading AI-powered business management platform empowering service-based business owners. Our tools help creative entrepreneurs attract leads, connect with clients, and manage projects with efficiency and confidence. Since 2013, we've supported over 25 million client relationships and processed more than $12 billion in transactions.

​

We're seeking an experienced Webflow Developer to join our team on a 6-month contract. You'll help build, optimize, and elevate our marketing website, supporting rapid experimentation and scalable design—all within a no-code environment. This role offers the flexibility of remote creative work, though Bay Area-based candidates are encouraged for occasional onsite collaboration.

​

Responsibilities

  • Webflow Development: Design, build, and launch new landing pages, campaign modules, and CRO test variants in Webflow.
  • Custom Integrations: Add interactive custom code modules (JavaScript) for personalization, advanced interactivity, event logging, and unique marketing needs.
  • Analytics Setup: Integrate and validate analytics tools (Mixpanel, Google Analytics, Datadog) and ensure accurate event tracking across the site.
  • Performance Optimization: Audit site speed (e.g., Lighthouse), refactor code, and implement improvements to enhance page load times and user experience.
  • Workflow Documentation: Create and document Webflow development processes for teams and partners, including version control and review checklists.
  • Third-Party Tool Integration: Seamlessly configure tools like Google One Tap, cookie consent modules, chat widgets, and analytics scripts within the Webflow ecosystem.
  • Marketing Experimentation: Collaborate with marketing for A/B testing, experiment tracking, and iterative design recommendations based on campaign data.
  • Feature Consultation: Advise on feasibility, performance, and timelines for new feature requests.

​

Requirements

  • Proven experience in front-end development for marketing websites, with advanced proficiency in Webflow and no-code solutions.
  • Strong knowledge of developing scalable, high-performance landing pages optimized for conversion and maintainability.
  • Comfortable integrating third-party APIs, analytics, and using custom JavaScript within Webflow projects.
  • Understanding of performance optimization, SEO best practices, and structured QA processes.
  • Collaborative experience with marketing, design, and cross-functional teams.
  • Familiarity with analytics and monitoring tools (e.g., Segment, Mixpanel, Google Analytics 4, Datadog) is a plus.
  • Ability to document workflows and communicate technical concepts clearly.
  • Location: Pacific Time Zone strongly preferred (San Francisco Bay Area ideal), but open to remote applicants for creative job flexibility.

​

Project Terms

  • Contract: 6 months
  • Up to 40 hours/week
  • Independent contractor status (no employment relationship implied)

​

Perks & Benefits

  • Flexible, remote creative job — work from anywhere in the U.S.
  • Build high-impact, visible projects in the no-code space
  • Collaborate with marketing and design teams on innovative web solutions
  • Join a mission-driven, creative company transforming how entrepreneurs grow

​

How to Apply

Please submit:

  • Your resume
  • A portfolio or list of Webflow landing pages you’ve built (with project dates)
  • Your proposed pricing structure (hourly or project-based)

​

Follow us on Medium, BuiltIn, and Instagram for a behind-the-scenes look at HoneyBook.

​

For details on how we handle applicant information, please review our [Privacy Policy].

title: Senior Webflow Developer – Growth & No-Code Expert
jobDescription:
Join Kajabi as a Senior Webflow Developer (Growth Team)

​

Location: United States (Remote with occasional onsite)

​

About Kajabi

Kajabi is the leading creator commerce platform, empowering entrepreneurs and creators to turn their expertise into thriving online businesses. Our all-in-one platform supports online courses, communities, digital products, and more—serving tens of thousands of creators who have generated over $10 billion in revenue.

​

Summary

We're seeking a Senior Webflow Developer to join our Growth Team. This is more than a traditional web development role—you'll be a strategic no-code specialist building high-impact, scalable web experiences that drive user acquisition and retention. If you're passionate about Webflow, innovative web solutions, and rapidly-growing remote creative jobs, we want to hear from you.

​

Responsibilities

  • Lead Web Innovation: Architect, develop, and optimize advanced Webflow projects, focusing on large-scale features and performance.
  • Drive Growth: Design and iterate high-velocity A/B tests, CRO experiments, and new landing pages to maximize conversion and key business metrics.
  • Strategic Collaborator: Serve as the crucial bridge between web development, engineering, and marketing, leading technical initiatives and mentoring junior developers.
  • Integrate AI & APIs: Leverage engineering AI tools and APIs to automate workflows and create dynamic, personalized content.
  • Optimize for SEO: Ensure top-tier SEO, web performance, and scalable, clean Webflow structures across all projects.
  • Own Projects: Independently scope, execute, and analyze end-to-end web and no-code projects, providing actionable insights from data.

​

Requirements

  • 7+ years of web development (with significant Webflow and no-code expertise)
  • Demonstrated leadership delivering growth-driven website enhancements
  • Advanced skills in Webflow, HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and front-end development
  • Experience with APIs, React, SEO best practices, and web analytics
  • Proven track record with conversion optimization and marketing tech
  • Strong collaborator, technical mentor, and clear communicator
  • Comfortable working in a fast-paced, data-driven remote creative environment

​

Perks & Benefits

  • Competitive salary: $93,000–$125,000 base + equity + bonus
  • Company-paid medical, dental, and vision for self & family
  • Sponsored HSA and 401K with 100% match up to 6%
  • Flexible vacation policy
  • Mental health and wellness resources
  • Fitness incentives
  • Hybrid work style: Primarily remote, with occasional in-person collaboration (2-3 times per quarter—Western US preferred)

​

At Kajabi, we value equity, inclusion, and creativity. We’re proud to offer remote creative jobs where your voice and skills can make a real impact.

​

Ready to shape the future of creator commerce? Click apply to join our innovative team!

​

Kajabi LLC is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, national origin, disability, age, veteran status, or any other protected status.

title: Webflow Brand & Website Designer (Hybrid, NYC)
jobDescription:
Join Lawtrades as a Webflow Brand & Website Designer

​

Location: New York City, NY (Hybrid: 3 days in-office, 2 days remote)

​

At Lawtrades, we're transforming how legal work gets done by connecting top legal professionals with the companies that need them most. Design, technology, and storytelling are at the heart of our brand—and we're looking for a motivated creative to help shape our digital and visual identity.

​

About the Role

This is a full-time, hands-on position for a talented Brand & Website Designer with strong Webflow, no-code, and frontend development skills. If you excel at building beautiful web experiences, developing brand visuals across platforms, and thrive in innovative, fast-paced environments focused on remote creative jobs, we’d love to connect.

​

Please note: You must have the legal right to work in New York City. Sponsorship is not available for this role.

​

---

​

Key Responsibilities

​

Website Design & Development

  • Lead end-to-end website projects: from ideation and wireframes in Figma, to responsive Webflow sites ready for launch
  • Build custom Webflow components, page animations, and interactions (plus HTML/CSS/JavaScript coding where needed)
  • Ensure websites are responsive, accessible, and optimized for performance across all devices
  • Create landing pages and conversion-driven campaign sites for sales and marketing teams, integrating tools such as Webflow, Hubspot, and Zapier
  • Drive QA processes for all digital products

​

Brand & Visual Design

  • Own and evolve Lawtrades’ visual identity, tone of voice, and brand guidelines across digital and print channels
  • Design high-impact assets: social media graphics, infographics, slide decks, banners, business cards, merchandise, and more
  • Support product launches with motion design, animated reels, demo walkthroughs, and video edits
  • Maintain brand consistency across marketing, product, sales, and internal culture
  • Collaborate closely with sales and social media teams to review and revise branded content as needed
  • Update and enhance our help center design

​

---

​

Requirements

  • 1+ year of experience designing and developing responsive websites in Webflow (portfolio required)
  • Proficiency with Figma for high-fidelity wireframes and prototyping
  • Strong frontend coding skills (HTML, CSS, JavaScript)
  • Experience across both digital and print design (Adobe Illustrator, InDesign)
  • Motion design skills (Adobe After Effects, Premiere Pro, or similar)
  • Strong eye for typography, color, and layout
  • Excellent organizational, planning, and communications skills
  • Ability to manage multiple projects and meet tight deadlines in a fast-paced environment
  • Exceptional attention to detail and self-motivation; able to own projects from concept to launch

​

Bonus Skills

  • Experience integrating generative AI into design workflows
  • Hubspot proficiency
  • Social media content management

​

---

​

Perks & Benefits

  • Impact: Shape and elevate the brand and web presence of a rapidly growing startup
  • Ownership: Be the point person for brand and web design at Lawtrades, influencing every part of the company
  • Growth: Expand into product/UI/UX design as our platform evolves
  • Collaboration: Join a passionate, mission-driven, and creative team in NYC
  • Hybrid Schedule: 3 days in-person, 2 days remote flexibility
  • Competitive Compensation: $50,000–$100,000, based on experience
  • Comprehensive Benefits

​

---

​

How to Apply

Send your portfolio, resume, and a short note about yourself to michelle@lawtrades.com. Include examples of Webflow websites you’ve built and brand design work across digital and print. A portfolio is required to move forward in our process.

​

Our process: Portfolio review → 1–2 interviews with design team → Job offer

​

---

​

Commitment to Diversity

Lawtrades is committed to inclusion and diversity. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and experiences to join our supportive culture.

title: Webflow Developer (Contract) – Remote Creative Role
jobDescription:
Join HoneyBook as a Webflow Developer (Contract)

​

HoneyBook is an AI-powered business management platform serving independent creatives, freelancers, and service-based businesses. We empower professionals to attract leads, book clients, and manage projects—fast, smart, and seamlessly integrated with no-code tools and creative solutions.

​

We're seeking a skilled Webflow Developer for a 6-month remote contract to elevate our marketing site, optimize landing pages, and drive performance for growth-driven campaigns. If you thrive in remote creative jobs, excel at no-code development, and love collaborating with dynamic marketing teams, we want to hear from you.

​

Responsibilities

  • Webflow Design & Implementation: Build, deploy, and optimize high-converting landing pages and campaign modules with Webflow—delivering fully functional prototypes, project files, and staging links.
  • Custom Technical Enhancements: Integrate custom JavaScript for site interactivity, personalization, robust event tracking, and advanced user experiences.
  • Analytics & Event Tracking: Set up and measure event tracking with Mixpanel, Google Analytics, and similar tools. Deliver supporting documentation and verification reports.
  • Performance Optimization: Perform audits (Lighthouse, etc.), refactor code for faster page loads, and troubleshoot tracking or site issues with a performance-first focus.
  • Webflow Workflow Documentation: Establish and document scalable Webflow workflows, design system best practices, and quality checkpoints for in-house teams.
  • Third-Party Integrations: Configure tools such as Google One Tap, cookie consent, analytics scripts, and chat modules within Webflow.
  • Growth Experimentation: Support A/B testing for marketing, advise on iterative design, infrastructure, and analyze user engagement to optimize results.
  • Feature Consulting: Consult on the feasibility and performance impact of new web features and enhancements.

​

Requirements

  • Advanced experience with Webflow, no-code development, and modern front-end best practices.
  • Solid portfolio of high-performing, conversion-optimized landing pages built in Webflow.
  • Proficiency with HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and integrating third-party marketing tools.
  • Strong understanding of analytics tools such as Mixpanel, Google Analytics, Segment, or Datadog (bonus points: experience with these platforms).
  • Proven ability to optimize web performance, implement SEO strategies, and deliver readable documentation.
  • Collaborative mindset—comfortable working with design, marketing, and technical teams.
  • Preferred: Based in Pacific Time / San Francisco Bay Area for occasional in-person collaboration; remote and asynchronous work welcomed.

title: Cleo hiring Digital Designer, Email & Landing Pages in New York, NY | LinkedIn

We’re looking for a creative and detail-obsessed Digital Designer with a passion for building standout emails and high-converting landing pages.   • Support broader creative studio initiatives when needed, while keeping email and landing page design as your core focus.
  • Manage your own timelines and priorities, contributing proactively to a high-performing, remote-friendly creative team.

​

What We’re Looking For

​

  • 3–5 years of hands-on design experience across email and web, preferably within a fast-paced tech or consumer brand environment.

  • Expertise in Figma for layout, prototyping, and component libraries; comfortable with Adobe Creative Suite.

  • Familiarity with CRM and email platforms—such as Customer.io, Beefree, and general marketing automation workflows.
  • Deep understanding of UX/UI principles, mobile-first design, accessibility, and performance marketing funnels.
  • Collaborative mindset—enjoys partnering with copywriters and thinking holistically about content, interaction, and user intent.
  • Clear communicator, confident in defending design decisions and passionate about performance-driven, brand-led creative.
  • Bonus: Experience with animation, CMS integrations, or CRO tools (e.g., Hotjar, VWO, Optimizely).

​

What You’ll Get

​

  • Competitive compensation (base salary + equity) with bi-annual reviews.
  • Transparent growth framework and a clear progression plan—see our salary bands here: https://cleo-ai.progressionapp.com/
  • The chance to work at a high-growth, VC-backed unicorn alongside a global team of innovators in creative, marketing, and no-code development.
  • Flexible working environment: hybrid in London or remote-first (we cover travel for in-person meetups).
  • Comprehensive benefits:  
  • 25 days annual leave (plus public holidays, with extra days for tenure)
  • Regular pay reviews and equity top-ups for high performers
  • 6% employer-matched pension (UK)
  • Private medical, dental, and life assurance
  • Enhanced parental leave
  • Paid sabbatical after 4 years
  • OpenAI subscription covered
  • Mental health and family support programs
  • Regular social events

Compensation

Cleo welcomes applications from candidates of all backgrounds—including people of color, LGBTQ+ individuals, people with disabilities, neurodivergent talent, parents, carers, and those from lower socio-economic backgrounds. If you have specific requirements or need accommodations, please let us know.

title: Brand & Webflow Designer (No-Code, Hybrid NYC)
jobDescription:

​

Location: New York City, NY (Hybrid: 3 days on-site, 2 days remote)

At Lawtrades, we're redefining how legal work gets done. Through our innovative platform, we connect leading legal professionals with the companies that need them. Brand & Visual Design

  • Take ownership of Lawtrades’ visual identity, expanding brand guidelines and maintaining consistency across all channels.

Requirements

  • Motion design: Experience with animation and demo/video tools (Adobe After Effects, Premiere Pro, Blender, etc).
  • Comfortable working across both digital and print design.

  • Excellent communication, organization, and multi-tasking abilities.
  • Thrive in a fast-paced, evolving workplace.

​

Nice-to-haves:

---

​

Perks & Benefits

---

How to Apply

Send your resume, portfolio, and a brief note about yourself to michelle@lawtrades.com. Please include:

A portfolio is required to proceed in the interview process.

Hiring steps: Portfolio review → 1-2 design team interviews → Job offer

Lawtrades is dedicated to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and welcoming environment. We strongly encourage candidates of all backgrounds to apply.

Why AgePrecisely?

  • Oversee regular updates, patches, backups, and upgrades to ensure platform stability and business continuity.
  • Integrate modern web and marketing technologies (such as XHTML, CSS, JavaScript, and PowerShell scripting) to further enhance user experience and security.

​

What We’re Looking For

​

  • Strong expertise in SharePoint administration, including architecture, configuration, and upgrade management (on-premises and Microsoft 365/Cloud).
  • Proficient in PowerShell scripting and automation for SharePoint, as well as IIS and SQL Server (2016/2019 high availability clustering).
  • Experience supporting users, resolving technical questions, and championing a collaborative, remote creative work environment.
  • Solid understanding of web technologies—HTML, CSS, JavaScript—and their application in optimizing SharePoint and no-code solutions.
  • Knowledge of security best practices, compliance requirements, encryption, and firewalls.

  • Familiarity with Microsoft Active Directory and Microsoft 365 integrations.
  • Excellent problem-solving abilities and strong interpersonal communication skills.

​

Preferred Qualifications

​

  • 8–10 years’ experience with a BS/BA, 6–8 years with an MS/MA, or 3–5 years with a PhD.
  • Certifications: Microsoft 365 Certified (Fundamentals, Enterprise Administrator Expert), Microsoft Certified: SharePoint Server, or equivalent (MCITP, MCSE, MCSD, MCSM).

​

Compensation

​

  • $120,000 – $170,000 (based on experience and qualifications; Maryland area)

​

​

Benefits & Perks

​

  • Equity ownership (RSUs) in our privately-owned, fast-growing company.
  • 100% employer-paid healthcare, vision, and dental coverage for full-time team members and dependents.
  • 12 weeks paid parental leave for all caregivers, with additional pregnancy disability leave for birthing parents.
  • Flexible time off and sabbatical programs.
  • Access to mental health support, coaching, therapy, and an Employee Assistance Program.
  • Monthly stipends to support remote work and wellness.
  • 401(k) or pension schemes (where required), plus additional financial wellness resources.
  • Benefits for temporary contract roles may vary based on location and contract type.

Compensation

Our cash compensation aligns with the cost of labor by location. Your specific offer will depend on geographic zone, experience, and skillset.

​

United States:

  • Zone A: $115,000 – $155,500 USD
  • Zone B: $108,000 – $146,000 USD
  • Zone C: $101,500 – $137,000 USD

​

Canada (ON & BC):

  • $131,000 – $176,500 CAD

​

Please check our Careers page or ask your recruiter to confirm your pay zone.

Work Remotely, Stay Connected

​

We’re a remote-first team with a global mindset. Webflow is committed to building an inclusive team across backgrounds and experiences. We are proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer and consider qualified applicants regardless of race, gender, orientation, disability, or veteran status.

Ready to join a creative company shaping the future of no-code and remote creative jobs? Apply today—or stay in touch by following us on our Webflow Blog, LinkedIn, X (Twitter), and Glassdoor.

Webflow provides reasonable accommodations for applicants with disabilities throughout the hiring process. Upon interview scheduling, you’ll receive instructions for confidential accommodation requests.

Employment at Webflow may require legal authorization to work in your country and successful background screening as permitted by law. Learn more in our Applicant Privacy Notice.

This is a hybrid role based in Chicago, IL, with in-person onboarding in San Francisco, and offers the chance to collaborate with a team that’s pioneering remote creative jobs and the next wave of web development.

  • Propose and help implement improvements to sales processes, with a focus on scale and efficiency
  • Travel up to 15% (primarily for onboarding, team events, and industry conferences)

​

Who You Are

​

  • You have relevant work or academic experience that translates to SaaS sales, business development, or marketing
  • Adept at meeting and exceeding sales targets—you're a relationship-builder, problem-solver, and thrive in metric-driven environments
  • Comfortable with (or eager to learn) CRM and sales engagement tech; you’re passionate about creative technology and new digital tools
  • Determined, persistent, and resilient, with an entrepreneurial “hunter” mentality
  • Exceptional communicator—able to explain complex concepts simply and convincingly
  • Excited about Webflow’s mission and the no-code revolution
  • Team player with experience working cross-functionally
  • Open to coaching, mentorship, and exploring new skillsets—especially as they relate to emerging tech such as AI
  • Bonus: Fluency in Spanish or French

​

Our Core Values

​

  • Build lasting customer trust by putting users first  
  • Win together as one team  
  • Continuously reinvent ourselves and push boundaries  
  • Deliver with speed, quality, and attention to craft  

​

Benefits & Perks

​

  • Equity ownership in a fast-growing company
  • Competitive compensation with On Target Earnings starting at $77,000 (may vary by location)
  • 100% employer-paid health, dental, and vision coverage for employees and dependents
  • 12 weeks of paid parental leave, plus additional leave for birthing parents
  • Flexible PTO, sabbatical program, and monthly work/wellness stipends
  • Access to mental wellness resources and professional coaching
  • Retirement plans and financial wellness support

​

Diversity & Inclusion

​

Webflow is committed to building an inclusive and diverse team. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and make employment decisions based on business needs, qualifications, and experience.

​

Additional Information

​

  • You must be legally authorized to work in the U.S. or Canada without Webflow’s sponsorship
  • Offers may require a background check, as permitted by law

​

Ready to join a team that’s shaping the future of no-code, marketing, and creative technology? Apply today, or follow Webflow on LinkedIn, Twitter, Glassdoor, or our Webflow Blog to stay connected and learn more about remote creative jobs and career opportunities.

title: Part-Time Webflow Designer/Developer (Remote, US)
jobDescription:
<h2>Join Rare Candy as Our Next Webflow Designer/Developer</h2>

<p><strong>Location:</strong> Remote (US preferred, EST overlap)<br><strong>Compensation:</strong> $25–40/hr (Part-time: 10–15 hrs/week, with growth potential)<br><strong>Department:</strong> Design / Product<br><strong>Reports to:</strong> CEO & Product Lead</p>

​

<h3>About Rare Candy</h3>

<p>Rare Candy is transforming the $25B trading card marketplace by offering a unique, community-focused platform dedicated entirely to trading card games. As the go-to destination for Pokémon, MTG, Lorcana, and more, our AI-powered platform empowers collectors to buy, sell, and showcase their cards through innovative collection tools and high-impact drops.<br><br>We’re passionate trading card enthusiasts, builders, and creative team members—growing rapidly and driven by a mission to connect, empower, and inspire the next generation of collectors.</p>

​

<h3>Role Overview</h3>

<p>We are seeking a talented <strong>part-time Webflow Designer/Developer</strong> to elevate our digital brand and marketing site. If you have a passion for no-code tools, creative web design, and building seamless user experiences for remote creative jobs, you'll thrive here. You’ll collaborate with product, design, and growth teams to deliver beautifully designed, responsive, and high-converting web experiences that tell our story and drive business growth.</p>

​

<h3>Key Responsibilities</h3>

<ul>

  <li><strong>Design & develop in Webflow:</strong> Build visually engaging, responsive landing pages and microsites that support campaigns and product launches.</li>

  <li><strong>Bring brand to life:</strong> Apply our evolving visual identity to every digital touchpoint, ensuring brand consistency and creative flair.</li>

  <li><strong>Optimize for growth:</strong> Utilize best practices in conversion rate optimization (CRO), SEO, and analytics to boost site performance and user engagement.</li>

  <li><strong>Maintain & scale:</strong> Manage updates, troubleshoot site bugs, and enhance features across rarecandy.com.</li>

  <li><strong>Collaborate cross-functionally:</strong> Partner with Growth, Product, and Design teams for launches, campaigns, and brand initiatives.</li>

  <li><strong>Build a scalable system:</strong> Develop and document a Webflow-based design system so the website can scale quickly and efficiently.</li>

</ul>

​

<h3>What Success Looks Like</h3>

<ul>

  <li><strong>First 90 days:</strong><br>- Refresh the marketing site with a new homepage and at least two optimized landing pages.<br>- Implement a robust Webflow design system (components, styles, responsive rules).<br>- Improve site speed and strengthen SEO fundamentals.</li>

  <li><strong>By end of year one:</strong><br>- Grow the site to support multiple campaigns, launches, and partnerships.<br>- Launch A/B testing and analytics strategies to improve conversion rates month-over-month.</li>

</ul>

​

<h3>About You (Requirements)</h3>

<ul>

  <li>3+ years of experience designing and developing in <strong>Webflow</strong> (portfolio required).</li>

  <li>Strong visual design and UX/UI skills, with expertise in responsive web best practices.</li>

  <li>Proficient with light custom code (HTML, CSS, JavaScript) to extend Webflow when needed.</li>

  <li>Hands-on experience integrating analytics, forms, CMS, and SEO tools into Webflow projects.</li>

  <li>Excellent time management for independent, remote creative work with quick, polished delivery.</li>

  <li>Bonus: You’re passionate about trading card games (Pokemon, MTG, Lorcana) and understand collector communities.</li>

</ul>

​

<h3>Perks & Benefits</h3>

<ul>

  <li>Significant impact on our brand during a high-growth phase.</li>

  <li>Flexible remote position with potential for expanded hours and responsibility (perfect for remote creative jobs enthusiasts).</li>

  <li>Collaborate closely with a seasoned team from StockX, eBay, Topps, and the wider creative no-code tech space.</li>

  <li>Opportunity to define the look, feel, and future of the premier TCG marketplace before we go mainstream.</li>

</ul>

<p>Rare Candy is proud to be an equal opportunity employer, welcoming every creative background and identity. Join us and help shape the future of trading card communities!</p>

title: Remote Ecommerce Manager – Shopify & No-Code Ops
jobDescription:
Join Prosana as Remote Ecommerce Manager

​

Prosana, a cutting-edge direct-to-consumer beauty brand, is seeking a detail-oriented Ecommerce Manager to drive our Shopify and CheckoutChamp operations. If you’re passionate about web technology, have a knack for QA and dev coordination, and want to be part of a remote creative team shaping the future of anti-aging skincare — this role is for you.

​

About Us

​

We are a science-driven skincare company with a mission to redefine the industry through real, clinically-proven results. Our flagship anti-aging product has a growing global fanbase, reflecting our dedication to transparency and innovation. As a remote-first and fast-growing brand, we emphasize action, autonomy, and rapid iteration. Every team member has a direct impact on our strategy and growth trajectory.

​

Role Summary

​

As our Ecommerce Manager, you’ll own the technical infrastructure powering our Shopify, CheckoutChamp, and no-code tools. You’ll optimize ecommerce workflows, ensure a seamless customer journey, and take charge of QA, integrations, and rapid launch cycles. This is a full-time, remote creative job offering flexibility and substantial growth potential.

​

Key Responsibilities

​

  • Oversee ecommerce infrastructure across Shopify, CheckoutChamp, and key integrations
  • Lead QA: Test user flows, storefronts, VSL funnels, and checkout experiences to ensure flawless customer journeys
  • Coordinate dev teams and manage no-code landing page/funnel builds for product and campaign launches
  • Monitor backend operations (SKU mapping, order integration) to guarantee accurate processing
  • Maintain up-to-date product listings, bundle logic, and merchandising across all platforms
  • Manage Shopify apps and troubleshoot integrations (Loox, Klaviyo, payment gateways)
  • Support international expansion: implement translations and streamline localized storefronts
  • Collaborate with growth, product, and operations teams to influence conversion funnels and technical performance

​

Requirements

​

  • Experience in direct-to-consumer or high-performance ecommerce environments
  • Deep technical knowledge of Shopify; CheckoutChamp experience is a strong plus
  • Exceptional QA skills: you proactively spot and resolve site issues quickly
  • Comfortable managing developers and third-party app integrations
  • Strong understanding of no-code tools; familiarity with Webflow, Klaviyo, and Loox
  • Skilled in managing SKUs, bundles, and backend systems at scale
  • Data-driven approach; use analytics to prioritize and improve operations
  • Thrive in remote, async setups and can deliver under deadlines
  • Bonus: German language skills

​

What Success Looks Like

​

30 Days: You’ve mapped the full store architecture, audited QA processes, and taken over daily site checks.

​

60 Days: Confidently managing devs, launching new funnel pages, fixing data issues, and stabilizing checkout flows.

​

90 Days: Driving product launches, leading international rollouts, and serving as the backbone of technical performance.

​

Perks & Benefits

​

  • 100% remote creative job with flexible hours
  • Full ownership of ecommerce operations with high impact
  • Fast-paced, supportive team culture focused on growth
  • Exposure to advanced no-code systems, Webflow builds, and multi-country ecommerce setups
  • Career growth into senior leadership as our ecommerce function scales

​

Ready to shape the future of beauty ecommerce using Webflow, Shopify, and no-code strategies? Apply now to join Prosana’s innovative team!

Recess is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Applicants must be authorized to work full-time in the United States.

title: Web Designer
jobDescription:
Finalsite is the first community relationship management platform for K-12 schools, transforming how schools attract students, engage families, build community — and thrive. More than 7,000 schools and districts worldwide trust Finalsite’s integrated platform for their websites, communications, mobile apps, enrollment, and marketing services. Finalsite is headquartered in Glastonbury, CT, USA with employees who work remotely in nearly every state in the U.S. as well as Europe, South America, and Asia. For more information, please visit www.finalsite.com.

​

VISION

​

Finalsite will transform the way school communities engage with their schools.

​

Summary Of The Role

​

The Web Designer is responsible for creating effective designs for our clients in close collaboration with project managers and front-end developers. They understand and work well within clearly defined project constraints, including client goals, accessibility guidelines, branding requirements, technical considerations, project budget, and timeline.

​

LOCATION

​

100% Remote - Anywhere within the US

​

Responsibilities

​

  • Design responsive sites for use in Finalsite’s CMS
  • Create a mixture of unique, specific pages and comprehensive visual systems for each client
  • Take creative control of projects, serving as the key point of contact for project design direction and decisions
  • Create new design solutions and work within existing structures
  • Assist in the execution and maintenance of internal design needs such as Theme designs (our website templates), shared base files, and more
  • Consult with clients over the phone, via Zoom, and in writing to provide direction and leadership, communicating persuasively and welcoming them into our design process
  • Interact closely with various team members (internal, freelance, and outside agencies)
  • Exercise judgment in determining both the strategic and tactical approaches for project-specific design initiatives

Qualifications And Skills

​

  • At least 4 years of professional web design experience, with a robust portfolio showcasing exceptional digital work.
  • Proven experience in client engagement and communication, with a track record of successfully managing and directing complex design projects.
  • Proficiency in Figma, our design, prototyping, and developer handoff tool, experience with Sketch and Adobe Creative Suite a bonus
  • A sharp eye for detail and highly developed organizational skills, including the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously and meet deadlines
  • Knowledge of front-end web development, experience working with schools, project management experience, and experience with accessible design preferred

RESIDENCY REQUIREMENT

​

Finalsite offers 100% fully remote employment opportunities, however, these opportunities are limited to permanent residents of the United States. Current residency, as well as continued residency, within the United States is required to obtain (and retain) employment with Finalsite.

​

DISCLOSURES

​

Finalsite is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. EEO is the Law. title: Webflow Developer
jobDescription:
COMPANY OVERVIEW 

Domo's AI and Data Products Platform lets people channel AI and data into innovative uses that deliver a measurable impact. Anyone can use Domo to prepare, analyze, visualize, automate, and build data products that are amplified by AI. 

POSITION SUMMARY

This role is required to be in office 5 days a week.

Domo is seeking a skilled Web Developer with extensive Webflow experience to join our marketing team. In this role, you will contribute to the development and maintenance of various websites and web applications, with a primary focus on domo.com. The ideal candidate is a creative problem-solver with a strong foundation in both design and development, a keen eye for detail, and a passion for turning concepts into innovative digital experiences. As part of the marketing team, you'll work closely with marketing to best support their needs, ensuring timely, high-quality solutions that align with business goals. 

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES  

As the Web producer on the Domo marketing team you will need to:  

  • Support the web development team with ongoing website tasks, ensuring smooth project execution.
  • Collaborate with team members to gather requirements and ensure project success.
  • Update existing web pages, build new ones, and convert design concepts into interactive web pages.
  • Implement responsive design for a seamless experience across devices.
  • Integrate third-party services and APIs (e.g., Google Analytics, CRM) into Webflow websites.
  • Ensure web content meets compliance, accessibility (WCAG), and company standards.
  • Participate in QA activities and audits to ensure quality standards.
  • Help troubleshoot and resolve bugs.
  • Optimize websites for better SEO and performance.
  • Maintain and update websites to align with brand guidelines.
  • Stay updated on Webflow features, trends, and best practices.
  • Join marketing team meetings and complete tasks from weekly sprints. 

JOB REQUIREMENTS  

  • 5+ years of programming experience.
  • Expert in everything Webflow.
  • Fluent knowledge of HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and jQuery.
  • Experience with RESTful APIs.
  • Experience working with GitHub and Git repositories.
  • Ability to code secure and safe applications.
  • Strong collaboration and project management skills. 

NICE TO HAVE

  • Experience integrating CMS platforms (e.g., WordPress, HubSpot) with Webflow. 
  • Familiarity with front-end frameworks such as React, Angular, and Vue.js. 
  • Strong understanding of UX/UI design principles and how they apply to web development. 
  • A portfolio showcasing successful Webflow projects and design solutions. 

LOCATION: American Fork, Utah    

BENEFITS: https://www.domo.com/company/careers/culture 

Domo is an equal opportunity employer.

title: UX/UI Designer - South Africa
jobDescription:
UX/UI Designer – Mid to Senior Level

Location: Remote (Cape Town-based, with 1 in-person team day/week) Remote (Other areas of South Africa, with 1 in-person team day/month)

Type: Full-time, Permanent Placement

Salary: Market-related, based on experience

We're Looking for a Designer Who Lives for the Details

Do you obsess over balance, composition, and typography? Do your interface designs communicate as clearly as they delight? We're on the lookout for a visually gifted UX/UI Designer who brings design thinking and aesthetic finesse together to create meaningful digital experiences.

This is more than a production role — it’s a chance to bring your creativity, craft, and storytelling to a team that values design as a strategic force. You’ll work across exciting product domains like FinTech, EduTech, PropTech, and Crypto, with a passionate team that thrives on thoughtful collaboration and creative freedom.

What You'll Do

  • Turn complexity into clarity through stunning UI and thoughtful interaction design.
  • Lead user journeys, wireframes, prototypes, and high-fidelity interfaces that feel as good as they look.
  • Collaborate closely with UX researchers, developers, and product owners — you're not just taking a brief, you're shaping it.
  • Bring originality to visual design: from layout to iconography, animation to typography.
  • Refine designs based on user feedback and performance insights.

What We’d Love You to Have

  • 5+ years of hands-on experience designing for digital products and platforms.
  • An exceptional portfolio that demonstrates both your visual storytelling and your design logic.
  • Mastery of Figma or equivalent tools — with strong attention to systems, grids, and hierarchy.
  • Deep understanding of user-centered design principles, accessibility, and responsive design.
  • Ability to manage your own time, take initiative, and collaborate in a remote team.
  • A genuine love of design, craft, and pushing the visual bar.

Bonus Points For

  • Exposure to emerging tech industries (FinTech, Crypto, SaaS, etc.).
  • Comfort with animation/microinteraction design or visual storytelling techniques.
  • A personal design philosophy — we’d love to know what guides your eye and approach.

Why You’ll Love This Role

  • Projects that are meaningful, fast-paced, and creatively challenging.
  • A team that values curiosity, design rigor, and individuality.
  • Support for continuous learning, experimentation, and creative risk-taking.

title: Webflow Developer
jobDescription:
Social Driver is a digital agency with the strategies, ideas, creative, and technology to connect with people today, across websites, social media, branding, marketing, advocacy, and more. Our vision is to be the trusted agency partner for our clients. Our company mantra captures our mission, culture, and values: We believe the future is bright, all progress is social, and the drivers make history. We aim to provide our clients with digital solutions that help them meet their goals and, through that work, make an impact for a brighter future.

About this Position:

Our design team is looking for a talented Webflow Developer to bring cutting-edge websites to life for a diverse range of clients. As a Webflow Developer at Social Driver, you'll collaborate within a dynamic, cross-functional team, working alongside art directors and designers to develop fully custom, mobile-optimized websites that push creative boundaries. This remote, contractor-to-hire role offers the chance to work on high-impact projects and deliver sleek, responsive web experiences. Applicants must be based in the U.S. and ready to make their mark in a fast-paced, digital environment.

Objectives & Responsibilities:

Lead the Full Development Lifecycle: Own the end-to-end development process of new Webflow websites, from initial concept to final deployment, while also managing updates and new requests for existing sites.

Create Engaging Interactive Features: Design and develop dynamic, interactive elements and animations that enhance the user experience for both new and existing websites.

Ensure Smooth Launches: Play a key role in website launches, optimizing for minimal downtime, preserving SEO rankings, and ensuring a seamless user experience.

Maintain High Coding Standards: Adhere to best practices in coding, following guidelines set by technical leadership and industry standards. Conduct thorough cross-browser testing and accessibility checks to ensure optimal UX across platforms and a diverse audience base.

Support Training & Documentation: Collaborate with content strategists to create clear and detailed CMS documentation, making it easy for clients and internal teams to navigate and maintain their websites.

Communicate Clearly & Effectively: Share your ideas, technical recommendations, and feedback both verbally and in writing to help drive project success.

Attention to Detail & Quality Assurance: Focus on accuracy and quality at every stage of development, ensuring that the final product aligns with the creative vision and meets all technical requirements.​

Requirements:

  • 3+ years of hands-on Webflow development experience, ideally within an agency setting.
  • Expertise in using the Client-First style system and building with the Webflow/Relume component system.
  • Solid foundation in HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, with intermediate to advanced-level proficiency.
  • Familiarity with Figma for design collaboration and seamless project handoff.
  • Experience with integrations such as Google Tag Manager, HubSpot, Wized, and/or similar tools.
  • Strong understanding of modern UI/UX design principles and best practices.
  • Proficient in accurately tracking time using Harvest and efficiently managing multiple tasks with project management tools such as Asana.
  • Bonus: Proficiency with GSAP (GreenSock Animation Platform), Lottie and Spline integrations for creating smooth animations and interactive elements.

How to Apply:

Apply online with your resume and a cover letter that outlines how you would be a great fit for the position.​

Benefits:

We take pride in our culture and offer a competitive salary with great benefits, like flexible PTO, 401k, continuing education, phone reimbursement, medical dental, and vision, short-term/long-term disability, a bike-share membership, flexible work schedules including Fast Lane and No Meeting Fridays, and great colleagues.

Commitment to DEI:

Social Driver is committed to advancing diversity, equity, and inclusion in hiring, marketing, programming, and in all other aspects of the work we do. Diversity, equity, and inclusion are deeply connected to our mission, our success, and our culture.

​

Remote work

title: Webflow Developer
jobDescription:
Vectra® is the leader in AI-driven threat detection and response for hybrid and multi-cloud enterprises.

The Vectra AI Platform delivers integrated signal across public cloud, SaaS, identity, and data center networks in a single platform. Powered by patented Attack Signal Intelligence, it empowers security teams to rapidly prioritize, investigate and respond to the most advanced cyber-attacks. With 35 patents in AI-driven threat detection and the most vendor references in MITRE D3FEND, organizations worldwide rely on the Vectra AI to move at the speed and scale of hybrid attackers. $76,000 - $146,900 USD

title: Webflow Developer
jobDescription:
Healthcare providers go into medicine to care for people, but end up losing valuable time each day to admin work and other workplace challenges. Time that could otherwise be spent helping patients. And patients end up suffering as a result. At Commure, we build solutions that simplify providers' lives and keep them connected to their patients so they can focus on doing what matters most: providing care. 

Our innovative suite of software and hardware – augmented by advanced LLM AI, RTLS, and healthcare workflow automations – boosts efficiency across every domain of healthcare, freeing up healthcare providers to spend more of their time caring for patients. Our growing suite of technologies include staff duress alerting, asset tracking, patient elopement, revenue cycle management, clinical documentation and intake, provider copilots, patient engagement and communication, home health, remote patient monitoring, and more.

Today, we support over 250,000 clinicians across hundreds of care sites around the country. And we’re only just getting started: Healthcare’s watershed moment for AI-powered transformation is here – so join us in creating the technology to power healthcare!

About the Role

Commure + Athelas is seeking a Webflow/Frontend Developer to develop and optimize our website and web applications. This role will work closely with our engineering and design teams to build efficient, maintainable, and scalable frontend solutions that align with our technical and business goals. Your expertise in Webflow, web design principles, and front-end development will help us create an engaging and seamless online presence that aligns with our brand and business objectives.

What You'll Do

  • Develop, maintain, and enhance Commure’s website and landing pages using Webflow, HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and modern frontend frameworks
  • Work closely with designers to translate Figma designs into responsive, high-quality Webflow and landing pages
  • Maintain and extend design system consistency cross-browser and mobile compatibility
  • Implement animations, interactions, and other advanced Webflow features to improve user experience
  • Ensure high performance, accessibility, and SEO best practices across all web assets
  • Maintain a scalable and well-structured Webflow CMS
  • Implement frontend logic, reusable components, and integrate APIs to enhance functionality
  • Troubleshoot and resolve website and web application performance or technical issues
  • Stay up to date with the latest trends in frontend development, Webflow capabilities, and best coding practices

What You Have

  • 3+ years of experience in frontend development with a strong portfolio of responsive and interactive web applications
  • Proficiency in Webflow, HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and frontend frameworks like React or Vue
  • Experience with web performance optimization, accessibility, and best coding practices
  • Familiarity with analytics tools and debugging techniques
  • Experience integrating APIs and third-party services
  • Strong understanding of version control (Git) and CI/CD workflows
  • Ability to work independently, manage multiple projects, and collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams

Why you’ll love working at Commure + Athelas:

  • Highly Driven Team: We work hard and fast for exceptional results, knowing we’re doing mission-driven work to transform the country’s largest sector. 
  • Strong Backing: We are backed by top investors including General Catalyst, Sequoia, Y Combinator, Lux, Human Capital, 8VC, Greenoaks Capital and Elad Gil. 
  • Incredible Growth: Prior to our merger, Commure and Athelas had independently grown more than 500% YoY for three consecutive years. We’ve achieved Series D funding, have an industry-leading runway, and continue to scale rapidly. 
  • Competitive Benefits: Flexible PTO (pending specific geographical locations) , medical, dental, vision, maternity and paternity leave. Note that benefits are subject to change and may vary based on jurisdiction.

Commure + Athelas is committed to creating and fostering a diverse team. We are open to all backgrounds and levels of experience, and believe that great people can always find a place. We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations to all applicants throughout the application process.

Please be aware that all official communication from us will come exclusively from email addresses ending in @getathelas.com, @commure.com or @augmedix.com. Any emails from other domains are not affiliated with our organization.

​

Employees will act in accordance with the organization’s information security policies, to include but not limited to protecting assets from unauthorized access, disclosure, modification, destruction or interference nor execute particular security processes or activities. Employees will report to the information security office any confirmed or potential events or other risks to the organization. Employees will be required to attest to these requirements upon hire and on an annual basis.

title: Webflow Developer
jobDescription:
The Webflow Web Developer Contractor will support the Web team in developing, managing, and optimizing websites built on Webflow for a variety of clients. This role is primarily project-based and involves executing technical updates, content edits, and performance optimizations to ensure seamless website functionality. The developer will play a key role in supporting SEO initiatives and enabling internal teams to focus on strategy rather than platform-specific learning.

​

Requirements:

As a Webflow Web Developer, you will...

  • Develop and customize Webflow websites for clients across multiple industries.
  • Implement SEO optimizations, including structured data, on-page enhancements, and performance improvements within Webflow.
  • Execute technical updates and troubleshoot issues within Webflow’s CMS and custom code editor.
  • Work closely with account and SEO teams to ensure seamless site implementations without requiring them to navigate Webflow’s complexities.
  • Assist with website launches and ongoing maintenance.
  • Provide light training and documentation to help internal teams manage minor updates in Webflow.
  • Collaborate with SEO, Content, and Design teams to ensure development aligns with brand and UX/UI best practices.

Requirements:

  • Expert-level experience with Webflow (this is a must—candidates should be highly proficient).
  • Strong knowledge of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, with experience customizing Webflow sites.
  • Experience implementing SEO best practices in Webflow, including metadata, schema markup, and site speed optimizations.
  • Ability to translate design mockups and wireframes into fully functional Webflow websites.
  • Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to troubleshoot Webflow-specific issues efficiently.
  • Excellent communication and project management skills to collaborate with cross-functional teams.
  • Minimum 2 years of industry experience working with Webflow in a professional setting.

Nice to Have:

  • Experience with Webflow CMS migrations and API integrations.
  • Familiarity with other CMS platforms (WordPress, Shopify, etc.).
  • Knowledge of UX/UI best practices and responsive design principles.
  • Examples of Webflow projects showcasing development expertise.

Benefits:

  • Option to work completely remote

Logical is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We strongly encourage candidates of all different backgrounds and identities to apply. Each new hire is an opportunity for us to bring in a different perspective, and we are always eager to further diversify our company.

title: Website Designer
jobDescription:
Website Designer – Full-Time/Part-Time

Location: Oakville, Ontario (Remote or Hybrid options available)

Job Summary:

We are seeking a talented and creative Website Designer to join our growing team. The ideal candidate will be responsible for designing and developing visually appealing, user-friendly, and responsive websites for our business ventures, which include real estate, immigration consultancy, and other service-oriented platforms.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Design modern, clean, responsive websites using WordPress, Webflow, Wix, or custom code.

  • Work closely with the marketing team to integrate social media, lead capture forms, and analytics tools.
  • Troubleshoot bugs, update plugins/themes, and maintain site security.

Qualifications & Skills:

  • Proven experience as a web designer or in a similar role (Portfolio required).
