title: Graphic Designer
jobDescription:
**This is a remote position for candidates all over Canada. There is a chance we will move into an office in the GTA, however, we will always offer WFH employment.

If you love creating multi-platform social media graphics, your eyes light up when you’re mocking up an intuitive and engaging website layouts in Figma, you have fun editing video content for Reels and TikTok, you can whip out email designs using Figma components that make people want to click, you’re not afraid to leverage Adobe AI (or other tools) to quickly fix up the focus or white balance on RAW photo assets, or even use it to create net new image or vector files, get excited crafting and developing branding design (maybe even packaging design, too), get a kick out of creating stop motion or other small animated graphics, and enjoy doing it all in sweatpants in front of the TV while Friends or The Office is on in the background and virtually hanging out with some cool people, then we have the position for you!

We are hiring a full-time Graphic Designer with a focus on UI/UX design, social media graphics and short form videos, and email designs, to work in our design & dev team, while collaborating with our cross-functional marketing and customer service teams to enable our "Full Circle Approach to Digital Marketing" methodology. If you have a passion for making digital deliverables that are eye-catching, intuitive, and that inspire action, we’d love to hear from you!

Founded in 2015, we are a 20+ person Shopify Plus digital marketing agency working remotely and based in Toronto. We do everything related to digital marketing including, but not limited to; paid and organic social media, email/SMS marketing and automation, website design & development, influencer marketing, branding and design, Ecommerce & Amazon management, US based fulfillment, and online customer service, for clients across the world in industry verticals ranging from skincare, apparel, beauty, restaurants, home decor, CPGs, baby products, health and wellness, real estate, accounting, and more.

Some of our clients have been featured in Vogue, Cosmopolitan, Forbes, Business Insider, GOOP, and are sold in major retail chains like Walmart, Target, and Loblaws, and we even have a client who hosted the White House Correspondents Dinner!

WORK WITH US AND YOU WOULD BE:

  • Designing Social Media Graphics: working alongside our social media team to create on-brand, engaging static and animated graphics that align with corresponding copy and underlying strategy to be used across social platforms
  • Designing Websites & Landing Pages: creating visually appealing, accessible, intuitive, and high-converting website UI mockups across mobile and desktop placements using Figma for the Shopify, Webflow, and WordPress platforms, as well we performing design review with the development team to ensure consistency between mockup and live builds
  • Designing Advertising Creative: working with our Growth team to create high converting and strategic static, motion, and video assets across Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, Google and other advertising ecosystems
  • Supporting the Design & Building of Email Layouts: supporting our Lifecycle team in designing email templates via Figma and building them in the corresponding ESPs (Klaviyo, Attentive, etc.)
  • Owning Brand Identity, Brand Guidelines, & Quality Management: ensuring that our Clients’ brand guidelines are adhered to across all creative deliverables by creating and referencing brand documentation, and, in some cases, developing new brand guidelines where needed, and ensuring all assets align with Client brand guidelines, as well as In Social's high standard of deliverable quality
  • Designing Print & Product Collateral: as requested (infrequently), designing print collateral such as business cards, banners, flyers, patterns, merchandise, or other physical objects that align with clients' existing digital brand identity in accordance with relevant printing requirements
  • Providing Strategic & Creative Insight: lending your knowledge of design and design standards, as well as ideas and feedback to team members to ensure we are providing the highest quality deliverables possible
  • Continuous Improvement: always improving your skill set, knowledge, and performance through self-development, training courses, webinars, as well as collaborating with, and taking feedback from, our Director of Brand strategy
  • Supporting Deadline Management: working with your team lead and Project Coordinator to ensure deadlines can be met or requesting additional time as needed
  • Optimizing from Insights: receiving data-backed feedback from our Digital Performance Analyst, as well as other team members to identify opportunities and optimizations to our creative deliverables to drive stronger results
  • Contributing to the Full Circle Approach: being a team player and collaborating across all service verticals to understand, adapt, strategize, and deliver on digital strategies that consider the entire customer journey, in an effort to drive the best results possible to our clients

YOU WILL BE MEASURED AGAINST

  • Your ability to produce, create, design, and deploy high quality assets (qualitative)
  • Your ability to deliver assets within given timelines and provided budgets (quantitative)
  • The overall results of the deliverables you create such as Click-Through-Rates (CTRs), Thumbstop Rates, Impressions, Views, etc. (quantitative)
  • Client satisfaction (qualitative)
  • Your ability to take strategic concepts and create impactful, captivating, and engaging artwork (qualitative)
  • Your teamwork - everyone relies on each other for success! (qualitative)

TO WORK WITH US YOU SHOULD HAVE:

  • At least 1- 2 years of experience working in the Adobe Suite and know it inside and out (Photoshop, Illustrator, Premier, After Effects)
  • At least 1-2 years of experience working with Figma
  • At least 1-2 years of experience designing for both organic and paid social media placements
  • At least 1-2 years of experience designing email layouts
  • At least 1-2 years of experience creating intuitive, accessible, and responsive web experiences, and, either handing them off to, or working in conjunction with, a developer
  • A working knowledge of Ecommerce
  • The ability to show examples of your work
  • An understanding of how to leverage analytics to inform and improve your designs
  • Exceptional attention to detail and include the code isgd2025 in your cover letter
  • An appreciation for a super casual work environment, but still know how to be productive
  • The need to enjoy your job, be a team player, and do cool things with cool people

WHEN YOU WORK WITH US, YOU GET:

  • A comfortable work environment, being remote (forever) with a potential hybrid model addition (if you choose)
  • A team that actually enjoys each other, support each others, and works collaboratively together
  • Weekly stretch and game time (you won’t be able to beat our boss at Mario Kart Tour, trust us)
  • Events like summer retreats in Muskoka, winter holiday parties, murder mystery party nights, painting and drink making events, and whatever else you can think of!
  • Health spending account (benefits)
  • 2 weeks vacation + 5 personal days
  • Free swag (we have a store and a rewards program for said store: shop.insocial.ca)
  • Additional opportunities for bonuses based on trackable performance
  • Opportunity for growth – we promote internally 95% of the time
  • Health benefits (we offer a Health Spending Account)
  • Free stuff from our clients
  • The chance to be creative, think outside the box, take risks, and do cool sh*t

WHAT YOU SHOULD KNOW IF YOU JOIN OUR TEAM:

  • Trustworthiness is one of the most important attributes you’ll need to be successful here
  • We train extremely hard upfront so that we can prepare you and let you go to do your thing
  • Our female led business is built to help women succeed in their careers without boundaries
  • We are an 2SLGBTQIA+, BIPOC, differently-abled allied agency
  • We expect a lot from each other, and we expect you to expect the same from us

If, after reading the above, this sounds like you, then we want to hear from you! We prefer that you shoot us a note with your CV to careers[at]insocial[dot]ca. Please also provide examples or a portfolio of your work.

Job Types: Full-time, Permanent

Pay: From $55,000.00 per year

  • Extended health care
  • Paid time off
  • Vision care

Flexible language requirement:

Schedule:

Education:

Experience:

  • Adobe Creative Suite: 1 year (preferred)
  • Figma: 1 year (preferred)
  • Graphic design: 1 year (preferred)

Language:

  • English (required)

Work Location: Remote

title: Marketing Web Designer & Developer (Remote - Raleigh, NC)
jobDescription:
Guidebook is a simple yet powerful event management software that lets you promote your event, manage registration and create an app in four easy steps with no technical skills required.

Thousands of organizations across the globe use Guidebook to create mobile applications for events, university campus open houses, venue guides, onboarding for associations, corporate training and so much more. These apps help people stay organized, connect with their peers, and access more information - but it doesn’t stop there.

Our mission is to support the people who bring people together - event organizers. Events have never felt more important in building connections and helping shape and change minds in today’s fractured world. We are a driven team on a mission to elevate event experiences for everyone – and believe we can accomplish that while highly valuing balance, togetherness, and fun!

ABOUT THE JOB:

You’re a hybrid thinker—a digital designer with a marketer’s mindset. You know how to make websites not just look good, but perform well. With a deep understanding of Webflow and a knack for intuitive user experience, you can confidently manage and optimize a site like guidebook.com, bringing both creative flair and strategic insight to the table. You’re equally at home collaborating with marketers, salespeople, and developers, and you’re excited to see your design choices translate into measurable business results.

In this role, you’ll take ownership of Guidebook’s web presence—rebuilding and maintaining our site in Webflow, supporting our martech integrations, and contributing to brand-aligned marketing design projects. You will play a central role in executing and iterating on digital strategies that drive awareness, conversions, and engagement.

Our ideal candidate blends strong visual design skills with technical chops. You understand how SEO, user experience, and design intersect—and you’re always looking for ways to improve site performance, conversion rates, and brand consistency. Experience in a fast-paced, collaborative B2B or SaaS environment is a big plus, especially if you’ve juggled multiple marketing initiatives at once.

WHAT YOU'LL DO:

  • Own and manage the Guidebook marketing website in Webflow, initially leading on a re-design and re-development project and then making updates and improvements as needed
  • Collaborate with marketers to build landing pages, optimize user flows, and support campaign launches
  • Apply technical SEO best practices to ensure discoverability and performance across pages
  • Manage the Guidebook Blog on WordPress
  • Use your HTML, CSS and Javascript skills to help manage our WordPress blog, and support important integrations
  • Proactively suggest and implement website improvements based on data, benchmarks, and user experience trends
  • Design visually compelling digital assets and maintain brand consistency across web and marketing materials
  • Contribute to the development of social and paid campaign assets where needed
  • Help connect web performance to marketing KPIs, including traffic, conversion rates, and SEO rankings
  • Manage projects alongside the marketing team within a structured project management process using two-week sprints in Jira
  • Bring a creative and analytical lens to problem-solving—balancing great design with measurable results

THE IDEAL CANDIDATE:

  • 3+ years of digital or marketing design experience, with a strong portfolio of Webflow design and development projects.
  • Experience building and implementing design systems in Webflow.
  • Demonstrated experience managing and optimizing a B2B marketing website
  • Proficient with HTML, CSS and Javascript - supporting our WordPress blog, and integrations with marketing platforms like Marketo and Chili Piper
  • Familiar with technical SEO, including on-page optimization, structure, and site health
  • Strong design sensibility and the ability to articulate a clear point of view based on data and brand goals
  • Experience analyzing site performance and translating insights into design and UX improvements
  • Collaborative and communicative—you enjoy working cross-functionally with marketers, sales, and leadership
  • Analytical yet creative—you’re eager to A/B test and refine assets to improve performance
  • Experience producing work through project management tools such as Jira, Asana, Monday, etc
  • Is able to work effectively with a manager based in a remote office
  • Bonus: Experience in a smaller B2B and/or SaaS company where you owned multiple design/development functions
  • Bonus: Experience with Google Tag Manager

WITHIN 3 MONTHS, YOU’LL:

  • Gain a solid understanding of Guidebook’s product, brand, customers, and website structure
  • Conduct a full audit of the website with recommendations for design, UX, and SEO improvements
  • Establish relationships across marketing, sales, and product teams
  • Begin contributing to new landing pages and website updates using Webflow
  • Participate in sprint planning and align your work within the team’s 2-week cadence
  • Understand the Guidebook tone and visual identity—and begin forming ideas for evolution
  • Identify low-hanging fruit to improve conversion rates and technical performance

WITHIN 6 MONTHS, YOU’LL:

  • Take full ownership of Guidebook’s Webflow site, managing updates and improvements with autonomy - including updates to our martech integrations
  • Take full ownership of Guidebook’s blog on WordPress, managing updates and improvements with autonomy
  • Regularly analyze and report on website performance—setting clear goals and benchmarks
  • Design and launch new campaign pages and test variations to improve engagement and lead gen
  • Partner with demand gen to create high-converting landing pages and performance-focused assets
  • Contribute to improving project workflows and creating reusable design components in Webflow

WITHIN 12 MONTHS, YOU’LL:

  • Establish a clear connection between your work and core marketing KPIs (e.g. SEO, conversion rates, engagement)
  • Standardize best practices for Webflow development, website QA, and marketing design operations
  • Build scalable systems to support growth—templates, automation, atoms/molecules
  • Play a leadership role in maintaining and evolving the Guidebook brand voice and visual identity
  • Drive measurable improvements in performance through data-informed design decisions
  • Be seen as the go-to resource for high-quality, performance-driven design within the marketing team

PERKS

  • 100% paid benefits: medical, dental, and vision
  • Short term and long term disability benefits
  • Unlimited vacation time
  • 401k program with matching benefit
  • Stock options
  • Make your workstation yours: desk customization (monitor; docks/stands; keyboards; whatever else you need to be productive)
  • Awesome annual company retreats!

EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER:

At Guidebook, we're committed to cultivating an inclusive work environment for everyone – and we know that's best achieved, in part, with a diverse workforce. We're always working on increasing diversity across the company. If you're looking to join a team that is smart, highly motivated, and also values work/life balance, we can't wait to hear how you can add to our growing culture!

We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, national origin, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any other federal, state or local protected class.

COMPENSATION:

The salary range for this role is $75,000-100,000 annually (including variable quarterly bonuses), depending on relevant experience.

title: Spécialiste SEO / SEO Specialist
jobDescription:
Spécialiste SEO

Qui nous sommes

Nous sommes Ubiweb, l'agence de marketing numérique qui connaît la plus forte croissance au Canada. Nous offrons des solutions numériques aux petites et moyennes entreprises. Nous sommes également des gens passionnés qui cherchons à avoir un impact positif sur les activités d’affaires de nos clients et dans la vie de chacun.

Intéressé à plonger dans l’univers passionnant du marketing digital et des nouvelles technologies?

Nous sommes à la recherche d’un(e) Spécialiste SEO créatif(ve), organisé(e) et motivé(e) qui souhaite travailler dans un environnement dynamique, au cœur d'une entreprise en pleine croissance!

Dans ton rôle, tu accompagneras nos abonnées avec leur stratégie de référencement organique, ainsi que l'optimisation de leur site web pour les moteurs de recherche. Tu collaboreras également à divers projets internes liés à nos offres SEO pour les abonnés Ubiweb.

Si tu souhaites développer ta carrière dans le marketing digital, ce poste est fait pour toi!

  • Fournir des recommandations basées sur les données et les bonnes pratiques de l’industrie pour déterminer la stratégie SEO des abonnés
  • Effectuer des recherches et des analyses de mots clés et élaborer des stratégies de référencement qui correspondent aux objectifs de nos abonnés
  • Effectuer des optimisations continues sur les sites de nos abonnés
  • Assurer le suivi et la communication des performances du référencement de nos abonnés à l'aide de nos outils d'analyse spécialisés
  • Être en tout temps un ambassadeur pour l’équipe d’Ubiweb

title: Webflow Creative Designer – No-Code Design & Development
jobDescription:
Join an innovative SaaS startup and be our first creative hire! As a Webflow Creative Designer, you will blend design and light development to shape our platform from the ground up.

​

What You’ll Do:

  • UI/UX Design: Lead the design of engaging web and mobile interfaces, creating captivating brand visuals using tools like Figma and Adobe Creative Suite.
  • No-Code Development: Build, maintain, and optimize landing pages and interactive features using Webflow and low-code platforms, ensuring seamless user experiences.
  • Social Media Content: Craft and execute eye-catching content strategies for LinkedIn, Instagram, and other platforms to boost awareness and engagement.
  • Brand Consistency: Ensure our brand remains cohesive across all customer touchpoints, enhancing our visual identity.
  • Tool Development: Help implement and optimize tools that enhance our creative workflow and productivity.
  • Collaboration with Founders: Translate visionary ideas into polished designs, coding, and content that resonate across our platform.

​

This Role is For You If:

  • You thrive on bringing life to clean Figma designs and enjoy seeing them in action on Webflow.
  • You have a knack for juggling typography, CSS, and creative storytelling without losing your momentum.
  • You want to be part of a small, dynamic team where your contributions are significant.

​

What You Bring:

  • 3–5 years of experience in design, light front-end development, and social media content creation.
  • A strong portfolio showcasing your UI/UX, brand design, and digital projects.
  • Proficiency in Figma, Adobe Creative Suite, and comfort with no-code tools like Webflow.
  • Solid HTML/CSS skills, with bonus points for JavaScript familiarity.
  • Experience in crafting and managing engaging social media campaigns.
  • A startup-ready mindset; creative, resourceful, and comfortable with fast-paced environments.

​

Why Join Us:

  • Competitive salary up to $80,000 based on experience.
  • Early-stage equity with potential for significant upside.
  • Direct mentorship from experienced founders and a voice in key strategic decisions.
  • Work from the vibrant mHUB in Chicago, with flexible remote options.

​

If you’re ready to bring your creativity and innovation to a groundbreaking startup, let’s connect! We’re excited to see how your skills can shape our future.

title: Webflow Developer
jobDescription:
Are you excited about what is possible with tools like bubble․io, Webflow or Integromat and believe that no-code development will be one of the main drivers for innovation in the next few years? You want to turn your hobby into a profession? This is your chance to become a full-time No-Code Developer (Webflow) (m/f/d).

Drive innovation with us

Using the latest low-code technologies only, we can realize projects in a fraction of time compared to regular dev teams. Where others need months, we only need weeks - enabling startups and corporates to bring innovations to market particularly quick and move faster. Although we just have started to become Europe's leading low-code powerhouse, in the last year our startups, such as Avi Medical or Caronsale, already raised about USD 120 million. We develop first-moving digital products.

Tasks

We are looking for someone to support our team with implementing complex no-code projects. Experience with no-code tools like Webflow, Integromat, bubble․io or Shopify is an advantage, but experience with other no-code tools is also sufficient. What matters most is that you want to learn a more efficient way of developing digital products. In general, your tasks will be to:

  • Create digital products
  • You will be developing innovative digital products with tools such as bubble․io or Webflow in close collaboration with your project peers.
  • Increase value-added
  • When developing, one of our core values is to always drive value-added. That means you are not only developing but actively consulting our clients, helping to create the best version possible.
  • Foster internal products
  • Aiming to become Europe's leading low-code powerhouse, our company cannot be optimized enough. You will be helping to develop internal tools or create own products developed with no-code from ideation to market entry.

Requirements

Below you can find soft requirements that we recommend having when starting at 02100 Digital.

  • Basic no-code understanding
  • You have first experience with creating digital products using low- or no-code tools such as bubble․io or Webflow. Ideally, you have completed the bubble․io or Webflow university.
  • Find solutions fast
  • You are goal-oriented and have solid communication skills.
  • Agile way of working
  • You know how to collaborate in digital projects and ideally have worked with agile principles before.

Benefits

At 02100 Digital we know that each and everyone in our team is what drives success so we try to give as much back as possible.

  • Join a team of makers
  • If you join 02100 Digital you will become part of a young team that consists of certified no-code experts (Webflow, bubble․io, Shopify and Integromat). As part of your journey with us, you will be trained to become a top-notch no-code dev and join forces with like-minded professionals.
  • Competitive profit share
  • We distribute 10% of annual profits to our team according to our new Performance-based bonus system.
  • Training budget
  • We provide an annual budget of €1.000 EUR for each employee to participate in workshops, courses or other events teaching new skills.
  • Brand new office
  • In 2021 we moved into our new 200m² office in one of Hamburg's prime locations and made it as cozy as possible. In our two-story backyard house located at Hamburg's well-known "Mittelweg", the ground floor is dedicated to spending your free time, relaxing and eating while the first floor is equipped for a productive and creative work environment.
  • Team events
  • we have team events such as team dinner, TGIF meetings and various other after-work events. Besides, we are leading multiple Meetup Groups that will become more active after the pandemic.
  • Hybrid working
  • If you are not working remotely, we offer a hybrid way of working. Mondays for example are always remote (if you wish).

Are you interested in joining our team and willing to help us become Europe's leading low-code powerhouse? Then quickly apply using our Careers page on our website. We will only review applications sent via our website.

title: Webflow Developer
jobDescription:
Wonderist Agency is a full-service marketing agency that supports almost 1000 dentists around the country. We pride ourselves on ditching the old fashioned marketing model of stock photos and templated sites. We strive to tell our clients’ stories with bespoke brands, impactful photo and video shoots, and stunning website design. We also provide ongoing services such as SEO, PPC, Social Media Marketing, and Funnels to help our practices get butts in seats. (Here is a great example of our work: precisiondentalbowlinggreen.com)

We need a skilled Webflow Developer with the flexibility to turn on your heels when priorities change, the thoughtfulness to do what’s in the best interest of the team, and the desire to always produce your best work. 

We’re a team of over 55 passionate marketers nestled in sunny San Diego. We are walking distance from local hangouts, bars, and the famed Liberty Station. While many companies simply check the required boxes when it comes to culture, a well-balanced team and positive office culture has been the core of our success from the start. We are a social team at heart, and have embraced a hybrid work model where team members come in at least two days a week. This allows us to work from home and have flexibility, without altogether forgoing the excitement and moments of collaboration and inspiration you can only get from in-person time. Not to mention, we also genuinely enjoy spending time together and socializing.

We celebrate new ideas and prioritize a culture of trust and respect where team members can grow and take risks. Each quarter we host events under our four cultural pillars; health & wellness, personal & professional development, social good, and social. We are proud to have been named one of San Diego Business Journal’s Best Places to work in 2018, 2021, 2022 and 2023, made the Inc 5000 Regionals Fastest Growing Companies 2021, 2022, and 2024, made the San Diego Business Journal’s Fastest Growing Private Companies list in 2019, 2020, 2022, and 2023 and made the Inc. 5000 for the last 4 years! 

If you work at Wonderist, you likely enjoy a challenge, appreciate autonomy, actually care about clients, and are looking for a team that you can enjoy being part of.

Job Description

The Webflow Developer will be responsible for creative content management design and ongoing maintenance, building client marketing websites that drive leads and convert leads to customers. This role is development-focused and requires foundational understanding of CSS, HTML and Javascript. We are looking for an experienced Webflow designer interested in executing front end website projects, directly applying their creativity to websites during the build process. A strong eye for design, organized workflow, and ability to multi-task is essential.

Essential Functions:

  • Designing & Building Websites: You will be designing & building your own websites as well as building custom websites mocked by our Design team in Figma. Using the Webflow content management system, you will create unique, aesthetically exciting websites that reflect the clients’ brand while adhering to our high quality standards.
  • Ongoing Maintenance: Our client relationships don’t end when the website launches. Our clients will submit post-launch update requests that you will vet for clarity and scope, make recommendations on, and ultimately execute.
  • Creative Direction: Conceptualizing page layouts, animations and user interactions are critical to successful website projects. You will provide clients with suggested color palettes, typography pairings, and high-level page layouts prior to full build-out.
  • Quality Assurance: You ensure that your own work and that of your peers is branded appropriately for each client, accurate, and high-quality. From reviewing appropriate padding, alignment and animation execution to testing mobile device responsiveness, you will always strive for an aesthetic that is fresh and effective.
  • Marketing Strategy: You will make recommendations on design, conversion opportunities, and user experience to make the most of a client’s marketing package. You will participate in client kick-off calls and speak directly with the end client. You think critically and make judgements in the best interest of the website visitor.

Core Competencies:

  • Intuitive Communication: You will interface directly with clients at specific touch points and will be responsible for communicating clearly and effectively, both with clients and internal teams. You must be personable, professional, and responsive. You will need to know when to ask the right questions and be prepared to get answers to our front line sales and account management teams quickly and accurately.
  • Obsessive Attention to Detail: You must recognize when spacing isn’t quite right or an extra digit exists in a phone number. You will share accountability for the accuracy and quality of our company’s work. Attention to detail is crucial, so please include the title of your favorite movie or book at the beginning of your email or application.
  • Expert Organization and Project Management: If someone asks you to describe yourself and you wouldn't say "organized," you need not apply. You must be organized and able to multitask many projects at once, hitting your deliverable deadlines at various stages of the project lifecycle. A honed sense of urgency in order to prioritize your workload is paramount to success at our agency.
  • Keen Analytical Skills: We measure everything we do by its ROI. You will need a basic understanding of marketing metrics and KPIs, along with the ability to dissect and solve problems through data. Working on and with the web team, you will need to be able to recognize when an approach isn’t working and make suggestions to improve our processes and procedures in order to gain back efficiencies that ultimately benefit our clients’ website projects.
  • “Big Picture” Thinking and Knowledge of Digital Marketing: Every digital marketing service we offer ties into the next. When a client makes a request to update their website, how will that affect their SEO or PPC campaigns? We need someone who can see the connections and make good choices for both the client and the agency as a whole.
  • Resourceful and Confident Self-Starter: Once you learn the ropes, we’ll expect you to own your projects to keep them moving forward. You will need to take the initiative to make decisions and recommendations, and to follow up with Account Managers to progress projects and hit our monthly projections. You will solve problems every single day, so we need someone with a solutions-oriented mind.
  • Takes Pride In Work: We need someone who takes any project, big or small, and puts everything they have into it. Mistakes are okay. Sloppiness is not.

Personality Musts:

‍Our perfect fit is someone who is a problem solver who understands the difference between “yes, and…” and “yes, but…”. You should be a hungry, scrappy personality with a positive attitude and aims to deliver. Working in the client service industry can mean going the extra mile, and we need someone who is always willing to rise to the occasion for our clients and strives to put forward beautiful work that exceeds client expectations. Someone who is detailed and organized, driven and displays vigor and comes ready to play every day. Confidence, self-discipline, and a congenial, curious mind are things we love and admire.

Minimum Experience

  • 1+ years designing websites in the Webflow content management system
  • Familiarity with Lumos, Client-First, and other Webflow build frameworks.
  • Understanding of other content management systems (CMS) such as WordPress, SquareSpace, Duda or Wix.
  • Foundational understanding of HTML, CSS and Javascript and how they work together
  • A passion for helping clients and small businesses succeed

Bonus Points for Applicants With…

(if you don’t have these but are organized and want to learn, we want to hear from you!)

  • Volume web design experience in an agency setting
  • Digital marketing experience (including but not limited to: Google Analytics, Google Ads, SEO, Social Media Content and Ads Management)
  • GSAP Animation experience
  • Project management experience
  • UX/UI design background
  • Knowledge of dental industry or dental services

​

title: CONVERSION RATE OPTIMIZATION (CRO) SPECIALIST
jobDescription:
Full job description

WHY YOU SHOULD APPLY:

Coalition Technologies is devoted to delivering clients the highest quality work while providing our team a fun, thriving, and innovative environment. Along with the opportunity for tremendous career growth and rapid advancement, CT offers:

​

The most competitive profit-sharing bonus plan in the industry, paying up to 50% of company profits to full-time employees each month!

A highly competitive Paid Time Off plan, promoting quality work-life balance.

Subsidized gym memberships to help team members feel their best.

Medical, dental, vision, and life insurance packages for all US-based team members.

International Health Insurance Reimbursement Program for all international team members, a benefit unique to Coalition.

Device upgrade and learning reimbursement programs.

Motivating career development plans with clearly defined goals and rewards.

Additional job-specific incentives and bonuses.

Plus, 100% of our team works remotely with the support of time tracking software. Our company culture specializes in supporting remote team members, and we’ve been doing so for more than a decade. CT welcomes your application, wherever in the world it's coming from!

CORE RESPONSIBILITIES:

As a CRO Specialist, you will use a mix of creativity and strong analytical skills to identify website optimization opportunities for your clients and then develop and execute strategy to turn those ideas into an optimization and testing plan.

​

A CRO Specialist should have a strong understanding of website content management systems, including major eCommerce solutions, and should have enough statistical and analytical abilities to help us understand data in a way that results in actionable suggestions for our clients.

​

YOUR DUTIES AND TASKS:

Performing audits of clients’ web analytics implementations and making recommendations to ensure accurate tracking is in place.

Using qualitative and quantitative sources of data to develop and execute a conversion rate optimization strategy and testing plan for your clients.

Developing hypotheses and building and executing A/B or multivariate tests according to the optimization strategy and testing plan.

Partnering with our internal design and development team to recommend changes based on winning test results.

Using an iterative approach to optimization, continuously taking test results and data into account in formulating new tests and hypotheses.

Helping create initial, quarterly, and monthly client reports based on test results and other ongoing initiatives.

Monitoring industry trends and new product offerings to stay up to speed and ahead of the competition.

Contributing to the development and improvement of the CRO team’s processes and strategies.

Performing other duties as assigned.

MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS:

Strong analytical and creative problem-solving skills.

Experience in HTML, CSS, JavaScript and/or jQuery.

Expertise with Google Analytics, Google Ads, and other analytical tools.

Experience using major web content management systems including Wordpress, BigCommerce, Shopify, and Magento.

Experience in cloud-based CRM solutions a plus (Salesforce, Hubspot, etc).

A demonstrated capacity and willingness to learn new technologies.

Ability to effectively communicate with clients and colleagues at all levels.

Intermediate to advanced Excel skills.

Ability to type 50+ wpm.

We are looking for talented and diligent candidates who excel in our skills tests, and will consider these candidates even if past experience or educational background criteria aren't met.

​

California, New York, Washington, and Colorado: starting base pay for this position ranges between $17 - $35 per hour.

​

Compensation may vary based on factors such as experience, qualifications, skills test performance, geographic location, and seniority of the position offered. Outside of California, New York, Washington, and Colorado compensation may fall outside the above ranges.

title: Senior SEO Content Specialist - Hong Kong, China
jobDescription:
At Aspire, we’re more than just a FinTech company—we’re the leading all-in-one financial operating system built to empower the world’s innovators and entrepreneurs. We are on a mission to reinvent business finance, empowering startups and businesses to realise their full potential.

Founded in 2018, Aspire has raised over USD 300M+ across equity and debt from world-class investors. In 2023, we successfully closed an oversubscribed USD 100 million Series C equity round led by Sequoia Capital and Lightspeed Ventures with participation of Tencent, Paypal Ventures, LGT Capital Partners, Picus Capital and MassMutual Ventures. To power our solutions, we have partnered with some of the best companies in the world such as Visa and Wise and empowered more than 50,000 businesses using our suite of products.

Aspire has consistently been recognized for excellence, earning Best Employer and Startup of the Year by the Asia FinTech Awards in 2022 and 2023, ranked as LinkedIn’s Top Startup in Singapore, and listed on CB Insights’ Top 100 Global Fintech in 2023 and 2024.

You will be amazed by the energy and experience of our team! Aspire serves as an environment for you to innovate and drive change with our team of ex-entrepreneurs, ex-founders, and high-achievers with international and diverse backgrounds.

Are you a top talent who is passionate about entrepreneurship? Join our rapidly growing team to make an impact in the fintech space! 

About the team:

At Aspire, we’re on a mission to establish ourselves as the unrivaled #1 Finance Operating System for businesses in APAC. Our Marketing Team is at the forefront of this exciting journey, playing a pivotal role in building our brand, acquiring new customers, and nurturing existing relationships. Our Marketing team crafts and executes strategies to resonate with our target audience, and also to scale the acquisition of new users by identifying untapped channels and optimizing conversions.

 

About the role:

As the SEO Content Manager for Hong Kong, you’ll be responsible for driving the growth of organic traffic in both English and Traditional Chinese. You’ll be responsible for ensuring that organic qualified traffic drives both online signups and generates quality leads to help us scale the acquisition of customers in Hong Kong. 

You’ll collaborate closely with the rest of our team working on the Hong Kong market, leaning on people like our Hong Kong Field Marketing Manager to gather or validate local insights that could make powerful content opportunities. You’ll report to Aspire’s Head of Digital Marketing, and work alongside a team of specialists who deliver content, creative production and website updates. This will be a hands-on role and you will be expected to be an individual contributor and manage freelancers and agencies to deliver ambitious goals. 

 

What you’ll be doing:

  • Drive SEO content production for the Hong Kong market in Traditional Chinese and English 
  • Own the delivery of organic growth for the Hong Market, identifying content and scalable opportunities 
  • Generate increasing online signups and leads to acquire new customers for Aspire 
  • Use market, competitor and keyword research to build a prioritised content plan 
  • Work with internal and outsourced resources to publish content at scale 
  • Use tools like Ahrefs for research and opportunity forecasting
  • Develop Aspire's expertise to deeply understand our product and target audience  
  • Measure and report content performance to iterate and optimise results 
  • Adhere to internal guidelines and processes to ensure we remain compliant 
  • Share market insights with the rest of the marketing team to inform broader campaigns 

 

Minimum qualifications:

  • Strategically minded and able to understand how to shape a plan to deliver results 
  • Curious by nature, you spot problems and identify solutions on your own
  • Highly process-driven, analytical, and conscientious
  • You have 3-4 years experience in SEO and understand best practices already 
  • You’re experienced in the use of SEO tools (Ahrefs, SEM Rush, Google Search Console, Google Analytics 4)
  • Based in Hong Kong (understanding of the startup scene is a plus) or have HK market knowledge 
  • Fluent in Traditional Chinese and English 
  • Ability to understand Aspire’s needs and organize workstreams and projects with clear timelines and deliverables
  • A background in a B2B company would be a significant advantage 
  • You should be a hands-on, growth-mindset, target and deadline driven team-player

 

What we offer

  • Uncapped flexible annual leave.
  • Hybrid work arrangement. 
  • Training subsidy for your professional growth.
  • Wellness benefit.
  • Team bonding budget to foster collaboration and sense of belonging.
  • Flexibility to work from anywhere (for up to 90 days per annum).
  • Culture is Key: We always strive to cultivate a special culture that brings special talents together - You can learn more about our culture on our careers site and LinkedIn Life page.

Equal Opportunity Statement 

Aspire is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to providing equal employment opportunities to all qualified individuals without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, or any other protected characteristic as outlined by applicable laws. 

Please note: by submitting your application, you acknowledge that you have read and understood Aspire’s [Data Protection Policy for Employees, Freelancers, Contractors and Job Applicants](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1vLznVZhG59UpJ2IO0vUyy0dskPf7ZAF/view?usp=sharing)[ (the “Policy”)](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1vLznVZhG59UpJ2IO0vUyy0dskPf7ZAF/view?usp=sharing), and consent to the collection, use and disclosure of your personal data by Aspire for the purposes set out in the Policy. You may withdraw consent for such collection, use and disclosure, and make an access or correction request in respect of your personal data, in accordance with the Policy by emailing people@aspireapp.com.

title: Senior Webflow Developer
jobDescription:
Full job description

Oct 15, 2024 (Upd Oct 19, 2024)

​

​

Remote

Job Description:

​

We are an agency seeking an experienced Senior Webflow Developer with a deep understanding of Webflow’s custom code capabilities and API. The ideal candidate will transform detailed Figma designs into fully functional Webflow websites, leveraging custom code to enhance functionality and user experience. Responsibilities include integrating APIs, optimizing performance, and ensuring responsive design across all devices.

title: UI/UX Designer (Remote Contractor)
jobDescription:
The UX/UI Designer Contractor will support the Web and SEO teams in enhancing user experience (UX) and conversion rate optimization (CRO) across client websites. This role requires a deep understanding of user behavior, site structure, and design best practices, going beyond simple content rearrangement for SEO. The designer will play a key role in analyzing existing websites, identifying UX improvements that align with SEO opportunities, and advising on landing page design and full-site optimization strategies.

​

Requirements:

As a UX/UI Designer, you will...

  • Analyze existing websites to identify user experience issues and SEO opportunities, prioritizing updates based on data-driven insights.
  • Provide strategic guidance on landing page design and site layouts to improve conversion rates and user engagement.
  • Collaborate with SEO, Content, and Web teams to ensure design changes support both user experience and search visibility.
  • Work with designers like Mallory and Maddie to enhance their UX expertise and improve overall design consistency.
  • Develop wireframes, prototypes, and user flows to optimize site structure and navigation.
  • Ensure best practices in mobile responsiveness, accessibility, and site speed.
  • Offer expert recommendations on UX/UI improvements that drive measurable business results.

Requirements:

  • 3+ years of experience in UX/UI design, with a portfolio demonstrating successful website optimizations and CRO improvements.
  • Strong background in user experience research, usability testing, and behavior analysis.
  • Experience using heatmaps, session recordings, and analytics tools to drive design decisions.
  • Proficiency in Figma, Adobe XD, or similar design tools.
  • Familiarity with SEO principles and how UX impacts search rankings and conversions.
  • Ability to work independently, providing strategic insights and design recommendations to improve site performance.

Nice to Have:

  • Experience working with SEO teams to align UX improvements with search performance goals.
  • Knowledge of CRO tools like Google Optimize, Hotjar, or A/B testing platforms.
  • Familiarity with Webflow, WordPress, or Shopify site structures.
  • Ability to train and mentor designers on best practices in UX/UI.

Benefits:

  • Option to work completely remote

Logical is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We strongly encourage candidates of all different backgrounds and identities to apply. Each new hire is an opportunity for us to bring in a different perspective, and we are always eager to further diversify our company.

title: SEO Content Contractor (Remote)
jobDescription:
An SEO Content Contractor at Logical Media Group will be responsible for various digital marketing activities, primarily focused on creating and developing strategy for copy with Search Engine Optimization (SEO) best practices in mind in order to boost visibility of client marketing efforts and Logical Media Group internal marketing efforts, particularly in search engine ranking for projects as assigned. This will include site research, keyword research, content development, optimization, and performance analysis for our clients.

​

Requirements:

Deliverables

  • Content Creation: Creating and strategizing high-quality, SEO-optimized content for both Logical Media Group and our external clients target audiences. Responsible for writing 15-20 pieces of optimized keyword-rich SEO content based on variety of industries, topics, trends, and valuable industry updates which should be tailored to search engines and provide high value to targeted audiences.
  • Keyword research: Use keyword research and content strategies for targeting and ranking for high-volume keywords.
  • Optimization: Post optimized articles and/or optimized content following industry standards to our CMS platform, social channels and more. Optimize current copy, landing pages and metadata for maximum organic exposure and conversions while maintaining engaging targeted content.
  • Edit: Provide oversight of SEO content written by team members by editing and optimizing per SEO best practices.
  • Reporting: Outline key insights and takeaways from reports and other tools such as Google Analytics and Google Search console. Effectively articulate results of recommended optimizations and initiatives and formulate next steps accordingly (whether positive or negative).

Strategy & Project Management

  • SEO Content Strategies: Craft SEO Content strategies and provide proactive recommendations for clients tailored to their unique goals, strengths, and challenges. Prioritize initiatives by impact and ease of implementation.
  • Deliverable Timelines: Formulate timelines for key deliverables. Delegate and assign deadlines for key deliverables to supporting team members.
  • Cross-Team Communication and Strategy: Work cross-functionally with web, paid, and creative teams to ensure on-time delivery of initiatives and cohesive digital strategy.
  • Account Monitoring: Monitor monthly retainer and project hours to ensure the client receives maximum value for retainer without significantly overspending on hours.

Additional Responsibilities

  • Continued learning: Keep up-to-date with and proactively research news and trends in SEO, content, analytics, trending topics, etc. and share with the team.
  • Business development: Identify opportunities for and pitch clients on up-sells to aid in the growth of SEO Content business.
  • Team training: Help train SEO Content support staff on basic management initiatives and setup tasks.

Requirements:

  • Basic knowledge of SEO and how it applies to Content marketing
  • Experience with tools such as Google Analytics, Google Search Console, SEMRush
  • Expertise in creative writing incorporating SEO best practices, critical analysis, and editing skills
  • Understanding of and experience in local search optimization
  • Understanding of basic HTML as it relates to SEO
  • Ability to implement foundational SEO recommendations in a Wordpress environment.
  • Ability to apply best practices and content strategy on various social media platforms (Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, Youtube, etc.)
  • Excellent analytical, organizational, project management and time management skills
  • Attention to detail and ability to effectively manage multiple clients and deadlines simultaneously
  • Bachelor’s Degree in relevant fields such as english, journalism, Communication, Marketing, etc.
  • At least 3 years of experience in writing for online publications

Benefits:

Logical is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We strongly encourage candidates of all different backgrounds and identities to apply. Each new hire is an opportunity for us to bring in a different perspective, and we are always eager to further diversify our company.

This position is for Canadian citizens, permanent residents of Canada, or foreign nationals with a valid Canadian work permit. One Net will not sponsor work or immigration visas at this time.

One Net often hires value-added contractors (freelancers) in the areas of design, illustration, web development, and video production. If you are a contractor interested in working with our agency, please send your resume and work examples to: partners@onenetinc.com

title: Webflow Developer
jobDescription:
Reel Unlimited is a global creative services solution offering startups and SMBs unlimited design and content writing at a flat rate. Launched in 2021 as a remote-first company, Reel Unlimited is pursuing its missing of delivering reliable, scalable, affordable, and better creative content to any startup. Today, Reel Unlimited serves over 300 startups and agencies, from bootstrapped or VC-backed. Check us out at www.reelunlimited.com. 

About the role

Reel Unlimited is currently looking for talented and motivated Webflow Developers to join our design team and get flat daily pay to design amazing Webflow websites and landing pages for our clients. As a freelance position, you will be compensated based on the number of Webflow projects you deliver.

Your responsibilities will include the following:

  • Use Webflow and Figma to build responsive marketing websites and landing pages.
  • Optimizing and updating existing Webflow website's performance, SEO and responsiveness.

How it works: 

  • Once accepted into our network, you get daily notifications about Webflow orders matching your skills.
  • You will have access to our dashboard to review project details, including deadlines, and will also have access to a private Slack channel for communication and collaboration with clients. 
  • Every 2 weeks, you bill us for all your completed orders, and we direct deposit amounts owed to your bank account within 2 business days.

Compensations:

  • Compensation includes a flat rate of $100-$200 per Webflow task, bi-weekly payouts for completed tasks, and bonuses and incentives. So, there is no need to fight for clients or pitch without the promise of pay.

The minimum requirements for this position include the following:

  • Proficient in Webflow and Figma
  • Experience with Client-First Style System for Webflow
  • Good understanding of Webflow CMS and SEO principles
  • Good English communication, detail-oriented and customer-friendly.
  • Respect the average project turnaround of 48–72 hours per order.
  • Deliver a minimum of 2 orders per day, with the ability to take on multiple projects simultaneously.
  • Remote, freelance position with the freedom to choose when and where you work.
  • Strong communication, conceptual thinking, typography skills, and design skills.
  • Good interpersonal skills and client-handling experience.
  • Team player with a sense of humor.

How to apply:

Please send a portfolio of at least 5 completed Webflow projects with public access to apply. If your style matches our needs, we will contact you for the next steps. We prioritize applicants who are ready to start immediately.

title: E-Commerce Sales Manager/ CRO Specialist
jobDescription:

It is important to note that this is an in-office position, not remote or hybrid. Our office is located in Buda, TX

​

Position: E-Commerce Sales Manager/ CRO Specialist

​

Overview:The E-Commerce Sales Manager, CRO Specialist will report directly to the Director of Sales & Business Development and collaborate closely with the Digital Marketing team to drive the growth of our online store sales. This role is essential for enhancing the customer experience and increasing sales through strategic advertising, customer experience initiatives, and efficient inventory management.

Provide regular reporting and insights on CRO performance metrics, test results, and actionable recommendations to key stakeholders.

title: E-commerce Specialist
jobDescription:
Full job description

About Us: AYC is a fast-growing brand specializing in the beauty, spa and salon equipment industry. Our vision is to build a world class Distribution Channel for the Salon Furnishing Industry. We are committed to delivering high-quality products and an exceptional shopping experience to our customers. As we continue to scale, we’re looking for a dynamic and results-driven Amazon Marketplace Specialist to join our team and drive growth on Amazon.

​

Job Overview:The Amazon Marketplace Specialist will be responsible for managing and optimizing our Amazon seller account to drive revenue and grow the brand's presence across Amazon's various platforms. This role requires a deep understanding of Amazon's marketplace ecosystem, including listings, advertising, inventory management, and customer service, combined with strong analytical skills to measure performance and implement improvements.

​

Key Responsibilities:

​

Account Management: Oversee day-to-day operations of our Amazon seller account, ensuring all listings are accurate, well-optimized, and compliant with Amazon policies.

Product Listings: Create, optimize, and update product listings, ensuring they are keyword-rich, accurate, and visually appealing to maximize visibility and conversion rates.

Inventory Management: Monitor inventory levels, analyze trends, and work with the logistics team to ensure products are in stock and shipped promptly.

Pricing Strategy: Develop competitive pricing strategies by researching market trends and analyzing competitor pricing to ensure our products remain competitive and profitable.

Advertising & Promotions: Plan, execute, and manage Amazon advertising campaigns (Sponsored Products, Sponsored Brands, etc.) to drive traffic, increase conversions, and improve ROI.

Data Analysis & Reporting: Analyze key performance metrics (sales, traffic, ACoS, ROAS, etc.) and generate detailed reports to track performance and identify areas for improvement.

Customer Service: Monitor customer reviews and feedback, responding to inquiries and resolving issues in a timely and professional manner to maintain a positive brand image.

Market Research: Continuously track industry trends, Amazon updates, and competitor strategies to identify new opportunities and maintain a competitive edge.

A/B Testing: Conduct A/B tests on product listings, titles, images, and other key areas to determine the most effective strategies for improving conversion rates and sales.

Qualifications:

​

Proven experience as an Amazon Marketplace Specialist or similar role with a track record of success in growing sales on Amazon.

Strong understanding of Amazon Seller Central and Amazon's ecosystem (FBA, PPC, A+ Content, etc.).

Expertise in keyword research, SEO best practices, and Amazon’s algorithm.

Experience with Amazon Advertising (Sponsored Products, Sponsored Brands, and other ad types).

Analytical mindset with the ability to analyze data, identify trends, and generate actionable insights.

Excellent communication and problem-solving skills.

Ability to work independently and as part of a team.

Strong organizational and multitasking skills.

Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, PowerPoint, Word, Teams) and data visualization tools.

Preferred Qualifications:

​

Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, or a related field.

Experience with third-party Amazon management tools (e.g., Helium 10, Rithum, Fishbowl, Quartile, etc.)

Experience in managing multi-marketplace accounts (e.g., Walmart, eBay, etc.).

Familiarity with Amazon's global marketplaces and international shipping.

Why Join Us?

​

Competitive salary and benefits package.

Opportunities for professional growth and development.

A collaborative and dynamic work environment where your contributions make a direct impact.

Pay: $42,000.00 - $56,000.00 per year

401(k)

Dental insurance

Employee discount

Health insurance

Vision insurance

8 hour shift

​

Bachelor's (Preferred)

​

Marketing: 1 year (Preferred)

Digital Marketing Occupations: 1 year (Preferred)

Ability to Relocate:

​

Dallas, TX 75238: Relocate before starting work (Required)

title: SEM/SEO & Digital Marketing Specialist
jobDescription:
Our client, an innovative and rapidly expanding technology-driven third-party logistics (3PL) provider, is looking for a results-oriented SEM/SEO & Digital Marketing Specialist. This role will be instrumental in boosting online visibility, driving high-quality traffic, and generating leads across various digital channels. As the company broadens its reach into the U.S. market, this position will play a key role in refining search engine strategies and optimizing paid marketing efforts.

​

Key Responsibilities:

  • Search Engine Optimization (SEO):Develop and execute an SEO strategy to increase organic traffic and brand exposure.
  • Enhance website content, metadata, and structure by incorporating high-intent industry keywords (e.g., "top 3PL provider for e-commerce brands").
  • Conduct in-depth competitor analysis using tools like SEMrush, Ahrefs, and Google Search Console to improve rankings.
  • Oversee both on-page and off-page SEO tactics, including link-building initiatives and content optimization.
  • Implement local SEO best practices to enhance visibility in priority markets across Canada and the U.S.
  • Search Engine Marketing (SEM) & Paid Advertising:Design and manage Google Ads campaigns to attract high-quality leads.
  • Leverage LinkedIn Ads, Google Display Network (GDN), and remarketing strategies to maximize conversion rates.
  • Conduct ROI analysis and A/B testing to enhance SEM campaign performance and efficiency.
  • Research and bid on high-value keywords relevant to the industry (e.g., "alternatives to Flexport," "3PL fulfillment in Toronto").
  • Content & Website Optimization:Work alongside the content team to create SEO-optimized blog posts, landing pages, and case studies.
  • Update and refine website pages using WordPress, ensuring optimal performance, fast load speeds, mobile responsiveness, and structured data implementation.
  • Apply multi-language SEO techniques to attract international clients seeking fulfillment services in North America.
  • Analytics & Performance Tracking:Utilize Google Analytics, Looker, and Google Data Studio to monitor website performance, engagement, and lead conversion rates.
  • Prepare monthly reports detailing campaign effectiveness and provide data-driven recommendations for ongoing improvements.
  • Align SEO and SEM initiatives with business development and sales objectives.
  • Qualifications & Experience:Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Digital Marketing, Information Technology, or a related discipline.
  • Minimum of three years of hands-on experience in SEO, SEM, PPC campaigns, and lead generation.
  • Proficiency in Google Analytics, Google Ads, SEMrush, Ahrefs, Moz, and Google Search Console.
  • Knowledge of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript for SEO enhancement, along with WordPress website management.
  • Experience with A/B testing, bid management, and performance optimization tools.
  • Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret data and apply insights effectively.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
  • Preferred Qualifications:Background in logistics, e-commerce, or fulfillment services.
  • Understanding of B2B lead generation strategies tailored for enterprise clients.
  • Experience with API integrations, affiliate marketing, and advertising on platforms like Amazon Ads or Shopify SEO.
  • Familiarity with multi-language SEO techniques for global expansion.
  • Compensation & Benefits:$60,000 - $80,000 based on experience.
  • Comprehensive benefits package
  • Onsite parking
  • Annual performance-based bonus and salary reviews.

This is an exciting opportunity to contribute to a growing company at the forefront of tech-driven logistics. If you’re a strategic thinker passionate about digital marketing and search engine optimization, we encourage you to apply!

​

Only candidates already eligible to work in Canada will be reviewed. Only qualified candidates will be contacted.

title: Web Analytics and Optimization Manager
jobDescription:
At Sparklight/Cableone and our Cable One family of brands, we keep our customers and associates connected to what matters most. For our associates, that means: a thriving and rewarding career, respect for the communities where they live and work, a focus on health and wellness, an excellent work/life balance, and an open and inclusive workplace.

The Web Analytics and Optimization Manager to play a key role in overseeing website performance, conducting analytics, and implementing optimization strategies to drive direct-to-consumer customer acquisition and retention.

You will be responsible for data collection, analysis, full funnel reporting, conducting A/B testing, and collaborating with other teams to improve user experience and conversion rates.

As Web Analytics and Optimization Manager you will play a key role in enhancing the digital journey for prospects and customers by optimizing and personalizing customer touchpoints across digital channels. This individual will work closely with cross-functional teams to improve online engagement, satisfaction, and loyalty. This role focuses on creating a seamless digital experience, ensuring alignment with customer needs and business objectives.

The ideal candidate will possess a strong understanding of the competitors and trends within the internet, fixed wireless and mobile industries. The candidate also needs to provide high-level data analysis that translates raw data into actionable solutions for stakeholders to prioritize.

We are open to hiring remote if we find the right talent in any of the following states: AL, AR, AZ, FL, GA, IA, ID, IL, IN, KS, LA, MD, MN, MO, MS, NC, ND, NE, NM, NV, OR, OK, PA, SC, SD, TN, TX, UT.

What you will do to contribute to the company's success

  • Develop and implement web analytics strategies to rack key metrics and KPIs
  • Analyze website traffic and user engagement data to identify trends and areas for improvement.
  • Provide insights and recommendations to the Marketing and Media team based on data analysis.
  • Collaborate with stakeholders to create customized reports and dashboards.
  • Monitor and report on the performance of digital marketing campaigns.
  • Conduct A/B testing and multivariate analysis to optimize website and marketing performance.
  • Stay current on industry best practices and emerging trends in web analytics.
  • Utilize tools such as Google Analytics, Looker and other relevant platforms effectively.
  • Identify opportunities for improvement in website usability and conversion rate optimization.
  • Work closely with the UX/UI, Media and Web Development teams to ensure accurate tracking and data integration.
  • Present findings and recommendations to senior management
  • Implement tagging strategies to track full funnel marketing campaigns accurately.
  • Troubleshoot tracking issues and ensure data accuracy.
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to drive data-informed decisions.
  • Implement data visualization techniques to convey insights effectively.

Qualifications

  • 5+ years in DTC analytics or agency consulting, preferably within the telecommunications, mobile or fiber to the home industries
  • Bachelor's degree in marketing, Statistics, Business, or a related field.
  • Proven experience in web analytics, digital marketing, or a related field.
  • Preferred proficiency in Google Analytics, Google Tag Manager, Looker, Databricks, Power BI, Funnel.io and other web analytics tools.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
  • Excellent communication and presentation abilities.
  • Detail-oriented with strong organizational skills.
  • Experience with A/B testing and multivariate analysis.
  • Knowledge of SEO principles and best practices.
  • Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced, dynamic environment.

Core Competencies 

  • Committed: Values each customer, while working hard to keep their business and support our communities.
  • Helpful: Delivers support in the ways that are most useful to our customers and addresses their needs with expertise, respect, and empathy.
  • Proactive: Understand what our customers need, and actively works to make their relationship with use seamless, easy, and rewarding.
  • Personal: Knows our customers well, and tailors our communications and interactions to address their needs and expectations.

Benefits

Cable One and our family of brands appreciates the role our associates play to help the company grow, and in return an excellent benefits package is offered to our associates to recognize the importance of their contributions, such as:

  • Medical, dental, and vision plans – start when you start!
  • Life insurance (self, spouse, children)
  • Paid time off (vacation, holiday, and personal/sick days)
  • 401(k) - 100% company match starts day 1 of employment (up to 5% of eligible compensation)
  • Group Legal plan with Identity Theft Protection

Additional Perks 

  • Tuition reimbursement (up to $5,250 on 1st year)
  • Annual community support to various organizations across the U.S.
  • Associate recognition & awards programs
  • Advancement opportunities
  • Collaborative work environment
  • FREE Cable One services for associates who live in a serviceable area
  • Up to $75/mo. Stipend
  • Remote Access to select premium channels (Cable One, Sparklight, Cable America and ValueNet Fiber Only)
  • Vehicle provided for daily work purposes, if residing within reasonable radius from office location

We’re an Award-Winning Organization!

  • Forbes’ “America’s Best Midsized Employers” 2021-2023

Our Commitment

Diversity lies in the communities we serve and among the associates who dedicate themselves to ensure our continued success. Here at Cable One and our family of brands, we believe it is our individual and unique talents, backgrounds and perspectives that, when combined, truly make us an unstoppable force. "Stronger Together" is not just a verbal cue, it is the motto that our associates live by, exemplify, and embody each and every day.

Cable One and our family of brands is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to, among other things, race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, status as a protected veteran, or disability.

 

 

Pre-hire Processes

Only after successfully passing these pre-hire clearances are individuals approved for hire and ready to start their successful and rewarding career.

title: Web Developer
jobDescription:
Headquartered in Calgary, AB Eagle Copters Ltd. is a privately owned and operated business with over 40 years of experience in the helicopter industry. Eagle Copters has a well-earned reputation for taking a highly personalized approach to providing leading helicopter solutions to customers worldwide. Specializing in helicopter fleet management, including industry leading capabilities in sales, leasing, completions, product development and engineering, and a MRO provider to major OEM platforms.

Location: Calgary, Alberta, Canada

Position: Full Time

Hours: 7:30am-4:00pm

Job Description:

Reporting to the Senior Manager, Marketing, the Web Developer will lead the design, development, and maintenance of Eagle Copters' new consolidated website, which will serve as a digital hub for the main company and its affiliated brands. This position will also oversee the site’s performance, updates, and analytics on an ongoing basis, with some support responsibilities across marketing administration.

This is an in-person, full-time role based in Calgary.

Responsibilities:

  • Design and develop a large-scale, modern website to support Eagle Copters and subsidiary companies.
  • Create and manage scalable site architecture with mobile responsiveness and optimal load performance.
  • Implement and maintain a secure CMS platform (WordPress, Webflow, or equivalent) for internal use.
  • Ensure SEO best practices, web standards, and accessibility are followed.
  • Manage hosting environments, DNS, SSL, backups, and security protocols.
  • Conduct routine updates and maintenance for all digital properties.
  • Set up and monitor web analytics tools (e.g., Google Analytics, Tag Manager, Search Console).
  • Generate and present monthly website performance reports with actionable recommendations.
  • Assist the marketing team in building landing pages and integrating forms and lead capture tools.
  • Support occasional administrative tasks related to digital marketing, such as email automation and CRM updates.
  • Troubleshoot and resolve website issues promptly to ensure consistent uptime.
  • Other duties as assigned.

Required Skill, Knowledge & Qualifications:

  • 3+ years of experience in front-end or full-stack web development.
  • Strong proficiency in HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and web design best practices.
  • Experience with CMS platforms such as WordPress, Webflow, or similar.
  • Working knowledge of SEO, web performance optimization, and security.
  • Familiarity with tools like Google Analytics, Search Console, Tag Manager.
  • Basic understanding of UI/UX principles and responsive design.
  • Excellent communication and problem-solving skills.
  • Self-directed with strong organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple priorities.

Preference will be given to applicants who also possess:

  • Experience integrating CRM systems or marketing automation tools (e.g., HubSpot, Mailchimp).
  • Knowledge of graphic design tools (Figma, Adobe XD, Canva).
  • Familiarity with web hosting and domain management tools.
  • Background in aviation, industrial, or manufacturing sectors.

Why work at Eagle?

We believe that everyone should enjoy their job and their place of work. We are constantly striving to ensure our workplace is a respectful, safe and; most importantly a great place to be. Eagle puts a high value on cooperation, accountability and dependability.

Eagle acknowledges and respects industry experience and bases wages on prior experience and qualifications.

Eagle benefit package for permanent employees comprises of health, dental, vision care, paramedical coverage (massage therapy, chiropractic, acupuncture etc.), health care spending account, travel medical, life insurance, AD&D, short and long term disability and paid sick/personal days. We also offer a group RRSP program with company matched contributions. We hold employee appreciation and social events throughout the year, and on-going learning and development programs.

Eagle is an equal opportunity employer and has policies in place to prevent discrimination in our hiring practices based on current Canadian legislation. We encourage applications from qualified aboriginal persons, persons with disabilities, visible minorities and women.

All applicants must be eligible to work in Canada, are a permanent resident or hold a current and valid authorization (i.e. work permit).

No phone calls will be accepted. We appreciate your interest but only candidates selected for interviews will be contacted.

Job Types: Full-time, Permanent

Benefits:

  • Company events
  • Dental care
  • Disability insurance
  • Extended health care
  • Life insurance
