title: Webflow Developer
jobDescription:
At BMI Group, we focus on Betterment, Mobilization, and Impact. We reimagine and revitalize legacy real estate and industrial infrastructure while developing innovative logistics, housing, hospitality, and business ventures. Guided by our commitment to environmental stewardship and positive impact, we aim to create lasting change and meaningful outcomes.

​

If you’re looking to grow with a company driving transformative projects, BMI is the place for you.

​

Position Overview

We’re seeking a skilled and experienced Webflow Developer to join our in-house marketing team. In this role, you’ll build and enhance responsive websites, optimize them for performance and SEO, and create dynamic communication platforms for our projects.

​

This position requires in-office work in Stratford three days per week.

​

Key Responsibilities

​

Design and develop responsive websites in Webflow, ensuring top-tier user experience, performance, and accessibility.

Collaborate with the marketing and communications team to translate design concepts into interactive web experiences.

Maintain and update Webflow sites with fresh, optimized content.

Implement SEO strategies and ensure site structure supports search engine rankings.

Integrate third-party tools and services (e.g., forms, analytics, CRM systems).

Troubleshoot and resolve technical issues related to website development and maintenance.

Qualifications

​

Proven experience as a Webflow Developer with a strong portfolio.

Expertise in Webflow, including CMS and e-commerce functionalities.

Solid understanding of responsive design, UX/UI principles, and web performance optimization.

Intermediate proficiency in HTML, CSS, and JavaScript (or a willingness to learn advanced skills to expand Webflow capabilities).

Familiarity with SEO best practices and web analytics tools.

Experience working with Figma, Adobe XD, or similar design tools.

Detail-oriented, delivering clean, scalable, and maintainable code.

Strong collaboration and communication skills, with the ability to work effectively with non-technical stakeholders.

Bonus Skills

​

Knowledge of additional front-end frameworks or CMS platforms beyond Webflow.

Experience with integrations like CRM systems, marketing automation tools, or custom APIs.

Familiarity with accessibility standards and implementation.

Why Join BMI?

​

A family-first work culture that values learning and professional growth.

Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits package.

Work on transformative, innovative projects in a supportive environment.

Be part of a small, dynamic team shaping the digital experience for a growing company.

Participate in our legendary Annual General Meetings (AGMs)—they’re the best!

How to Apply

If this role aligns with your skills and goals, we’d love to hear from you! Applications without supporting materials will not be considered. Join us in creating impactful, transformative projects with BMI.

title: Webflow Developer
jobDescription:
Are you a skilled Webflow developer? Do you thrive in dynamic, collaborative environments where innovation is encouraged? Look no further! Signifly is seeking a talented Webflow Expert/Developer to join our team in London. If you're passionate about crafting exceptional digital experiences, driving business growth, and being part of an exciting journey, we want to hear from you!

​

Position Overview

We are seeking a skilled and versatile Webflow Expert/Developer to join our dynamic team in London. The ideal candidate will possess a unique blend of creative flair, technical proficiency, and an entrepreneurial mindset. As a key member of our team, you will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams to deliver high-quality digital solutions for our clients.

​

Key responsibilities

Utilize your expertise in Webflow to develop visually stunning websites that meet client objectives.

Work closely with our team to translate design concepts from Figma into functional, responsive web experiences.

Provide technical guidance and support to clients, assisting with the setup of Shopify and other digital platforms.

Act as a trusted advisor, contributing to the development and execution of digital strategies that drive business growth.

Embrace an entrepreneurial mindset and actively contribute to Signifly's journey, fostering a culture of innovation and excellence.

About Signifly

Signifly is a leading digital agency that specializes in crafting exceptional digital experiences for brands worldwide. With a team of +80 talented individuals spanning various disciplines, such as strategists, designers, developers, consultants, content producers, and growth experts, Signifly operates across offices in Copenhagen, Oslo, Montreal, and London. We pride ourselves on our innovative approach, strong culture, and commitment to delivering impactful solutions for our clients

​

Signifly UK

We opened our office in London in May 2023. We pride ourselves on having a diverse and inclusive environment. We have a wider variety of nationalities on the team (+25 globally). Additionally, we work with both North American and European businesses. We work closely with our teams in Copenhagen and Canada. So, whether you work on Canadian, Danish, or global projects, you can expect to be working with team members from Canada, Denmark, Norway, Brazil, Hong Kong, Mexico, Venezuela, France... You get the point :-)!

​

Check out our work at Signifly.com

​

Please expect from us

The most important thing that we offer is an inspiring and ambitious work environment, emphasising learning and developing, both personally and as a team. We have an informal organisation with minimal hierarchy, where there is plenty of room for being ambitious within your area of expertise and taking part in shaping our team, culture, and product offering. Make sure to check out our Instagram for the latest and greatest

​

But, of course, we can also tempt you with:

​

Yearly one-week team trip with both social and professional focuses (paid) - last year was in Portugal.

Great office environment

Coffee, tea, soft drinks, snacks, etc. day and night

Voluntary sports & social activities

Competitive salary based on qualifications and performance

Career progress based on merits rather than seniority

And if you think something is missing, just let us know. We can probably work together to add it. After all, we are trying to create the best possible place to work!

​

Requirements

To be considered for this position, we require you to have:

​

Minimum of 3 years' experience in Webflow development, with a strong portfolio showcasing your design and development skills.

Proficiency in Figma and other design tools, with the ability to effectively collaborate with designers and other team members.

Technical proficiency and the ability to troubleshoot and resolve issues independently.

Experience with Shopify is advantageous but not required.

Strong understanding of business fundamentals and a passion for driving results.

Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work effectively in a collaborative team environment.

Enthusiasm for Signifly's culture and a desire to contribute to our ongoing success.

Join Our Team: If you're a creative problem-solver with a passion for digital innovation and a desire to make a meaningful impact, we want to hear from you! Join us on our exciting journey and be part of a team that values creativity, collaboration, and continuous growth.

​

Ready to do the best work of your career?

Join our digital journey!

title: Website Operations Specialist (Hybrid)
jobDescription:
We’re looking for a Website Operations Specialist to join our Marketing team. Our website is a first impression for our customers and a growth engine for our business. This role will support the website operations and execution of the website at Homebase.

As a Website Operations Specialist, you’ll oversee the execution, optimization, and technical enablement of Homebase’s website. This role supports cross-functional teams in executing page builds, website updates, and campaign launches efficiently. This role will manage web development workflows, and ensure our website is performing optimally. The ideal candidate has a blend of project management expertise, technical proficiency in Webflow, and an understanding of website performance, SEO, and content strategy.

You are a bar raiser, which means you come with

  • 2-3+ years of experience in website operations, web project management, or a similar role.
  • Proficiency in Webflow or similar CMS platforms.
  • Experience managing website update workflows and collaborating with developers and content teams.
  • Understanding of SEO, website performance optimization, and analytics tools.
  • Excellent project management skills with experience in agile methodologies.
  • Strong communication skills and the ability to translate technical requirements for non-technical stakeholders.
  • Experience with A/B testing, conversion rate optimization (CRO), and web analytics (Google Analytics, GTM, etc.), and a curiosity to learn more
  • Knowledge of basic HTML, CSS, and JavaScript for troubleshooting purposes.
  • Previous experience in a fast-paced SaaS or startup environment.

Key Responsibilities

Website Operations & Execution

  • Manage website updates, ensuring timely execution of content changes, redirects, and technical enhancements using Webflow.
  • Intake and prioritize website-related requests from various stakeholders (Marketing, Product, Content, and Brand Design teams).
  • Plan and manage website sprints, brief developers, track progress and ensure timely delivery of updates.
  • Ensure the website maintains high performance, uptime, and security.

Process Strategy & Optimization

  • Define and refine the web operations process from request intake to implementation.
  • Identify inefficiencies in the hand-offs between PMM, Content, Brand Design, and Development teams and propose solutions for improved collaboration.
  • Develop best practices for web updates and ensure alignment with broader marketing and brand strategies.

Technical Support & Enablement

  • Serve as the Subject Matter Expert (SME) for Webflow, providing guidance and troubleshooting for internal teams.
  • Support SEO initiatives, content updates, and accessibility improvements.
  • Create training materials and documentation to enable non-technical team members to execute basic website updates.

What We Offer

  • Ownership & Savings: Stock options + TFSA/RRSP with 4% company match
  • Health & Wellness: Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision for you and your dependents
  • Time Flexibility: Flex time off + company holidays + designated focus periods
  • Ideal Setup: 3 days WFH (Mon/Thu/Fri), 2 collaborative office days (Tue/Wed)
  • Family Support: Maternity/Parental Leave EI top-up support offered (after 6 months of service)
  • Work Your Way: Work From Anywhere Month + meeting-free weeks yearly
  • Protection Plans: Life insurance + short/long-term disability coverage
  • Workspace Perks: Meals provided, team offsites, and Customer Days

What to Expect During the Interview Process:

  • Meet the Talent Acquisition team, Alex V.
  • Meet the Hiring Manager, Philip S.
  • Participate in a Stakeholder Interview, Kerry M.
  • Meet the Leadership team, Nick M.
  • Professional Reference Checks
  • Background Check + Offer Stage
  • Welcome to the team, Homie

Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion at Homebase:

At Homebase, we take pride in fostering a welcoming space where every Homie of every gender, age, orientation, culture and walk of life can be their full selves. Diverse perspectives empower us to build the best-in-class platform for small businesses and hourly shift workers. We recognize that experience comes in many forms, so if you think you’re close to what we’re looking for (even if you don’t meet 100% of the qualifications), we encourage you to apply!

**Interview Recording Notice

By participating in interviews with Homebase, you consent to the use of Metaview, a recording and transcription tool, during the interview process. Please be aware that all interviews may be recorded and transcribed for the purpose of evaluating candidates and ensuring the quality of our recruitment process. If you do not consent to being recorded, please inform the Talent Team at the beginning of the call, and appropriate arrangements will be made to accommodate your preference. Your privacy is important to us, and the recorded interviews will only be used for internal evaluation and assessment of candidates.

About Us

Our mission is to make small business teams unstoppable.

Homebase is the everything app for hourly teams, with employee scheduling, time clocks, payroll, team communication, HR, and more. More than 100,000 small (but mighty) businesses rely on Homebase to make work radically easy and give their teams superpowers. As the leader in small business team management, Homebase tracked 1+ billion hours for 2.5+ million workers last year.

We're based in San Francisco, Houston, Denver, and Toronto. We're backed by leading venture investors L Catterton Growth, Emerson Collective, Notable Capital, Bain Capital Ventures, Khosla Ventures, Baseline Ventures, Cowboy Ventures, Bedrock Capital, and PLUS Capital.

You'll be part of an unstoppable team that puts customers first, embraces each day with excitement, and strives for excellence in everything you do. We’re revolutionizing the way small businesses manage their teams and grow their business. What this means for you is a shared passion for innovation and making a difference for the people we serve.

So what do you say, will you join us on our mission to empower small businesses?

title: Brand & Web Designer
jobDescription:
Juice is an innovative creative agency that is passionate about creating innovative and memorable experiences online. We're on a mission to revolutionize user experiences through intuitive and responsive web interfaces. Join our talented team of developers, designers, and producers to help make wonderful things.

Position Overview

We’re looking for a Web & Brand Designer to join our team on a full-time or contract basis. This role is all about creating beautiful, functional, and on-brand digital experiences — not just designing websites, but crafting the visual language that brings a brand to life online.

You’ll work primarily in Figma, creating thoughtful layouts, building intuitive page flows, and collaborating across disciplines to turn strategy into story. You should have a sharp eye for detail, a strong sense of typography and composition, and the ability to elevate a brand across web and digital assets.

If you care deeply about good design, understand how layout and interaction influence perception, and love shaping modern brand experiences — let’s talk.

What You’ll Do

  • Design full web experiences in Figma — from homepage to supporting pages, components, and mobile views
  • Develop branded design systems that are flexible, expressive, and consistent across digital
  • Turn strategy, goals, and content into emotionally resonant layouts that connect with the target audience
  • Pair smart UX thinking with strong visual execution
  • Explore layout options, build design narratives, and present creative rationale clearly
  • Collaborate with strategy, content, and dev teams to make sure designs scale and stay true to intent
  • Design light brand collateral (e.g. social, presentation decks, graphics) when needed to support campaigns or launches

Requirements

  • 3+ years experience in web and/or brand design — ideally in an agency, studio, or fast-paced creative environment
  • Strong portfolio showcasing modern, well-crafted web design and brand applications
  • Expert-level Figma skills, with a command of components, auto layout, and prototyping
  • Proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator at minimum)
  • Solid understanding of layout, hierarchy, and typography across responsive design
  • Comfort working within (and helping evolve) visual languages and design systems
  • Ability to work independently and clearly communicate creative thinking

Bonus Points

  • Experience designing for Webflow or collaborating closely with developers
  • Experience creating branded pitch decks or presentation design
  • Familiarity with motion/interaction principles or light prototyping
  • Comfort contributing to or evolving a brand identity system

Remote restrictions

  • Workday must overlap by at least 3 hours with Orlando, FL, USA
  • Must be a resident of Brazil, Canada, Mexico, or United States

​

title: Front End Developer- Marketing team
jobDescription:
Full job description

About Durable

Our mission at Durable is to make owning a business easier than having a job.

We believe in a world where everyone is a business owner. Whether it’s a full-time, part-time, or side hustle - we want to make it frictionless to start and operate a business.

Since launching in late 2022, over 5 million websites have been built with our AI powered website builder (http://durable.co/ai-website-builder). We are generating significant revenue, well-funded by Silicon Valley investors, and growing extremely fast.

Our vision is much bigger, and we’re looking for incredible people to join our team and make an impact.

​

What you’ll do:

As a Front End Developer on the Marketing team, you will build and maintain websites and customized landing pages. The ideal candidate will have a solid understanding of frontend technologies, an eye for design, and the capacity to communicate technical concepts clearly to technical and non-technical audiences.

​

Responsibilities:

Building public facing tools (ie. calculator tools, emails, landing pages, dynamic ads).

Translating wireframes into visual elements.

Using Webflow to design, build and launch landing pages.

Optimizing Durable's website for performance- adding/removing content, integrating APIs etc.

Building reusable code and libraries for future use.

Collaborating with the design and marketing team to build entire web experiences.

De-bugging and QA'ing your own work.

Skills required:

Strong portfolio demonstrating expertise in front-end technologies, including HTML, CSS, JavaScript, jQuery, and frameworks incl. React, Vue or Angular.

5+ years of experience working at a start-up or in a fast-paced environment (agency or in-house studio).

Advanced knowledge of Webflow.

Experience building mobile-friendly, cross-platform, high performing websites.

Familiarity with advertising technology concepts and trends.

Familiarity with Figma, XD or any design tools.

​

Salary

The annual salary range for this role is $100,000 - $125,000 + benefits.

title: Graphic & Web Designer
jobDescription:
About Novus: Who We Are

Novus is a progressive automotive group committed to innovation, exceptional customer experiences, and making a positive impact in our community. Representing five dealerships and seven automotive brands in the Durham region (Ontario Motor Sales, Clarington Hyundai, Clarington Kia, Durham Harley-Davidson, and Marigold Ford Lincoln), Novus Automotive is reshaping the way customers experience automotive ownership and care.

About The Role: Here’s What The Role Entails

We’re looking for a talented and innovative Web and Graphic Designer to join our team. This role is perfect for someone with a strong foundation in both graphic design and web development, who can seamlessly blend creativity with functionality. The ideal candidate will be skilled in designing engaging visuals for both print and digital platforms, while also having the technical expertise to optimize websites for an exceptional user experience. Your work will directly shape the visual identity of dealership websites and the Novus brand, making a significant impact on how we connect with our audiences.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Web Design & Development:
  • Design, build, and optimize websites using WordPress and WebFlow.
  • Ability to customize WordPress themes, templates, and CSS style sheets when required.
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure websites are aligned with brand guidelines and designed intelligently for the audience.
  • Reimagine and optimize dealership websites, ensuring seamless user experience and functionality.
  • Graphic Design:
  • Design high-impact custom graphics, illustrations, and infographics for print materials (brochures, flyers, posters), digital platforms, social media, and events.
  • Develop creative assets that drive marketing campaigns and support brand initiatives.
  • Ensure all designs are consistent with brand guidelines and optimized for a variety of formats and platforms.
  • User Experience (UX):
  • Apply a user-centric design approach to improve site navigation, usability, and engagement. Including the direction of simplifying web pages experiences leveraging multi-media and action-oriented copy.
  • Utilize SEO best practices to design websites that are optimized for search.
  • Enhance user experience through strategic layout design, intuitive navigation, and visual hierarchy.
  • Collaboration & Innovation:
  • Work closely with the Brand and Integrated Marketing Manager and Manager of Dealership Marketing to bring new ideas to life.
  • Collaborate with team members to gather feedback, iterate, and improve web and graphic designs.
  • Provide fresh and innovative design solutions that align with current design trends and technology.
  • Integration & Technical Expertise:
  • Knowledge of AI integrations and API connectivity is an asset.
  • Build and maintain integrations from CRM platforms (e.g., Klaviyo, DealerSocket) to the website.
  • Ensure website performance and compatibility across devices and browsers.

Qualifications:

  • Minimum of 5 years of professional experience in web design and graphic design.
  • College / design diploma
  • Strong proficiency in WordPress, WebFlow, CSS, and HTML.
  • Expertise in graphic design softwares (e.g., Adobe Creative Suite: Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign).
  • Understanding of SEO principles and how they apply to website design.
  • Experience in user experience (UX) principles, including wireframing and prototyping is an asset.
  • Ability to create designs for print, digital, and social media platforms.
  • Knowledge of AI integrations, API connectivity, and building integrations with CRM platforms (e.g., Klaviyo) is a plus.
  • Strong portfolio showcasing a range of design work, including websites, custom graphics, and print materials.
  • Excellent communication and collaboration skills.
  • Strong attention to detail and a creative problem-solving mindset.
  • A self-starter with the ability to manage multiple projects and meet deadlines.

Why Join Novus?

At Novus Automotive, you'll be part of a dynamic and creative team where your work will directly influence our brand’s success. We value innovation and collaboration, giving you the opportunity to make a tangible impact in the automotive industry. If you're passionate about creative marketing and want to bring your ideas to life, Novus is the place to be.

Benefits Include:

  • Paid Time Off: 5 personal days, 3 weeks of vacation time (increasing to 4 weeks after 5 years, and 5 weeks after 10 years), 2 paid volunteer days, 3 paid bereavement days, and 5 paid new parent leave days.
  • Comprehensive Benefit Plan: Enrollment in our benefit plan starting on day one.

title: SEO Specialist
companyName: Coast2Coast First Aid and Aquatics Inc.
jobDescription:
Coast2coast First Aid & Aquatics Inc. is an award winning and a leader in Safety, First Aid and CPR training, servicing nearly 50,000 people and over 2000+ companies every year,, in 23 cities across the country. We are looking for a highly experienced SEO Specialist who can drive organic growth, increase search rankings, and enhance our digital presence to generate more student registrations.

If you have a minimum of 5 years of experience in SEO, a deep understanding of ranking algorithms, and proven success in optimizing websites for lead generation and conversions, we want to hear from you!

We already have great rankings across many cities and many keywords, which is thanks to our successful in-house SEO Strategy. Now, we need someone to help take us to the absolute number 1 across the board —a strategic thinker who can add new elements to our marketing strategy beyond what we’re already excelling at.

If you have a proven track record of delivering successful campaigns for past employers or clients, and the ability to bring something new, creative, and impactful to the table, we want to hear from you!

Role Overview

As an SEO Specialist, you will be responsible for developing and executing a comprehensive SEO strategy to improve organic traffic, boost keyword rankings, and enhance our website's performance. You will work closely with our marketing team, content creators, and web developers to ensure SEO best practices are implemented across all digital channels.

Key Responsibilities

✔ Develop and implement SEO strategies to improve organic search visibility and increase leads.

✔ Conduct keyword research to identify high-impact search terms and optimize website content accordingly.

✔ Optimize on-page SEO, including metadata, internal linking, and content structuring.

✔ Oversee technical SEO, ensuring site speed, mobile-friendliness, and structured data compliance.

✔ Manage backlink strategies, including link-building outreach, competitor analysis, and partnerships.

✔ Track, analyze, and report SEO performance using tools like Google Analytics, Google Search Console, Ahrefs, or SEMrush.

✔ Collaborate with content creators to develop SEO-optimized blog posts, landing pages, and course descriptions.

✔ Stay up-to-date with Google algorithm updates and adjust strategies accordingly.

✔ Conduct competitor analysis to identify gaps and opportunities for improvement.

✔ Work on local SEO strategies to increase visibility in specific geographic areas (Google My Business, local citations).

What We’re Looking For

  • 5+ years of SEO experience with a proven track record of increasing rankings and organic traffic.
  • Deep knowledge of Google’s search algorithms and best SEO practices.
  • Expertise in technical SEO, including site audits, schema markup, and Core Web Vitals.
  • Hands-on experience with SEO tools (Ahrefs, SEMrush, Moz, Screaming Frog, Google Search Console).
  • Strong understanding of on-page, off-page, and local SEO.
  • Ability to conduct SEO audits and provide actionable recommendations.
  • Experience in link-building and outreach strategies.
  • Proficiency in Google Analytics and data analysis to track SEO performance.
  • Strong problem-solving and analytical skills with the ability to interpret large data sets.
  • Experience in service-based industries, education, or e-commerce SEO is a plus.

Why Join Coast2coast?

✅ Competitive salary + performance-based bonuses.

✅ Opportunity to lead and shape SEO strategy for a fast-growing company.

✅ Work with an experienced in-house marketing team that values innovation and results.

✅ Hybrid work model with flexibility.

✅ Career growth opportunities as we expand into the U.S. market.

Job Type: Full-time

Pay: $51,000.00-$70,000.00 per year

Benefits:

  • Casual dress
  • Dental care
  • Extended health care
  • On-site parking
  • Paid time off
  • Vision care

Schedule:

  • 8 hour shift

Ability to commute/relocate:

  • Thornhill, ON: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required)

Experience:

  • SEO: 5 years (required)

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Thornhill, ON

title: Senior Content Writer/Copywriter
jobDescription:
As a SeniorContent Writer/ Copywriter at Designity, you’ll be the driving force behind content that captivates, converts, and builds brand authority. From high-impact marketing campaigns to SEO-rich blogs and UX copy, you’ll craft compelling narratives that engage the right audience at the right time. Partnering with creative teams and using AI-powered tools, you’ll ensure every piece of content is not just well-written but strategically aligned with brand goals and designed to drive results.

‍

Designity is a Creative-as-a-Service (CaaS) platform that empowers businesses with a flexible, high-quality, and scalable alternative to traditional agencies and in-house teams.

‍

Our unique model connects marketing and creative leaders with the top 3% of US-based creative and marketing talent, all led by experienced Creative Directors, Project Managers, and Marketing Strategists. Through our collaborative platform, we streamline the creative process, ensuring fast, high-impact execution without the inefficiencies of hiring in-house or the high costs of traditional agencies.

‍

Brands like Calendly, Kung Fu Tea, and Marriott Hotels trust Designity to handle their design and marketing needs—proving that there's a better way to scale creativity. Unlike in-house hiring, which is expensive and skill-limiting, or marketplaces like Upwork and Fiverr, which lack reliability and creative oversight, Designity delivers a seamless, results-driven approach that makes creative collaboration effortless.

​

PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES

  • Develop compelling narratives for marketing campaigns, blog articles, and UX content that engage and convert.
  • Implement keyword strategies and SEO best practices to boost organic traffic and content performance.
  • Establish and refine content guidelines to maintain a clear, distinct brand identity.
  • Use AI-powered tools to boost efficiency, refine messaging, and optimize content for search and engagement.
  • Work with the Creative Director and creative teams to ensure brand consistency across all content.
  • Stay updated on industry trends, SEO changes, and audience engagement strategies.

REQUIREMENTS

  • 6+ years of experience in content writing, copywriting, or both, backed by a strong portfolio showcasing published work.
  • Experience crafting engaging narratives that resonate with various audiences and ability to adapt writing style and tone to suit the needs of different projects.
  • Proven experience optimizing content for SEO and increasing organic traffic.
  • Excellent grammar, editing, and proofreading skills.
  • Experience using AI-powered tools to improve results and efficiency.
  • Comfortable working independently and collaborating with teams to develop content.
  • Strong time management skills to meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.
  • Strong verbal and written communication skills in English.

BENEFITS:

  • Join a company at the perfect moment! Career making opportunity.
  • Work fully remotely from wherever you are.
  • Unwind during the flexible PTO, and company holidays.
  • Learn and grow with the best by becoming part of our exclusive Design Community.
  • Enjoy dedicated focus times designed to boost your productivity and creativity.

‍

In order to be considered for this position, you need to complete the application process at www.designity.com/careers/senior-content-writer-copywriter

‍

Please do NOT follow up on your application. We will be in touch within 2-3 weeks if you're qualified.

‍

At Designity, employment decisions are based on business needs, qualifications, and merit. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer, valuing diversity in culture, perspectives, skills, and experiences, which strengthens our team, clients, and products.

title: Senior SEO Analyst
jobDescription:
iPullRank is a ten-year-old digital marketing remote agency based in New York City, founded by industry trailblazer Michael King. We’re not here to follow trends—we set them. Our team blends technical expertise with creativity to deliver SEO, Content, and Generative AI services that drive results. We work with some of the biggest names across eCommerce, Financial Services, and Media brands, as well as ambitious mid-sized businesses, crafting strategies that don’t just compete but dominate.

Our Values: PROUD

At iPullRank, we live by our values:

  • Proactive: We don’t wait for problems—we solve them.
  • Reliable: When we commit, we deliver.
  • Outstanding: We aim for extraordinary, every time.
  • Useful: Everything we do has purpose and impact.
  • Dedicated: Your goals are our mission.

These principles shape every decision, every campaign, and every success story.

What We Do

We specialize in SEO, Content, and Generative AI strategy and implementation, helping brands win today while building future-proof strategies for tomorrow. Whether it’s cracking complex algorithms, crafting authentic, helpful content, or engineering AI solutions, we’re the partner brands trust to stay ahead.

Why Join Us?

At iPullRank, we’re more than an agency—we’re a team of thinkers, builders, and creators who believe the best ideas come from everywhere. We foster a culture where you’re encouraged to grow, take ownership, and make a real impact. We’re small enough to be nimble but big enough to work with global heavyweights like Rocket Mortgage, American Express, Nordstrom, Warner Music Group, Target, and Adidas.

We care about our people and back it up with competitive salaries, benefits, and opportunities to evolve into the marketer, content creator, SEO, or leader that you want to be. When you work with us, you’re not just filling a role—you’re shaping your career and redefining what’s possible in marketing.

If you’re ready to take on meaningful work with a team that challenges and inspires, we’d love to hear from you.

Position Description:

iPullRank is looking for a Senior Search Engine Optimization Analyst to join our team and help with site auditing, keyword research, competitive analysis, marketing strategy, user experience, conversion rate optimization, link building and content marketing. This Senior SEO Analyst is a master of all things Google, with an interest in learning more about how Organic Search plays with the rest of the marketing mix. The client-facing role works directly with Client teams to deliver best-in-class SEO that drives results. This person will contribute to SEO efforts and collaborate extensively with the Director of SEO and Mike King to actively contribute to SEO research to develop new strategies and with the SEO community. Salary range: $70,000-$85,000.

What You’ll Do:

  • Proactively identify on-page SEO opportunities
  • Perform comprehensive Site Audits
  • Perform in-depth log file analysis
  • Improve conversion of Organic Search traffic
  • Own performance monitoring reports and KPIs
  • Work with Content Strategy to determine keyword content opportunities for client sites
  • Work with Research Analysts to perform Persona-Driven Keyword Research
  • Perform competitive analysis to identify opportunities
  • Present deliverables and results to clients
  • Contribute to iPullRank inbound marketing efforts and other forms of thought leadership
  • Review algorithm updates to develop thought leadership content

Requirements

  • Minimum 3 years of search engine optimization experience
  • Possess thorough, up-to-date knowledge of search engine ranking factors and maintains current SEO practices
  • Previous client-facing experience
  • Agency or other multi-project experience
  • Proficiency with HTML, CSS required
  • Working knowledge of JavaScript and relevant frameworks such as React, and Angular preferred
  • Proficiency with Analytics packages and Google Search Console
  • Familiar with industry-standard SEO software
  • Highly proficient in Microsoft Office applications: Excel and PowerPoint
  • Strong analytical mindset and skills, excellent problem-solving skills
  • Exceptionally clear oral and written communication skills
  • High level of initiative and works well in a team environment

Skills of Interest:

    • Backend programming language such as Python or PHP
    • Relational Database skills such as MySQL
    • Experience working in an agency environment
    • Experience with Paid Search

Benefits

  • We Offer Competitive Salaries Commensurate with Experience
  • Paid holidays and sick days
  • 100% Medical, Dental, and Vision insurance
  • 3 weeks of vacation
