title: Senior Webflow Developer - Singapore, Hong Kong, India, Vietnam, Indonesia
jobDescription:
At Aspire, we’re more than just a FinTech company—we’re the leading all-in-one financial operating system built to empower the world’s innovators and entrepreneurs. We are on a mission to reinvent business finance, empowering startups and businesses to realise their full potential.

Founded in 2018, Aspire has raised over USD 300M+ across equity and debt from world-class investors. In 2023, we successfully closed an oversubscribed USD 100 million Series C equity round led by Sequoia Capital and Lightspeed Ventures with participation of Tencent, Paypal Ventures, LGT Capital Partners, Picus Capital and MassMutual Ventures. To power our solutions, we have partnered with some of the best companies in the world such as Visa and Wise and empowered more than 50,000 businesses using our suite of products.

Aspire has consistently been recognized for excellence, earning Best Employer and Startup of the Year by the Asia FinTech Awards in 2022 and 2023, ranked as LinkedIn’s Top Startup in Singapore, and listed on CB Insights’ Top 100 Global Fintech in 2023 and 2024.

You will be amazed by the energy and experience of our team! Aspire serves as an environment for you to innovate and drive change with our team of ex-entrepreneurs, ex-founders, and high-achievers with international and diverse backgrounds.

Are you a top talent who is passionate about entrepreneurship? Join our rapidly growing team to make an impact in the fintech space! 

About the role:

We are seeking a talented Webflow Developer to join our team. The successful candidate will be responsible for designing and developing websites using the Webflow platform. This role requires a strong understanding of web design principles, proficiency in Webflow, and an eye for detail.

Responsibilities:

  • Create high-quality, responsive web pages using Webflow, in collaboration with our design team
  • Manage projects from start to finish, ensuring quality and timely delivery
  • Conduct testing and debugging to ensure websites are optimized for speed and functionality
  • Develop custom code for advanced functionality, where necessary
  • Refactor CSS and also create Global Variables in Webflow

Minimum Qualifications:

  • 3+ years of experience in web development, with a proven track record of managing website projects end-to-end.
  • Experience working with CMS platforms such as Webflow, Wordpress or custom-built CMS.
  • Proficiency in front-end and back-end development is preferred, with strong skills in HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and modern frameworks (React, Vue.js, or similar).
  • Good understanding of UX/UI principles and best practices to ensure a seamless website experience.
  • Ability to work in a fast-paced environment, managing multiple projects and deadlines efficiently.
  • Excellent problem-solving skills, attention to detail, and ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Familiarity with web accessibility standards (WCAG) and responsive design principles.
  • Strong understanding of marketing technologies and integrations, including CRM and email automation tools.
  • Previous experience working in a SaaS or tech-driven environment.

What we offer

  • Uncapped flexible annual leave.
  • Hybrid work arrangement. 
  • Training subsidy for your professional growth.
  • Wellness benefit.
  • Team bonding budget to foster collaboration and sense of belonging.
  • Flexibility to work from anywhere (for up to 90 days per annum).
  • Culture is Key: We always strive to cultivate a special culture that brings special talents together - You can learn more about our culture on our careers site and LinkedIn Life page.

Equal Opportunity Statement 

Aspire is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to providing equal employment opportunities to all qualified individuals without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, or any other protected characteristic as outlined by applicable laws. 

Please note: by submitting your application, you acknowledge that you have read and understood Aspire’s [Data Protection Policy for Employees, Freelancers, Contractors and Job Applicants](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1vLznVZhG59UpJ2IO0vUyy0dskPf7ZAF/view?usp=sharing)[ (the “Policy”)](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1vLznVZhG59UpJ2IO0vUyy0dskPf7ZAF/view?usp=sharing), and consent to the collection, use and disclosure of your personal data by Aspire for the purposes set out in the Policy. You may withdraw consent for such collection, use and disclosure, and make an access or correction request in respect of your personal data, in accordance with the Policy by emailing people@aspireapp.com.

title: Web Developer
jobDescription:
Full job description

We are a leading technology and manufacturing company with a strong presence in the industry, dedicated to providing innovative solutions and high-quality products. Our company thrives on collaboration, continuous improvement, and a commitment to excellence. We operate in a dynamic environment, catering to various sectors, including defense, aerospace, and industrial markets. As a team, we value integrity, creativity, and customer satisfaction, and we are looking for talented individuals to join us in driving our mission forward.

​

We have an excellent opportunity for a Web Developer to join our team in Fremont, CA. Our ideal candidate will be responsible for maintaining the Company’s global and multi-language Website, E-Commerce platform and Product Information System (PIM) and their servers. The duties include working closely with the global Marketing team to design or update the website, optimization, fixing website errors/bugs in a timely manner and creating a response plan for down servers and running tests to make sure designs or updates function properly. In addition, the Webmaster multi-tasks and coordinates a wide range of improvement projects and has experience with software programs such as Webflow, Catsy, Shopify, Illustrator, InDesign or comparable, Project Management tools, and with HTML programming.

​

Responsibilities:

​

The Web Developer's primary responsibility includes enhancing user experience by creating user-friendly, visually appealing, and secure sites. You will work closely with business stakeholders, the IT teams, and external developers, and to ensure that the architecture aligns with the organization's needs and goals.

​

Website Maintenance: Regularly updating and maintaining the website to ensure it functions correctly, remains secure, and delivers a positive user experience.

Project Management: Lead / drive the (web-related) improvement projects (incl. planning, execution, monitoring and controlling, reporting)

Content Management: Adding, editing, and removing website content, including text, images, videos, and other multimedia elements.

Web Design: Implementing and managing the website's design, layout, and user interface to ensure it is visually appealing and user-friendly.

Search Engine Optimization (SEO): Optimizing web pages and content to improve search engine rankings and increase organic traffic to the website together with Digital Marketing experts.

Website Performance: Regularly monitor and optimize website performance, including page load times and server responsiveness.

Security Management: Implement security measures to protect the website from cyber threats, such as malware, hacking attempts, and data breaches.

User Support: Addressing user inquiries, feedback, and technical issues related to the website.

Website Testing: Conduct regular testing, including cross-browser and cross-device compatibility testing, to ensure the website works well on different platforms.

Domain and Hosting Management: Overseeing domain registration, renewal, and managing website hosting services.

Teamwork: Crosstrain and support marketing team members

Other duties as assigned by supervisor

Qualities:

​

Website development/administration and good understanding of digital marketing principles

Knowledge of best practices for web-based initiatives

Understanding of localization and translation processes and its implications for content development

Support multi-languages content and data, in particular English and German

Good eye for high-converting landing pages and experience working teams to develop them

CMS Expertise and Training: Proficiency in CMS administration (Webflow & Shopify preferred) and experience providing team training and support on CMS usage.

HTML and CSS

Project management tools (Wrike preferred)

Desired Skills: Caty, Google analytics 4, Google tag manager, Semrush, Lookerstudio, Wistia, Hotjar, Cookiebot, OptinMonster (Popup)

Collaboration and Technical Skills: Ability to work closely with global cross-functional teams, leveraging technical skills to drive outcomes.

Data-Driven and Analytical: Strong analytical abilities, with a focus on UX and conversion rate optimization (CRO), to ensure data-backed improvements.

Experience in project management, reporting and analysis

Self-motivation, effective communication skills and keeping deadlines

Desired: Fluent in English and German

Desired: Candidates living in the Bay Area of California that are willing to work in our Fremont, CA office at least 4 days a week

Preferred Education/Certifications

​

4+ years in corporate website content development, management, and maintenance.

2+ years participation in basic digital marketing (Example: SEO)

Basic wireframing and graphics capability

Minimum of two-year associate's degree

Please include a cover letter on how you specifically meet the responsibilities and skills described above.

​

Job Type: Full-time

​

Pay: $80,000.00 - $120,000.00 per year

​

Benefits:

​

401(k) matching

Dental insurance

Flexible spending account

Health insurance

Health savings account

Life insurance

Paid time off

Vision insurance

Compensation Package:

​

Performance bonus

Yearly pay

Schedule:

​

Monday to Friday

Application Question(s):

​

We are a federal contractor, and we are required to be ITAR Compliant. Are you a US Citizen or a Green card holder?

Ability to Commute:

​

Fremont, CA 94539 (Preferred)

Ability to Relocate:

​

Fremont, CA 94539: Relocate before starting work (Required)

Work Location: In person

title: Webflow Developer & UX Designer (Hybrid / Boston, MA)
jobDescription:
Skedda is the most customizable and user-friendly workplace management platform on the market. We help companies move beyond spreadsheets and clunky software to a smarter way to manage hybrid offices, studios, academic spaces, and more. Trusted by Harvard, Deloitte, and MIT, we combine power and simplicity to deliver “your space, your rules, made simple.”

Role Overview

We’re seeking a talented Webflow Developer & UX Designer to help us build high-converting web experiences that embody our brand and drive demand. This role blends UX design, front-end logic, and CMS architecture to support key marketing campaigns and site growth initiatives. 

You’ll work closely with our Growth and Engineering teams to launch stunning pages, improve site performance, and evolve our digital experience with a strong eye for conversion and SEO. 

What You’ll Do

  • Own the design and development of new landing pages, industry pages, and feature pages in Webflow
  • Collaborate with marketing and brand to align designs with Skedda’s core messaging pillars: flexibility, ease of use, and insights
  • Implement clean, semantic HTML/CSS in Webflow, optimize CMS collections, and maintain a scalable design system
  • Translate Figma mockups into responsive, optimized Webflow pages
  • Ensure top performance on Lighthouse and Core Web Vitals (load speed, mobile responsiveness, accessibility)
  • Add light interactive elements using native Webflow interactions and custom JavaScript when needed
  • Set up A/B test variants for page optimization in partnership with growth marketing
  • Maintain technical SEO hygiene (metadata, schema markup, crawlability)
  • Integrate HubSpot forms, tracking, and marketing automation snippets cleanly and without performance compromise
  • Help audit and clean up unused Webflow classes and outdated CMS structures

What We’re Looking For

  • 5+ years of Webflow experience with a portfolio of live projects (please include examples)
  • Strong eye for layout, typography, hierarchy, and UX best practices
  • Ability to build scalable CMS structures and use Webflow’s dynamic capabilities efficiently
  • Comfortable with Webflow’s interactions, animations, and light JavaScript tweaks
  • Solid understanding of responsive design and modern front-end principles
  • Familiarity with SEO, conversion rate optimization, and performance tools like Lighthouse
  • Experience integrating tools like HubSpot, Heap, Hotjar, or custom analytics

🛠 Tools We Use

  • Asana
  • Webflow
  • Figma
  • HubSpot
  • Hotjar
  • SEMrush
  • Google Analytics / Tag Manager
  • RevenueHero
  • Heap
  • Zapier

💡 Why This Role Matters

This role is critical to executing our strategic growth plan and directly impacts demo conversion, SEO performance, and brand consistency across digital surfaces. Your work will make Skedda’s value instantly clear to new prospects, improving their journey from awareness → consideration → demo.

About Us

We’re an international team from 20+ countries with a growing presence in Boston. We place a lot of value on collaborating asynchronously across time zones but also understand the benefits of working together in person. Our team has doubled in size in the last year and continues to grow quickly. 

At Skedda, our Mission is to empower organizations to bring workspaces to life. We take feedback from customers seriously and constantly iterate to improve our product. We’re a self-driven, curious, down-to-earth group of people who know how to balance moving quickly while maintaining a high bar for quality. Every team member at Skedda embodies our six core virtues: 

  • We are a community
  • Quality is at our core
  • Take ownership
  • Create momentum every day
  • Tackle hard problems
  • Be curious

We are defining the future of the workplace experience, helping businesses to design meaningful, seamless, fully integrated employee experiences and interactions in the hybrid workplace. We’re growing fast with a lot of new opportunities ahead!

To learn more about Skedda, please visit https://www.skedda.com/blog/skedda-mission-vision-virtues.

title: Webflow/Figma Expert
jobDescription:
We are seeking a talented and detail-oriented Web Designer to join our team. This individual will have a proven track record of creating high-quality, functional websites and digital experiences. The ideal candidate is adaptable, self-motivated, and collaborative, with a strong commitment to delivering exceptional results for clients. While this position is remote, we are seeking Canadian citizens to fill the role.

​

The Role

Primary Responsibilities

Design wireframes, prototypes, and high-fidelity mockups in Figma, with a strong focus on user experience and brand alignment.

Develop responsive, functional websites using Webflow, adhering to best practices for performance and accessibility.

Collaborate directly with clients via email and video calls to gather feedback, clarify project requirements, and present progress updates.

Create visually compelling social media content tailored to various platforms.

Integrate third-party tools (e.g., CRMs, marketing automation, and analytics platforms) to enhance website functionality and client workflows.

Technical Requirements

Proficiency in Figma, with a portfolio showcasing past work.

Advanced knowledge of Webflow, including CMS integration, animations, and customizations.

Experience with SEO optimization and implementing Google tools (e.g., Analytics, Tag Manager).

Familiarity with web technologies, including HTML and CSS. Knowledge of JavaScript is an asset.

Hands-on experience with automation tools such as Zapier or Make.

Preferred Experience

Strong understanding of UI/UX principles, with the ability to design intuitive, results-driven interfaces.

Experience working with other website builders like Shopify or Wix.

Knowledge of accessibility standards (e.g., WCAG compliance).

Ability to conduct QA testing to ensure design consistency and functionality across devices and browsers.

Skills in logo design and brand asset creation are a plus.

​

Attributes We Value

Attention to Detail: Precision in every aspect of your work, from layouts to interactions.

Client-Focused Mindset: Ability to understand and anticipate client needs, ensuring a seamless collaborative process.

Problem-Solving Skills: Adaptability and resourcefulness in addressing design or technical challenges.

Self-Starter: Highly independent and organized, capable of managing multiple projects simultaneously.

Compensation and Benefits

Hourly Rate: $25–$35 per hour, based on experience.

Work Schedule: Flexible hours, fully remote position.

Fridays off: A 4-day work week to prioritize work-life balance.

Benefits: Health and dental benefits.

​

This is an opportunity to have a meaningful impact on a wide variety of projects while working with a dedicated and creative team. If you meet the qualifications and are excited to grow with us, we encourage you to apply.

​

title: Content Marketing Manager
jobDescription:
Full job description

Job Title: Content Marketing Manager

​

Location: Remote

​

Role Type: Full Time

​

About the Role:

​

Healthpilot’s mission is a big one – to fundamentally shift the perception that finding the right Medicare plan has to be overwhelming. With the combination of an easy-to-use comparison platform, sophisticated matching technology, and undeniably human Medicare experts (who are not paid to push plans), we are uniquely poised to disrupt the profit-driven Medicare industry and finally put beneficiaries first.

​

The Medicare market is a conditioned one: folks don’t know Medicare shopping can be easier, and they don’t trust yet that what we have to offer isn’t too good to be true. That’s where you come in: we’re looking for a curious, collaborative content marketing manager motivated to help us build brand trust in the wild and strengthen our relationship with our customers, current and future.

​

A member of Healthpilot’s Growth team, you will be responsible for activating an omni-channel marketing strategy that responds to the needs of our customers throughout their entire Medicare shopping journey. Your goal is to build trust and confidence among new customers, and maximize loyalty and retention among current customers. You will streamline the sales cycle by providing high-quality content, maintaining a centralized library of messaging resources, and ensuring that we have consistent and accurate messaging throughout the Healthpilot ecosystem.

​

In this role, you will:

​

· Develop and execute a content marketing strategy aligned with business goals and Medicare enrollment cycles.

​

· Champion our organic content strategy, building out a robust Medicare resource library and drafting lifecycle communications to both current and future customers, optimizing for engagement over time.

​

· Create and optimize messaging for our omni-channel marketing campaigns across paid media, pay-per-click, SMS, programmatic, email, and more. Including but not limited to A/B testing for headlines, CTAs, email content and landing pages.

​

· Build out our messaging playbook, documenting winning messages and value props across channels at different phases of the customer journey.

​

· Work closely with our product marketing manager to understand our buyer personas and customer journey maps, testing and optimizing content to address customer needs throughout the marketing lifecycle.

​

· Track and analyze content performance metrics (organic traffic, engagement, conversions), continuously optimizing content based on data insights and user feedback.

​

· Ensure all content is clear, compliant, and easy to understand for seniors.

​

What we’re looking for:

​

✔ 3–5+ years of experience in content marketing, preferably with B2C experience creating customer-driven content. Experience in healthcare a plus but not required.

​

✔ Exceptional writing and editing skills, with a knack for simplifying complex topics.

​

✔ Strong understanding of SEO, keyword research, and content analytics tools (Bing Webmaster Tools, Google Analytics).

​

✔ Experience managing campaigns, blog strategies, and multi-channel content distribution.

​

✔ Ability to work cross-functionally with our marketing agency, product, and design teams.

​

✔ Familiarity with Medicare, health insurance regulations, and compliance (a plus but not required).

​

What We Offer:

​

· A collaborative and innovative workplace where your ideas aren’t just heard—they make a difference. Here, your contributions shape our future.

​

· Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits package, including health insurance and retirement plans.

​

· Paid maternity and paternity leave

​

· 401k program with company match

​

· As we gear up for growth, you’ll find ample opportunities to stretch, grow, and advance your career. At Healthpilot, we’re on the move, and we want you to move up with us.

​

Healthpilot is an Equal Opportunity Employer dedicated to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace.

​

Job Type: Contract

​

Pay: $80,000.00 - $100,000.00 per year

​

Schedule:

​

Monday to Friday

Weekends as needed

Work Location: Remote

​

title: Digital Marketing Expert
jobDescription:
As the Digital Marketing Expert at Designity, you will work with our clients lead marketing audits and provide them with data-driven strategies to optimize their digital presence. Your expertise in multi-channel marketing, performance analysis, and competitive positioning will drive clear, actionable recommendations. You’ll assess websites, SEO, paid media, content, social media, and email campaigns to identify areas for improvement and develop structured marketing roadmaps.

You will also implement AI-driven marketing tools to enhance automation, optimize campaign performance, and provide deeper insights—ensuring clients stay ahead in an evolving digital landscape.

‍

Designity is a Creative-as-a-Service (CaaS) platform that empowers businesses with a flexible, high-quality, and scalable alternative to traditional agencies and in-house teams.

‍

Our unique model connects marketing and creative leaders with the top 3% of US-based creative and marketing talent, all led by experienced Creative Directors, Project Managers, and Marketing Strategists. Through our collaborative platform, we streamline the creative process, ensuring fast, high-impact execution without the inefficiencies of hiring in-house or the high costs of traditional agencies.

‍

Brands like Calendly, Kung Fu Tea, and Marriott Hotels trust Designity to handle their design and marketing needs—proving that there's a better way to scale creativity. Unlike in-house hiring, which is expensive and skill-limiting, or marketplaces like Upwork and Fiverr, which lack reliability and creative oversight, Designity delivers a seamless, results-driven approach that makes creative collaboration effortless.

‍

PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES:

  • Conduct digital marketing audits, evaluating SEO, paid media, content, email, and social strategies.
  • Analyze multi-channel performance, identifying opportunities for optimization.
  • Develop strategic recommendations and actionable roadmaps to drive marketing effectiveness.
  • Assess SEO & website performance, including technical SEO, UX, and site speed.
  • Optimize paid media campaigns, ensuring cost-effective targeting across Google Ads, Meta, and LinkedIn.
  • Refine content and email marketing strategies, enhancing engagement and conversions.
  • Benchmark competitor strategies to uncover growth opportunities.
  • Implement AI-powered marketing tools for automation, audience targeting, and performance tracking.
  • Consult with clients, translating data into clear, actionable recommendations.
  • Stay ahead of industry trends, continuously refining marketing strategies.

REQUIREMENTS

  • 7+ years of experience in digital marketing strategy or consultancy.
  • Proven track record of optimizing multi-channel marketing campaigns.
  • Deep expertise in SEO, paid media, content marketing, and analytics.
  • Hands-on experience with AI-driven marketing tools (e.g., ChatGPT, Jasper, HubSpot AI, Persado, Phrasee).
  • Proficiency in marketing analytics tools (Google Analytics, SEMrush, HubSpot, etc.).
  • Strong client-facing communication skills to present insights and guide improvements.
  • Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, or a related field (Master’s preferred).

BENEFITS:

  • Join a company at the perfect moment! Career making opportunity.
  • Flexible PTO, and company holidays.
  • Enjoy dedicated focus times designed to boost your productivity and creativity.

‍

In order to be considered for this position, you need to complete the application process at www.designity.com/careers/digital-marketing-expert

‍

Please do NOT follow up on your application. We will be in touch within 2-3 weeks if you're qualified.

‍

At Designity, employment decisions are based on business needs, qualifications, and merit. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer, valuing diversity in culture, perspectives, skills, and experiences, which strengthens our team, clients, and products.

title: PPC/Social Ads Specialist
jobDescription:
Wonderist Agency is a full-service marketing agency that supports almost 1000 dentists around the country. We pride ourselves on ditching the old fashioned marketing model of stock photos and templated sites. We strive to tell our clients’ stories with bespoke brands, impactful photo and video shoots, and stunning website design. We also provide ongoing services such as SEO, PPC, Social Media Marketing, and Funnels to help our practices get butts in seats. (Here is a great example of our work: precisiondentalbowlinggreen.com)

We need a skilled PPC Specialist with the flexibility to turn on your heels when priorities change, the thoughtfulness to do what’s in the best interest of the team, and the desire to always produce your best work. 

We’re a team of over 55 passionate marketers nestled in sunny San Diego. We are walking distance from local hangouts, bars, and the famed Liberty Station. While many companies simply check the required boxes when it comes to culture, a well-balanced team and positive office culture has been the core of our success from the start. We are a social team at heart, and have embraced a hybrid work model where team members come in at least two days a week. This allows us to work from home and have flexibility, without altogether forgoing the excitement and moments of collaboration and inspiration you can only get from in-person time. Not to mention, we also genuinely enjoy spending time together and socializing.

We celebrate new ideas and prioritize a culture of trust and respect where team members can grow and take risks. Each quarter we host events under our four cultural pillars; health & wellness, personal & professional development, social good, and social. We are proud to have been named one of San Diego Business Journal’s Best Places to work in 2018, 2021, 2022 and 2023, made the Inc 5000 Regionals Fastest Growing Companies 2021, 2022, and 2024, made the San Diego Business Journal’s Fastest Growing Private Companies list in 2019, 2020, 2022, and 2023 and made the Inc. 5000 for the last 4 years! 

If you work at Wonderist, you likely enjoy a challenge, appreciate autonomy, actually care about clients, and are looking for a team that you can enjoy being part of.

Job Description:

The PPC Specialist will work collaboratively with our team to build, manage, optimize, and evolve our PPC and Social Ad client accounts. Driving quality conversions for our clients through Google Ads and Social Media Ads is at the heart of this role. As a PPC Specialist, you will be responsible for developing and executing data-driven strategies, continuously monitoring and optimizing campaigns, and providing expert guidance to both clients and internal teams.

Your role extends beyond the day-to-day management of campaigns. You will be expected to uncover actionable insights, develop innovative processes, and contribute to the overall growth and success of our clients' digital marketing efforts. This will involve close collaboration with various departments, such as web development and client success, to ensure a comprehensive approach to PPC and Social Ads.

As a PPC Specialist, you will demonstrate a deep understanding of the ever-evolving digital marketing landscape, staying up-to-date on the latest industry trends and best practices. You will leverage this knowledge to create compelling ad copy, optimize landing pages, and develop targeted keyword strategies that maximize ROI for our clients.

Essential Functions:

The PPC Specialist will be expected to know the ins and outs of PPC and Social Ads campaigns and how to best execute them.

  • Pay-per-click strategy: Develop and execute data-driven PPC strategies using keyword analysis, A/B testing, and campaign optimization. Uncover actionable insights to maximize ROI and continuously monitor campaigns to ensure alignment with client goals.
  • Landing page optimization: Collaborate with development teams to create new and optimize existing landing pages for maximum PPC conversions. Provide strategic guidance and direction for landing page development across various content management systems, such as WordPress, HighLevel, Unbounce, and Webflow, ensuring alignment with campaign objectives and best practices.
  • Understanding of Google Suite: Demonstrate a strong working knowledge of the full range of Google products, including Google Ads, Google Analytics, Search Console, Google Tag Manager, Gmail, and Google Drive.
  • Keyword Research: Conduct in-depth keyword research to inform new and existing PPC campaigns. Monitor search term trends and utilize tools such as SEMrush, Ahrefs, and Google Keyword Planner to identify high-value opportunities. Develop and implement new campaigns from the ground up based on keyword research findings.
  • Campaign Reporting: Monitor and speak to monthly performance reports on clients' Facebook and Google Ads campaigns, focusing on KPIs. Demonstrate proficiency in utilizing business intelligence dashboards to monitor campaign status, troubleshoot as needed, measure success, and provide data-driven updates and recommendations to client success managers.
  • Ad Copy Creation: Craft engaging, positive, and authentic ad copy for Google Ads and Facebook Ads, adhering to best practices and platform-specific constraints. Develop ad content that highlights the unique selling propositions and differentiators of client accounts. Write compelling landing page copy that maximizes visitor engagement and leverages quality score opportunities.
  • Client Communication: Lead PPC and social ads discussions during client kick-off calls, onboarding meetings, and other client interactions as needed. Provide expert guidance and support to the client success team and other departments when client communications involve PPC and social ads topics, ensuring clear and effective communication of strategies and recommendations.
  • Data Analyzation: Examine PPC campaign data to uncover actionable insights that inform strategy and drive the development of new processes. Implement these processes efficiently to enhance clients' PPC performance continuously.

Core Competencies:

  • Strong Communication: You will be the subject matter expert regarding Paid Search strategy and responsible for relaying updates to our team; you must be personable, professional, and responsive. There is an intuition needed in this role to differentiate between moments when an email will suffice and when you need to pick up the phone.
  • Deadline-driven: Take ownership of assigned projects and consistently provide timely updates on the status of projects. Demonstrate a strong commitment to meeting deadlines while delivering high-quality work.
  • Strategic Thinking: Proactively identify opportunities and goals for clients, developing targeted strategies to address their unique needs. Collaborate with other departments to assess the feasibility and execution of innovative marketing ideas. Confidently present data-driven recommendations to clients and internal team members, demonstrating expertise in PPC and social ads.
  • Obsessive Attention to Detail: Maintain an exceptional level of organization to ensure no detail is overlooked. Deliver and maintain fully-optimized, high-performing marketing campaigns that drive consistent results for clients. Demonstrate an unwavering commitment to precision and quality in all aspects of campaign management, recognizing that success relies on a meticulous approach to detail.
  • Analytical Mindset: Consistently measure the success of all efforts by their return on investment for clients. Proficiency in creating new ad campaigns and analyzing their performance, distilling complex data into clear, concise insights. Effectively communicate results and recommendations to clients and team members in a straightforward manner, ensuring a shared understanding of campaign performance and opportunities for optimization.
  • Solutions Oriented Problem-Solving: Be proactive beyond the call of duty and swiftly reactive when there's a need to right any wrongs. Analyze problems by gathering and organizing relevant information, identify cause-and-effect relationships, and present appropriate solutions.
  • Client-Centric Mindset: Prioritize the client's best interests in all decision-making and actions. Maintain a laser focus on providing a marketing channel that maximizes the client's return on investment and supports their overall practice goals. Ensure that all strategic decisions and optimizations made on client accounts are aligned with their objectives and contribute to their long-term success.
  • Continuous Learning: Embrace the ever-evolving digital marketing landscape by staying up-to-date on the latest industry trends and best practices. Demonstrate a commitment to ongoing professional development to deliver cutting-edge strategies and exceptional results for clients.‍
  • Task Prioritization: Effectively manage a large volume of client accounts, skillfully allocating resources to address their needs and ensure optimal client satisfaction. Demonstrate sound judgment in prioritizing tasks and accounts based on urgency and importance, adapting to changing circumstances as needed.

Personality Musts:

Our perfect fit is someone who is a problem solver who understands the difference between “yes, and…” and “yes, but…”. You should have a hungry, scrappy personality with a positive attitude and aims to deliver. Working in the client service industry can mean going the extra mile, and we need someone who is always willing to rise to the occasion for our clients and puts forward beautiful work that exceeds client expectations. Someone who is detailed and organized, driven and displays vigor and comes ready to play every day. Confidence, self-discipline, and a congenial, curious mind are things we love and admire. 

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Google Ads Certified
  • 2+ years Google Ads experience.
  • Bachelor’s degree in a related field (Marketing, Website Development, Digital Media, etc.)

Bonus Points for Applicants With:

  • 2+ years of agency experience
  • 1+ year experience healthcare marketing experience
  • Google Apps Script experience
  • Facebook Social Ads experience
  • Basic understanding of HTML/CSS
  • SEO Experience

​

title: SEO Specialist
jobDescription:
MaintainX is the world's leading Asset and Work Intelligence platform for industrial and frontline environments. We are a modern IoT-enabled cloud-based tool for reliability, safety, and operations on physical equipment and facilities. MaintainX powers operational excellence for 10,000+ businesses including Duracell, Univar Solutions Inc., Titan America, McDonald's, Brenntag, Cintas, Xylem, and Shell.

We've raised $104 million in venture capital following a recent Series C funding round led by Bain Capital Ventures, Bessemer Ventures, August Capital, Amity Ventures, Ridge Ventures as well as CEO’s from GE, Twilio, Coupa, Toast and PagerDuty.

As a SEO Specialist, you will play a key role in developing, implementing, and optimizing SEO strategies that improve organic visibility, traffic, and conversions. You will collaborate with content, product, and development teams to ensure SEO best practices are integrated across all initiatives. Reporting to the SEO Manager, you will be a key member of our Demand Generation team, driving initiatives that accelerate traffic and organic customer acquisition. 

This role is remote in Canada or USA

Key Responsibilities:

  • Develop and execute comprehensive SEO strategies to improve search rankings and organic traffic.
  • Conduct in-depth keyword research, competitive analysis, and site audits to identify growth opportunities.
  • Experiment and optimize AI-driven content optimization workflows (e.g., Surfer SEO, MarketMuse) to improve content performance and SEO efficiency.
  • Optimize on-page elements, including title tags, meta descriptions, headers, and content structure, for SEO effectiveness.
  • Work closely with content teams to create and optimize high-quality, search-friendly content.
  • Manage technical SEO initiatives, including crawlability, indexability, structured data, and site performance improvements.
  • Collaborate with developers to implement SEO-friendly site architecture, redirects, and schema markup.
  • Monitor, analyze, and report on SEO performance using tools like Google Analytics, Google Search Console, and SEO platforms (e.g., Ahrefs, SEMrush, or Surfer SEO).
  • Stay updated on search engine algorithms, industry trends, and best practices to keep our SEO strategy ahead of the curve.
  • Support the development and execution of link-building and digital PR strategies alongside our content and marketing teams to enhance domain authority.

About you:

  • 3+ years of experience in SEO, preferably in an in-house role at a fast-growing B2B SaaS company or equivalent relevant experience.
  • Strong understanding of on-page, off-page, and technical SEO best practices.
  • Proficiency with SEO tools such as Google Search Console, Google Analytics, Ahrefs, SEMrush, Screaming Frog, Keyword insights, and Surfer SEO.
  • Experience leveraging AI tools and technologies (e.g., ChatGPT, Jasper, MarketMuse) to enhance SEO workflows, content creation, or performance analysis.
  • Experience with Webflow CMS, strongly preferred but not mandatory.
  • Ability to analyze data, generate insights, and make data-driven SEO recommendations.
  • Familiarity with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript as they relate to SEO.
  • Experience with structured data implementation and Core Web Vitals optimization.
  • Strong project management skills and ability to prioritize tasks effectively.
  • Experience collaborating with cross-functional teams, including developers, designers, and content creators.

What’s in it for you:

  • Competitive salary and meaningful equity opportunities.
  • Healthcare, dental, and vision coverage.
  • 401(k) / RRSP enrollment program.
  • Take what you need PTO.
  • A Work Culture where:
    • You’ll work alongside folks across the globe that reflect the MaintainX values, Smart Humble Optimist.
    • We believe in meritocracy, where ideas and effort are publicly celebrated.

About us:

Our mission is to make the life of blue-collar workers easier worldwide by creating software that meets their needs and realities. Our product is truly life-changing for 80% of the workforce that doesn’t work behind a desk and needs enterprise-grade software at their fingertips.

MaintainX is committed to creating a diverse environment. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.

title: Senior Graphic Designer
jobDescription:
As a SeniorGraphic Designer at Designity, you will transform ideas into visually compelling designs that capture attention and bring brand stories to life. Your work will span branding, packaging, social media assets, and marketing collateral, helping clients establish strong visual identities. As part of the Designity Creative Community, you'll collaborate with other top-tier creatives while refining your craft through exciting and challenging projects and utilizing AI-powered tools to champion efficiency.

‍

Designity is a Creative-as-a-Service (CaaS) platform that empowers businesses with a flexible, high-quality, and scalable alternative to traditional agencies and in-house teams.

‍

Our unique model connects marketing and creative leaders with the top 3% of US-based creative and marketing talent, all led by experienced Creative Directors, Project Managers, and Marketing Strategists. Through our collaborative platform, we streamline the creative process, ensuring fast, high-impact execution without the inefficiencies of hiring in-house or the high costs of traditional agencies.

‍

Brands like Calendly, Kung Fu Tea, and Marriott Hotels trust Designity to handle their design and marketing needs—proving that there's a better way to scale creativity. Unlike in-house hiring, which is expensive and skill-limiting, or marketplaces like Upwork and Fiverr, which lack reliability and creative oversight, Designity delivers a seamless, results-driven approach that makes creative collaboration effortless.

‍

PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES:

  • Design branding, packaging, social media content, and marketing materials that align with client goals and industry trends.
  • Develop original concepts and bring them to life through polished, high-quality visuals.
  • Adapt designs to different industries and client needs while maintaining their brand integrity.
  • Collaborate with Creative Directors and fellow designers to refine and implement design concepts based on feedback.
  • Utilise AI-powered tools to streamline design workflows, experiment with creative concepts, and fine-tune visuals.
  • Stay up to date by exploring emerging design trends, software updates, and industry best practices.

REQUIREMENTS

  • 6+ years of experience in graphic design, backed by a strong portfolio showcasing expertise in branding, typography, layout, and visual storytelling.
  • Exceptional design skills and experience using various tools, including Adobe Creative Suite, as well as using AI-powered tools to enhance efficiency and creativity.
  • Experience translating marketing strategies into effective visual assets.
  • Ability to create polished designs with minimal input and proactively request feedback for improvement.
  • Detail-oriented, highly organized, and able to meet deadlines consistently.
  • Driven to push creative boundaries, share knowledge, and refine your craft within a collaborative design community.
  • Strong verbal and written communication skills in English.

BENEFITS:

  • Join a company at the perfect moment! Career making opportunity.
  • Work fully remotely from wherever you are.
  • Unwind during the flexible PTO, and company holidays.
  • Learn and grow with the best by becoming part of our exclusive Design Community.
  • Enjoy dedicated focus times designed to boost your productivity and creativity.
  • ‍

In order to be considered for this position, you need to complete the application process at www.designity.com/careers/senior-graphic-designer

‍

Please do NOT follow up on your application. We will be in touch within 2-3 weeks if you're qualified.

‍

At Designity, employment decisions are based on business needs, qualifications, and merit. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer, valuing diversity in culture, perspectives, skills, and experiences, which strengthens our team, clients, and products.

title: Senior Webflow Developer
jobDescription:
Tapcheck is looking for a Senior Webflow Developer to lead the development, optimization, and continuous evolution of our digital presence. In this role, you'll own our website, landing pages, and campaign pages, ensuring they are high-performing, visually compelling, and conversion-focused. You'll collaborate closely with the marketing team to bring campaigns to life, enhance user experience, and drive engagement across all digital touchpoints. Your work will play a key role in scaling Tapcheck's online growth and elevating our brand.

What You'll Do:

  • Own the end-to-end development and management of Tapcheck's Webflow website, ensuring a seamless, high-performing experience.
  • Collaborate with the marketing team to implement landing pages, campaign pages, and website updates that drive engagement and conversions.
  • Optimize site speed, responsiveness, and SEO best practices to enhance visibility and performance.
  • Manage and collaborate with external agencies to scale Webflow development efforts, ensuring high-quality execution while maintaining brand and performance standards.
  • Develop custom interactions, animations, and CMS-driven content to elevate UX/UI.
  • Integrate Webflow with marketing tools, analytics platforms, and automation workflows Troubleshoot and resolve technical issues to ensure uptime, security, and functionality.
  • Maintain design consistency, accessibility standards, and mobile-first best practices.
  • Stay ahead of Webflow updates, industry trends, and best practices to continuously enhance Tapcheck's digital presence.

What You'll Bring:

  • 4+ years of Webflow development experience, with a strong portfolio showcasing marketing-driven websites.
  • Design experience, with a keen eye for layout, typography, and user experience best practices
  • Strong understanding of SEO, site performance optimization, and conversion rate best practices.
  • Experience integrating Webflow with marketing tools, automation platforms, and analytics dashboards.
  • A collaborative mindset, working closely with designers, content strategists, and growth marketers.
  • A data-driven approach, leveraging insights to inform design and functionality improvements
  • Previous experience in B2B SaaS, fintech, HR tech, or a high-growth startup

At this time, Tapcheck does not provide sponsorship for employment-based visas (e.g., H-b, L-1, TN, etc.). Therefore, candidates must already possess the right to work in the United States without the need for employment-based visa sponsorship now or in the future.​

This is a remote-friendly role. Ideally, candidates will sit in the following states: AL, AZ, CA, CO, DC, DE, FL, GA, ID, IL, LA, MA, MI, MO, NC, NH, NJ, NV, NY, PA, OR, OH, RI, SC, TX, VA, WA, WI

About Tapcheck:

Tapcheck is a digital platform offering an easy and convenient way to access on-demand earnings early. Available at no cost to employers, our app-based on-demand pay solution helps relieve the financial stress that many employees experience on a daily basis. The Tapcheck team is passionate about our mission to improve financial wellness and boost business productivity. By giving workers the ability to transfer wages they've earned directly to their bank account or pay card without waiting for payday, Tapcheck eliminates the need for high-interest payday loans or employer-funded cash advances.

How We Get Things Done:

Our core values act as a steadfast guide, directing our decisions and anchoring our actions. We consider these values non-negotiable, especially when it comes to our hiring process.

  • Humility: We believe in the power of humility. We value team players who are down-to-earth, respectful, and open to learning from others. Our employees approach challenges with a positive attitude, acknowledging their strengths and weaknesses while celebrating the achievements of their colleagues
  • Grit: We admire individuals with grit - those who demonstrate unwavering determination and resilience in the face of obstacles. At Tapcheck, we take pride in overcoming challenges together, pushing the boundaries of what is possible, and embracing failure as an opportunity for growth
  • Raising the Bar: Continuous improvement is at the heart of our culture. We are committed to setting high standards and pushing ourselves to exceed them. We seek employees who are innovative and strive for excellence, constantly seeking ways to enhance our products, services, and processes
  • Striving for Growth: We foster an environment that encourages personal and professional development. Our employees are driven to learn, grow, and adapt to new circumstances. We support individuals who take initiative, seek out new challenges, and actively contribute to their own growth and the growth of the company

Why Join Tapcheck?

  • Flexible Paid Time Off
  • Health Insurance
  • Dental Insurance
  • Vision Insurance
  • 401K Match

Compensation: $130,000 - $140,000 base + bonus. The actual base salary will depend on numerous factors such as: location, experience, training, knowledge. and skills. Tapcheck reserves the right to amend, change, alter, and revise pay ranges and benefits offerings at any time. All applicants acknowledge that by applying to this position you understand that this specific pay range is contingent upon meeting the qualifications and requirements of the role, and for the successful completion of the interview selection and process. It is at the Company's discretion to determine what pay is provided to a candidate within the range associated with the role.​

Equal Employment Opportunity Policy​

Tapcheck, Inc. provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, recall, transfer, leaves of absence, compensation and training.

title: Web Design and Email Specialist (Hybrid)
jobDescription:
We are currently seeking a Web Design & Email Specialist to join our team in Ottawa.

​

In this role, you will support web design projects and tasks, email marketing campaigns, and brand design initiatives, contributing to visually compelling and user-friendly experiences. Your work will help businesses establish or strengthen brand identities, enhance marketing efforts, and optimize user engagement across platforms.

​

The ideal candidate is a self-starting, enthusiastic, and driven professional with a solid foundation in design and UX best practices. They should be comfortable working in a collaborative team environment, directly engaging with clients, and confidently presenting creative concepts.

​

This is a hybrid role, requiring in-office work three days per week. The Web Design & Email Specialist reports to the Design Lead and collaborates closely with designers, developers, and marketing professionals to deliver high-quality creative solutions.

​

Responsibilities

Qualifications & Skills

  • Proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite and Figma; Webflow experience is a plus

​

If you’re excited about working in a dynamic, creative environment and making an impact through design, apply today!

title: Webflow Solutions Architect
jobDescription:
At Webflow, our mission is to bring development superpowers to everyone. As the pioneer of the Website Experience Platform (WXP), we’re redefining how teams Build, Manage, and Optimize for the web — combining visual development, powerful content management systems, AI-driven personalization, seamless hosting, and end-to-end analytics in a single, unified platform. With AI at the core, Webflow helps teams move faster, create more performant digital experiences, and scale without heavy engineering support. From independent designers and creative agencies to global enterprises, hundreds of thousands of organizations use Webflow to turn ideas into reality — and to power what’s possible on the web.

We’re looking for a Solutions Architect to help customers build, launch, and grow with Webflow by providing technical guidance, best practices, and solutions that demonstrate a deep understanding of customers’ business needs, technical needs, Webflow’s product, and trends in the industry. You will collaborate with cross-functional team members to uncover and unblock opportunities that allow customers to maximize their business value from Webflow, driving product and feature adoption and influencing product direction on their behalf. 

With your technical expertise and industry knowledge you'll be able to help customers build with best practices in mind ensuring they'll build in a scalable manner and be able to grow in Webflow for years to come.

About the role:

  • Location: Remote-first (United States; BC & ON, Canada)
  • Full-time 
  • Permanent 
  • Exempt
  • The cash compensation for this role is tailored to align with the cost of labor in different geographic markets. We've structured the base pay ranges for this role into zones for our geographic markets, and the specific base pay within the range will be determined by the candidate’s geographic location, job-related experience, knowledge, qualifications, and skills.
  • United States (all figures cited below are in USD and pertain to workers in the United States)
      • Zone A: $130,500 - $165,000
      • Zone B: $124,000 - $156,500
    • Zone C: $117,000 - $148,500
  • Canada (all figures cited below are in CAD and pertain to workers in Canada)
    • 147,500 - 187,000

This role is also eligible to participate in Webflow's company-wide bonus program. Target amounts are a percentage of base salary and vary by career level. Payouts are based on company performance against established financial and operational goals. 

Please visit our Careers page for more information on which locations are included in each of our geographic pay zones. However, please confirm the zone for your specific location with your recruiter.

  • Reporting to the Director, Solutions Architects

As a Solutions Architect, you’ll … 

  • Collaborate with Sales and Customer Success peers to launch new customers, drive successful product adoption, and reduce time to first value from Webflow. You’ll help tailor our launch strategies according to each customer’s business needs and objectives.
  • Partner with Customer Success Managers to cultivate and deepen strategic relationships with multiple customers simultaneously, inspiring them and instilling them with confidence to grow their businesses with Webflow.
  • Provide technical guidance in a consultative manner for customers, uncovering opportunities and steering them with detailed implementation plans that unblock their ability to successfully launch and grow with Webflow. 
  • Advocate for customers and influence product direction by gathering product feedback, aggregating trends into themes, and surfacing insights via our Voice of Customer program.
  • Build cutting edge, repeatable artifacts to help tell Webflow's story in a compelling way that aligns with bringing our customers business value. You will combine product evangelism and your knowledge of customers to create templates, integrations, and site examples that make adopting Webflow easy.

In addition to the responsibilities outlined above, at Webflow we will support you in identifying where your interests and development opportunities lie and we'll help you incorporate them into your role.

About you:

You’ll thrive as a Solutions Architect if you:

  • Have 2-3 years of experience in web development or visual development, with a strong focus on Webflow.
  • Are enthusiastic about earning customer trust, getting to know their businesses, and helping them achieve their goals.
  • Can effectively communicate technical concepts to nontechnical audiences and can facilitate conversations by bridging that gap.
  • Are comfortable working in fast-paced environments, adapting to changing priorities, and collaborating with a wide variety of people across functions, backgrounds, and experience levels.
  • Have strong problem-solving skills and enjoy tackling challenges, with the ability to identify cases where challenges or solutions are net new vs. cases where previous learnings can be repurposed.
  • Have a basic understanding of front-end web development concepts and tools (including HTML, CSS, and JavaScript), and are eager to learn more.
  • Are open-minded with a bias toward growth, engaging in opportunities to learn, expand your current scope, and consider differing opinions.
  • Welcome a diverse mix of individuals (including customers and colleagues) and their different perspective
  • Stay curious and open to growth — actively building fluency in emerging technologies like AI to unlock creativity, accelerate progress, and amplify impact.

 

Our Core Behaviors:

  • Obsess over customer experience. We deeply understand what we’re building and who we’re building for and serving. We define the leading edge of what’s possible in our industry and deliver the future for our customers
  • Move with heartfelt urgency. We have a healthy relationship with impatience, channeling it thoughtfully to show up better and faster for our customers and for each other. Time is the most limited thing we have, and we make the most of every moment
  • Say the hard thing with care. Our best work often comes from intelligent debate, critique, and even difficult conversations. We speak our minds and don’t sugarcoat things — and we do so with respect, maturity, and care
  • Make your mark. We seek out new and unique ways to create meaningful impact, and we champion the same from our colleagues. We work as a team to get the job done, and we go out of our way to celebrate and reward those going above and beyond for our customers and our teammates

Benefits & wellness

  • Equity ownership (RSUs) in a growing, privately-owned company
  • 100% employer-paid healthcare, vision, and dental insurance coverage for full-time employees (working 30+ hours per week) and their dependents. Full-time employees may also be eligible for voluntary insurance options where applicable in the respective country of employment
  • 12 weeks of paid parental leave for both birthing and non-birthing caregivers, as well as an additional 6-8 weeks of pregnancy disability leave for birthing parents to be used before child bonding leave (note: where local requirements are more generous, employees receive the greater benefit); full-time employees also have access to family planning care and reimbursement
  • Flexible PTO for all locations and sabbatical program
  • Access to mental wellness and professional coaching, therapy, and Employee Assistance Program
  • Monthly stipends to support work and wellness
  • 401k plan or pension schemes (in countries where statutorily required), and other financial wellness benefits, like CPA and financial advisor coverage

Temporary employees may be eligible for paid holiday and time off, statutory leaves of absence, and company-sponsored medical benefits depending on their Fixed Term Contract and their country/state of employment.

Remote, together

At Webflow, equality is a core tenet of our culture. We are an Equal Opportunity (EEO)/Veterans/Disabled Employer and are committed to building an inclusive global team that represents a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, beliefs, and experiences. Employment decisions are made on the basis of job-related criteria without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status, or any other classification protected by applicable law. Pursuant to the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, Webflow will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records.

Stay connected

Not ready to apply, but want to be part of the Webflow community? Consider following our story on our Webflow Blog, LinkedIn, X (Twitter), and/or Glassdoor. 

Please note:

We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Upon interview scheduling, instructions for confidential accommodation requests will be administered.

To join Webflow, you'll need a valid right to work authorization depending on the country of employment.

If you are extended an offer, that offer may be contingent upon your successful completion of a background check, which will be conducted in accordance with applicable laws. We may obtain one or more background screening reports about you, solely for employment purposes.

For information about how Webflow processes your personal information, please review Webflow’s Applicant Privacy Notice.

title: Conversion Rate Optimization Senior Manager
jobDescription:
The Conversion Rate Optimization Manager will join the Digital Operations team within Marketing. This role will own the journey and conversion path to drive quality leads and account signups, in support of the company’s revenue goals. The candidate will have a proven track record of improving conversion for web forms, online applications, chat, multi-step wizards and other site conversions. 

This role will be responsible for leveraging data, analytics and technology to assess insights and recommend improvements to revenue marketing programs. You will collaborate with several different teams across the business on various activities including email campaigns, digital advertising, content marketing, website analytics and social media. This individual will help develop key measurement strategies to ensure optimal conversions from digital channels.

This person is curious and expected to strike the balance in lead forms to get the necessary information while creating an easy workflow that users want to engage without negatively impacting lead volume and lead quality, using testing as a methodical learning process.

Responsibilities: 

  • Build and test to create high-converting landing pages for a range of campaigns including paid search, content and more
  • Provide strategic UX, content & design related recommendations for improving the number of quality leads and conversions
  • Analyze and track KPIs, leveraging performance data to identify optimization within digital experiences
  • Ability to complete data analysis for website conversion and engagement metrics by channel and source
  • Research competition and investigate benchmarks to further site conversion rates and design
  • Develop dashboards and marketing reporting processes so visibility is accessible in real time
  • Develop key measurement strategies to ensure consistency in reporting and analysis across 
  • Drive standardization and automation in marketing performance reporting across analysis and reporting deliverables
  • Supports testing, site migrations, content and other updates from a data tracking and analytics perspective

Job Requirements

  • 5+ years of professional experience in conversion rate optimization for lead generation and cross sell. 
  • Bachelor’s degree preferred
  • Track record of systematic testing to improve conversion rates
  • Prior experience with UserTesting, Optimizely, Test and Target or similar tools to set up and run the optimization tests. 
  • International digital user experience or digital marketing experience preferred. 
  • Experience in B2B or Financial Technology lead gen preferred
  • Experience with single and multi-variant testing methodologies and best practices
  • Basic knowledge of web design procedures
  • Sense of ownership and pride in your performance and its impact on company’s success
  • Data proficiency with a passion for using data, metrics and insights to drive results
  • Lead and influence leadership to enhance marketing efforts through data-driven decision-making
  • Self-starter that will jump in to solve problems quickly and use data to answer questions, analyze user behavior and monitor performance 
  • Understands marketing automation and lead scoring analysis platforms such as Pardot.
  • Understands and utilizes business information to manage everyday operations and generate innovative solutions to approach business and administrative challenges
  • Ability to proactively think ahead and anticipate needs & considerations
  • Experience managing processes and delivering technical analysis 
  • Experience with tools such as Google 360, GA4, Google Data Studio, Google Optimize, and Unbounce.
  • Expert-level Excel and Google Sheets for data normalization, small-batch data processing, and business analysis
  • Excellent communication skills, particularly when articulating findings in meaningful, clear ways to stakeholders with an emphasis on actionable insight
  • Exceptional time management skills
  • Self-motivated and disciplined with the ability to define and achieve objectives with limited oversight

​

Global Payments Inc. is an equal opportunity employer.
