title: Digital Marketing-SEO (Internship)
jobDescription:
Roles & Responsibilities:

​

  • Assist in conducting keyword research and developing SEO-focused content strategies.
  • Optimize on-page elements such as meta titles, descriptions, headers, and image alt tags.
  • Audit website pages for SEO performance, broken links, and technical issues using tools like Google Search Console, Ahrefs, or SEMrush.
  • Support the creation and optimization of blog posts, landing pages, and web copy for JanBask’s core services (Salesforce consulting, web/app development, cloud solutions, etc.).
  • Monitor organic traffic, keyword rankings, and user behavior using tools like Google Analytics.
  • Stay updated with the latest SEO trends, algorithm changes, and competitor activity.
  • Collaborate with content creators, developers, and the marketing team to implement SEO best practices across all channels.

Position Requirements

​

  • Currently pursuing or recently completed a degree in Marketing, Communications, IT, or a related field.
  • Strong writing and editing skills with an interest in tech-focused content.
  • Basic understanding of SEO principles (on-page, off-page, and technical SEO).
  • Familiarity with SEO tools like Google Analytics, Google Search Console, SEMrush, or Ubersuggest is a plus.
  • Detail-oriented, analytical, and eager to learn.
  • Comfortable working in a remote environment with solid time management skills.

Email Resume to

​

Career@JanBask.com

title: Google AdWords & Digital Advertising Specialist - Richmond, BC
jobDescription:
About Jukebox

Jukebox is a global online printing company known for high-quality print products and excellent customer service. Based in Richmond, BC, we take pride in our craftsmanship and our practical approach to digital marketing. Our small team works closely together, including direct collaboration with our development and data colleagues.

What We're Looking For

We are seeking a seasoned professional who specializes in Google AdWords and digital advertising. With at least 5 years of solid industry experience, you will be responsible for planning, launching, and fine-tuning ad campaigns across Google, Facebook, Instagram, and other platforms. The bulk of your work will focus on Google AdWords, ensuring our ads convert effectively and deliver measurable results.

What You'll Do

  • Campaign Management: Create, launch, and optimize advertising campaigns on Google AdWords, as well as on Meta (Facebook and Instagram) and other digital channels.
  • Performance Tracking: Monitor campaign metrics, analyze data, and adjust strategies to boost conversion rates and overall ROI.
  • A/B Testing: Plan and execute A/B tests on ad creatives, landing pages, and targeting parameters to continually improve campaign performance.
  • Budget Management: Oversee ad spend and bidding strategies to maximize impact while maintaining budget discipline.
  • Collaboration: Work directly with our development and data teams to integrate technical insights and performance data into your advertising strategies.
  • Reporting: Prepare clear, actionable reports on campaign performance and suggest areas for improvement.

Who You Are

  • Experienced: You have a minimum of 5 years in digital advertising or online marketing, with a strong background managing campaigns on Google and Meta platforms.
  • Conversion-Driven: You know how to drive conversions and optimize campaigns through rigorous A/B testing and data analysis.
  • Data-Savvy: You are comfortable working with performance metrics and using tools like Google Analytics to guide your strategy.
  • Up-to-Date: You keep current with digital marketing trends and best practices, including retargeting strategies and dynamic ad creative.
  • Collaborative: You thrive in a small-team environment and value clear, direct communication.
  • Proven Success: You can demonstrate a strong track record by sharing examples of previous successful campaigns, including measurable results such as increased conversions and improved ROI.

What You Need

  • Relevant Experience: At least 5 years of direct experience in online advertising or digital marketing, with a heavy focus on Google AdWords.
  • Technical Skills: Proven expertise in setting up, managing, and optimizing campaigns on Google AdWords, as well as experience with Facebook and Instagram advertising.
  • Certification: Must hold current Google Ads and Meta (Facebook) certifications along with any other relevant digital advertising credentials.
  • Proven Track Record: Be prepared to present detailed examples or case studies of previous successful campaigns that highlight your ability to drive results.
  • Educational Background: A bachelor's degree in Marketing, Business, or a related field is encouraged; however, equivalent professional experience is also acceptable.
  • Collaboration: Ability to work in our Richmond, BC office and closely with our development and data teams.

Work Schedule and Compensation

This is a full-time, in-person role based in Richmond, BC. We offer flexible work hours and a competitive starting wage of $27-$30+ per hour, along with a comprehensive benefits package.

Join Our Team

If you are a results-oriented digital advertising specialist with a passion for Google AdWords and a proven record of successful campaigns, we'd like to meet you. At Jukebox, you'll work in a collaborative environment where your expertise directly impacts our online presence and overall success.

title: Senior Manager, Brand Marketing
jobDescription:
The Senior Manager of Brand Marketing will lead efforts to strengthen CloudZero’s brand positioning, increase awareness, and ensure consistency across all brand touch-points. You’ll develop and execute strategic marketing campaigns that reinforce CloudZero’s thought leadership, customer impact, and category leadership in cloud cost optimization.

This is an exciting opportunity to help define the voice of a high-growth SaaS company and drive engagement with enterprise audiences, industry analysts, and partners.

What You'll Do:

  • Lead CloudZero’s brand strategy and ensure consistent messaging across all channels (social, web, content, events, PR).
  • Conduct competitive and industry research to refine brand positioning.
  • Collaborate with Product Marketing, Growth, and PR on thought leadership content (blogs, reports, video, social).
  • Manage executive brand presence (CEO/CTO LinkedIn, speaking opportunities, bylines).
  • Own flagship brand campaigns, including annual reports and awards submissions.
  • Oversee social media strategy and content, focusing on follower growth and engagement.
  • Lead customer marketing efforts, including case studies and sales proof points.
  • Track brand performance metrics (awareness, share of voice, campaign effectiveness).
  • Guide visual brand identity and creative direction across digital, campaigns, and events.
  • Manage brand guidelines and ensure consistent adoption across teams.

What You'll Bring:

  • 8+ years of experience in brand marketing, content marketing, or communications—preferably in B2B SaaS, cloud, or FinOps.
  • Strong storytelling and content creation skills, with the ability to translate complex concepts into engaging narratives.
  • Experience developing integrated marketing campaigns that drive brand awareness and audience engagement.
  • Proven ability to collaborate across functions, working closely with Product Marketing, PR, Revenue and Growth Marketing, and Sales.
  • Passion for cloud technology, AI, and FinOps trends—with a drive to position CloudZero as the market leader.

Equal Opportunity Employer

CloudZero is an equal opportunity employer and values diversity. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, gender, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status or disability status. All job offers are contingent upon the candidate passing background and reference checks.

Applicants must be authorized to work for ANY employer in the United States. We are unable to sponsor or take over sponsorship of an employment Visa at this time.

title: Web Content Designer
jobDescription:
The Bank of Canada has a vision to be a leading central bank—dynamic, engaged and trusted—committed to a better Canada. No other employer in the country offers you the unique opportunity to work at the very center of Canada’s economy, in an organization with significant impact on the economic and financial well-being of all Canadians. You will be challenged, energized and motivated to excel in our environment.

​

Building on the principles that have always guided us – excellence, integrity and respect – we strive to be forward-looking and innovative, to welcome people with diverse perspectives and talents, and to earn trust by living up to our commitments and by clearly explaining the intent of our policies and actions.

​

With our defined-benefit pension plan, benefits, and high flexibility for work life balance - find out more about why we are annually ranked as one of Canada's top employers: Working Here - Bank of Canada

​

Find out more about the next steps in our Recruitment process.

​

What you will do

We’re looking for someone who enjoys designing and delivering high-quality, user-focused website content that is clear, concise, and accessible. If you are pursuing a career in content design and are an advocate for great user experience, this position may be perfect for you.

​

We’re a small agile team of designers, videographers and content specialists committed to making the Bank’s digital communications leading-edge. We collaborate with stakeholders across the Bank to make our websites useable and useful.

​

As a content designer on the team, you’ll advise on day-to-day requests and work with the Manager of Digital Content Design to evolve our content-design practice.

​

More specifically you will:

  • Content design practice and standards
  • Uphold and evolve the Bank’s content-design practice
  • Continuously improve our tools, processes, procedures and standards
  • Participate in content-design critiques
  • Consult on content-design requests

​

Collaboration and stakeholder management

  • Work with subject-matter experts and stakeholders on content for all the Bank’s websites:
    • https://www.bankofcanada.ca/
    • https://www.bankofcanadamuseum.ca/
    • intranet
  • Collaborate with developers, graphic designers and videographers to seek out opportunities to enhance our content with visuals
  • Prioritize work to support departmental and organizational goals
  • Facilitate meetings and discussions while clearly communicating the rationale behind recommendations
  • Lead with influence, forming and maintaining collaborative relationships

​

Audience focus and accessibility

  • Collaborate with our research and analytics partners to understand how our audiences use the Bank’s websites and make better, data-based decisions
  • Be an active champion for readability and accessibility; knowing when to bend language rules and style guidelines to ensure clarity over “correctness”
  • Collaborate with subject-matter experts (SMEs), editors and translators to build web content in both official languages

​

Content strategy and analysis

  • Analyze and shape content requirements to deliver compelling experiences
  • Perform content audits and analysis to find ways to enhance, or remove, what we already have available
  • Prototype content solutions for critique, ensuring usability for both desktop and mobile

​

What you need to succeed

  • Have a keen attention to detail and a strong desire to get things right — but not at the expense of getting things done
  • Experience with making complicated concepts easy to understand for various audiences
  • Experience with managing projects and triaging requests
  • Demonstrated knowledge of tools and processes for creating and managing content for the web

​

Application process

Your application must include the following:

  • curriculum vitae
  • examples of your work in content design or digital services
  • demonstrate excellent content design

​

Nice-to-have

  • experience using CMS and working in HTML/CSS
  • experience working with an agile Scrum team
  • experience improving the culture and process for developing content at a large organization
  • knowledge of Canada’s financial industry and the Bank’s business functions

​

Your education and experience

This position requires a university degree in a relevant discipline (such as information systems and design, library studies, information management, digital media design or communications) with two years of relevant experience or an equivalent combination of education and experience may be considered.

​

Language requirement

The Bank’s work environment is conducive to the use of both of Canada’s official languages - English and French. The position language requirement is Level 5 (Fully Functional). If a qualified candidate who meets the language requirement of the position is not found, a qualified candidate who does not meet the language requirement may be considered. Training may be provided to help reach the required level. Both bilingual and unilingual candidates are encouraged to apply.

​

What you need to know

  • Priority will be given to Canadian citizens and permanent residents
  • Security level required: Be eligible to obtain Reliability
  • Relocation assistance may be provided, if required
  • Please save a copy of the job poster. Once the closing date has passed, it will no longer be available.

​

Hybrid Work Model

The Bank offers work arrangements that provide employees with flexibility, enable high-performing teams, and support an excellent workplace culture. Most employees can telework from home for a substantial part of each month as part of the Bank`s hybrid work model, and they are expected on site at the Bank location a minimum of eight days per month to help build connections between colleagues. You must live in Canada, and within reasonable commuting distance of the office.

​

What you can expect from us

This is a great opportunity to join a leading organization and be part of a high-performing team. We offer a competitive compensation and benefits package designed to meet your needs at every stage of your life and career. For more information on key benefits please visit A great deal to consider.

​

  • Salaries are based on qualifications and experience and typically range from $80,967 to $95,255 (job grade 15)
  • The Bank offers an incentive for successfully meeting expectations at 5 to 7% of your base salary. The Bank offers additional performance pay (3%) for those who exceed expectations. Exceptional performers who far exceed expectations may be eligible for higher performance pay.
  • Flexible and comprehensive benefits so you can choose the level of health, dental disability and life and/or accident insurance coverage that meets your needs
  • Extra vacation days (up to five each year) that you can purchase to add to your vacation entitlement
  • Indexed, defined-benefit pension

​

We wish to thank all applicants for their interest and effort in applying for this position. Only candidates selected for interviews will be contacted.

title: Web Designer
jobDescription:
We help companies stay secure while moving fast.

​

Built by engineers for engineers, The Teleport Access Platform delivers on-demand, least privileged access to infrastructure based on cryptographic identity and zero trust, with built-in identity security and policy governance, making the happy path for engineers the secure path.

​

Teleport is trusted by the world’s fastest-moving companies, including Elastic, Snowflake, Doordash, and NASDAQ. We recently raised US$110M at a US$1.1B valuation. 

​

Our Vision

​

Today's computing environments have too much complexity, too many network boundaries, and too little trust. Complexity slows engineers down and leads to human errors. Complex systems can’t be secure despite the red tape of bureaucracy. We make trusted computing simple. This gives engineers the freedom to move and build a better future.

​

Why Teleport

​

At Teleport, we focus on empowering our people to accomplish their goals by working alongside highly talented people to make the most of their careers. You have the freedom, autonomy and trust to do what you’re great at and have a significant impact on the future prospects of the company. 

​

Whether that’s taking a feature or project from ideation to deployment or working with some of the biggest, most interesting companies in the world and solving real challenges for them, we want you to help us build the future.

​

We’re not a big company. You won’t get lost in a crowd. Instead, we move fast, with a team that wants to make an impact, that shares in our success, and gives you the freedom, power, and autonomy to become the very best at what you do.

​

As a Web Designer, you’ll work on crafting a user-friendly, visually striking, and conversion-optimized web experience that reflects the cutting-edge nature of our products and our customers' innovative spirit. From creating dynamic layouts to integrating complex technical details seamlessly into design, your work will directly impact how customers interact with our brand.

​

  • Who you are - To succeed at Teleport and take your career into the future, we are looking for people who are:Autonomous: We value those who take initiative and get things done. Our team trusts in their ability to make decisions that benefit the company and its customers, letting their work speak for itself
  • Security-minded: Working on the very fabric of the internet and providing infrastructure access to some of the world’s biggest companies means you need to think security-first. Our platform is the gatekeeper - The Wall. And winter is coming. We need to ensure we’re secure
  • Business-savvy: We don’t code for coding's sake. We build for our customers. Designed by engineers for engineers, we understand their environment, challenges and needs better than anyone else. That means we have built a business that can support them by making the right choices that ensure we are in business for the long haul
  • Professional: We are a team of dedicated professionals, committed to excellence. We set a high bar for joining Teleport, ensuring we attract top talent ready to help shape the future. If you’re passionate about being the best at what you do, Teleport is the place for you

​

  • What You’ll Be Doing:Design and optimize responsive, high-performance web layouts that align with Teleport’s brand and user-first principles
  • Collaborate closely with marketing, engineering, and product teams to create digital assets that communicate complex technical concepts through intuitive and visually engaging designs
  • Develop dynamic landing pages, campaign micro-sites, and interactive elements that enhance user engagement and drive conversions
  • Collaborate with developers to ensure seamless implementation of designs, bridging the gap between design vision and technical execution
  • Build and maintain a cohesive design system in Figma, ensuring consistency across web properties while allowing for innovation and scalability
  • Translate research and user insights into user-friendly designs, using wireframes, prototypes, and design specifications to communicate ideas effectively
  • Iterate on designs based on user feedback, analytics data, and A/B testing results to continuously improve the user experience
  • Incorporate accessibility best practices (e.g., WCAG 2.1 compliance) into all web designs to ensure usability for diverse audiences
  • Create visual elements for multimedia content, such as infographics, animations, and digital illustrations, that reinforce Teleport’s brand identity
  • Stay informed about the latest web design trends, tools, and technologies, bringing fresh ideas to keep Teleport’s web presence innovative and impactful

​

  • Who You Are:Autonomous: You’re a self-starter who takes initiative, sees problems as opportunities, and knows how to execute
  • Creative and Strategic: You’re not just about aesthetics—you design with purpose, creating experiences that delight and drive results
  • Detail-Oriented: You have a sharp eye for pixel-perfect precision and the patience to iterate until it’s just right
  • Tech-Savvy: You know how to balance design and performance, understanding how designs translate into code and impact SEO, loading speeds, and responsiveness
  • Collaborative: You thrive in cross-functional teams, gathering input and delivering designs that meet shared goals

​

  • What You’ll Bring:5+ years of professional web design experience, with a strong portfolio showcasing responsive, user-centric designs for B2B SaaS or technology-focused companies
  • Advanced skills in design tools like Figma, Adobe Creative Suite, with a solid understanding of HTML, CSS, and their impact on web design and development workflows. Familiarity with CMS platforms (e.g., Sanity, Strapi, WordPress, Adobe Experience Manager) is a plus
  • A deep understanding of design principles, including typography, layout, color theory, and branding, with the ability to translate complex ideas into visually compelling and functional designs
  • Knowledge of WCAG 2.1 or similar standards, ensuring your designs meet accessibility and usability requirements for diverse audiences
  • Experience leveraging user feedback, A/B testing, and analytics to refine designs and improve performance
  • Strong ability to work cross-functionally with developers, marketers, and product teams, communicating design concepts effectively and ensuring seamless implementation
  • Resourcefulness and adaptability to solve design challenges in a fast-paced, agile environment
  • A passion for staying current with web design trends, tools, and techniques, with a commitment to ongoing learning and professional development

​

​

The Benefits

​

While many companies flaunt their benefits and perks to convince you to join their company, we believe your career is more important than that. That’s why we focus on making your day-to-day the best it can be while empowering you to achieve your goals and aspirations. 

​

What does that mean?

​

- It means you’ll have the autonomy to make your own decisions and focus on what’s important to you and your role.

- It means having access to a senior team that supports you and wants to see you succeed. 

- You’ll have a smart team you can learn from, collaborate with, and grow with.

- It means being able to make an impact and have a voice in the future of the feature, product or company direction. We don’t just leave you to focus on a niche. We allow you to spread your wings and take advantage of opportunities, challenging projects and exciting problems.

- It means removing the bureaucracy and red tape that stifles innovation while giving you access to all the information you need to build and take action more quickly. 

- It means allowing you to have the career you’ve always wanted today, rather than having to “earn your stripes” and wait for the right moment. If you’re good enough, you’re good enough.

​

But we don’t stop there. 

​

In addition to the career opportunities at Teleport, we offer a whole range of benefits that help you to maximize your future, including:

​

- Extensive health coverage

- Annual expense budget

- Rest & recovery policies that maximize leave and your ability to recharge

- Investment in your future with retirement savings plans

- Equity in a US $1.1-bn business

- Professional development opportunities

​

Do you have what it takes?

​

Get to use (and know) Teleport through our unique interview process 

​

At Teleport, we do things a bit differently. And when we say we only hire top talent, we actually mean it. Because of this, our interview process is different too - and we’re proud of it. We let your work do the talking. We don’t go in for six rounds of interviews, live whiteboard or live-coding. We don’t hire people that can talk a good game. We only want the best. And for that, we need to see what you can do, in your own time, in your own way. For real.

​

But interviews are a two-way street. Through the project, you’ll get a real taste of life at Teleport, including: 

​

- We’re flexible - you’ll have plenty of time to complete your project, if life gets in the way, that’s ok. We can - work around you. 

- We give you autonomy - you’ll have the space and freedom to figure things out, make decisions, and problem-solve.

- We’re collaborative - Got questions? 

- We have answers. You’ll have support from your team - the one you’ll be working with day in day out if you’re successful in getting the role.

- We progress careers - During the project, see how much you learn. That’s what working at Teleport is like. We like learning, on the job. All-the-time. 

- We can move fast (if you can) - two weeks sound like a long time? No problem? Early submissions are fine, and we’ll keep the process moving. 

​

Think you’ve got what it takes? We’d love to see it! Unconvinced? We can guarantee three things: 

- It’ll challenge you.

- You'll learn a lot. 

- If you love the process, you'll love working at Teleport.

​

Teleport is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate against any employee or applicant on the basis of age, color, disability, gender, national origin, race, religion, sexual orientation, veteran status, or any classifications protected by federal, state, or local law.

​

Candidate Privacy Notice: For information about our collection and processing of job applicant personal data for this position, please see our Job Applicant Privacy Policy and Notice of Collection at https://goteleport.com/legal/apply/job-applicant/

​

​

The base salary range for new hires in this role is between $100,000 - $150,000 for a level 3. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. The range displayed on this job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hire salaries for the position across all US locations. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your preferred location during the hiring process.

title: Web Designer UI/UX Intern - Master’s Level Internship
jobDescription:
ob Title: Web Designer UI/UX Intern - Master’s Level Internship

Level: Master’s Level Internship

Department: Product - UI/UX

Duration: 520 Hours (approximately 3 months)

Work location: Remote (with hybrid options in Toronto)

Compensation: Hourly ($32)

About Us: At Vosyn, we embrace the exciting, game-changing world of Artificial Intelligence, driving innovation and pioneering impactful projects across various industries. We are a trailblazing Language Synthesis AI firm reshaping global communication by dissolving language barriers and empowering users. We believe in fostering a culture of flexibility, continuous improvement, and solution-focused strategies. Here, every idea is welcomed, nurtured, and has the potential to scale to new heights. Currently, we're at the forefront of a significant IPO endeavor, truly a unicorn in the making. We invite you to be part of our journey and leave your imprint on the future of AI.

About the Role: We are seeking Web Design UI/UX Interns to contribute to an innovative project in Artificial Intelligence (AI). If you're passionate about AI and web design, join our dynamic team to create and enhance exceptional user interfaces for our platforms. This immersive internship offers hands-on experience, collaboration with senior management, and the opportunity to work in a fast-paced, high-growth environment. While demanding, it promises to be a rewarding complement to your academic journey, equipping you with valuable skills and insights.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Contribute to the design and development of our product user interfaces
  • Collaborate with our software and AI teams to create intuitive, user-centric designs Participate in user testing and feedback sessions to refine our designs
  • Assist in the analysis and incorporation of user behaviour data into our UI/UX design Support troubleshooting and resolution of issues during the design and testing phases Contribute to the documentation of design processes and findings.

About You:

  • A completed degree in Design and User Experience, Computer Science, Digital Media, or a related field required. Master’s program enrollment or completion is preferred but not mandatory
  • Strong design skills with a portfolio showcasing your talent for UI/UX.
  • Deep understanding of design principles and a keen eye for aesthetics.
  • Excellent problem-solving abilities.
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills.
  • Passion for innovation and pushing the boundaries in design.
  • Previous experience in web design is a plus, but not required.
  • Ability to work both independently and collaboratively.

Why Join Us:

  • Be part of the invigorating journey of a start-up transitioning to an IPO.
  • Engage directly with senior management and strategic advisory board members.
  • Enhance your CV with a rich array of experiences unique to Vosyn and AI Venture Lab.
  • Gain hands-on experience contributing to projects that drive growth and make a lasting impact in a fast-paced startup environment.

DEI and Workplace Safety:

At Vosyn Inc., we are committed to fostering a diverse, equitable, and inclusive workplace where every employee feels valued and supported. We believe that diversity of thought, background, and experience enriches our company culture and enhances innovation. We are an equal-opportunity employer and encourage candidates from all walks of life to apply. As part of our commitment to creating a safe and healthy work environment, we prioritize workplace safety, adhering to all relevant regulations and promoting a culture of responsibility. We believe that a safe and inclusive workplace is essential for the well-being and success of our team members. Join us in building a workplace that values diversity, prioritizes equity, and ensures the safety and well-being of every individual.

Recruitment Process:

  • Application Submission: Candidates are invited to submit their resumes and cover letters through our career portal.
  • Written Questionnaire: Selected candidates will receive a set of 10 written questions to assess their fit and technical knowledge..
  • Video Questionnaire: Candidates who successfully pass the written assessment will be asked to answer 10 additional questions to be submitted in a video format to further evaluate their skills and cultural fit. The ability to video record your answers will be required.
  • Evaluation: Our team will review the responses to both questionnaires. Candidates who meet our criteria will be invited to the next stage.
  • Orientation Session: Successful candidates will be invited to participate in an orientation session where they will learn more about Vosyn, our projects, and what to expect during the internship. After this session, you will be given the opportunity to opt-in if you believe that this internship is for you.

At Vosyn, we hire on a rolling basis, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible. While we operate with flexibility, we also cater to academic semester work terms to align with school schedules, ensuring a smooth transition for students joining us.

Be a part of a fast-growing global organization that values diversity of thought, experience, and culture. Our interns come from top universities worldwide, and we invite you to contribute, learn, and grow with us on this exciting journey.

Please note that only candidates who apply through our website will be considered for the role.

Apply Now:

Vosyn Careers

Job Types: Full-time, Part-time, Internship / Co-op

Contract length: 3-6 months

Pay: $32.00-$35.00 per hour

Flexible language requirement:

  • French not required

Work Location: Remote

title: Webflow Designer
jobDescription:
Full job description

We’re ZGM. We are the largest, full-service agency in Alberta with over 100 employees based in Calgary and Edmonton. We’ve won both national and international awards. So, what does all this mean for you? It means we have depth. It means we have a great deal of talent and experience, and it means we are a great employer. ZGMers are curious; they aren’t afraid to speak their mind, and they come to work every day ready for the next challenge. We like to think we make a difference by delivering experiences and results for brands that value imagination and innovation - and we have a lot of fun doing it. Still not sure - check out our employee handbook here http://zgm.ca/careers/

​

​

Webflow Designer

​

ZGM is seeking to grow our list of qualified Webflow designers. Our team values curiosity, a passion for bringing exciting designs to life and an appreciation for the detail-oriented craft of Webflow site designs and execution from start to finish. If you have a strong background in Webflow, with a solid understanding and appreciation of web design principles, responsive design, and implementation, we would love for you to join our team in a consistent, fixed hour capacity. This position will be a part-time contract, with flexibility in hours and schedule. On average, to start, we are looking for someone to work roughly 20 hours per month with the possibility of increased hours throughout the duration of the contract. The successful candidate will be required to invoice ZGM on a bi-weekly or monthly basis for their hours. This role can be either in office or remote.

​

​

Requirements

​

Two + years experience designing and building responsive websites using Webflow

​

Able to design and build sites applying a client-first framework

​

Proficient in utilizing and interpreting Figma designs

​

An eye for Interaction & Motion design

​

​

Nice to haves

​

Knowledge in utilizing Relume components

​

WordPress experience

​

GSAP experience

​

​

If you have the experience and skills identified, you might just be perfect for us! Assuming of course you're also a team-player who is passionate about enhancing human experiences, staying curious, being generous, taking risks, and swinging for the fences. Sound interesting? Send us your resume and cover letter and tell us why you’d be a great fit for ZGM.

​

​

ZGM’S COMMITMENT

​

We at ZGM are committed to ensuring that all people, across age, race, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, faith, and physical abilities, feel welcome and are represented in our decision making. We promise our staff, clients, and communities that we will never stop learning, sharing, and working toward becoming a more equitable organization.

title: Webflow Designer
jobDescription:
Join our team as our next Webflow Designer! Machobear Studios is growing and we are always looking for talented individuals with a strong background in crafting intuitive solutions for web platforms and applications to join our expanding team. We are looking for someone to design and develop visually appealing and functional websites using the Webflow platform, for a 6-month contract. You’ll do well in this position if you are a problem solver, adaptable, thrive in a fast-paced environment and have strong communication skills.

Who Are We?

Machobear Studios is an established tech start-up based in Vancouver. We are the creators of liv.rent, an all-in-one platform that connects tenants with landlords. Our mission is to build a trusted rental community and eliminate the stress and uncertainty of renting.​

What You’ll Be Doing:

  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure alignment on product goals and deliverables
  • Assist with the design and maintenance of design systems to ensure consistency across all products
  • Design and develop responsive websites using the Webflow platform
  • Translate design mockups and wireframes into fully functional websites
  • Create custom interactions, animations, and dynamic elements
  • Ensure websites are optimized for performance, SEO, and accessibility
  • Maintain and update existing websites, ensuring they remain current and functional
  • Stay up-to-date with Webflow updates, web design trends, and best practices
  • Provide technical support and troubleshooting for website issues as needed
  • Communicate with stakeholders on product design development
  • Develop visually compelling designs that align with the brand's identity and enhance the overall user experience
  • Address design challenges with intuitive solutions that balance user needs, technical limitations and business goals

What You Bring Us:

  • 2-3 years as a Webflow Designer or similar role
  • Proficiency in Figma or similar design tools such as Sketch or Adobe XD
  • Strong understanding of HTML, CSS, JavaScript and responsive design principles
  • Experience with a Webflow Framework (such as Finsweet, Lumos or Mast)
  • Experience with creating animations and interactions in Webflow, Framer or similar tools
  • Ability to iterate designs and solutions efficiently, with strong attention to detail and eye for design
  • Demonstrated experience in taking ownership of website projects from conception to development and beyond
  • Familiarity in implementing web accessibility standards and following WCAG guidelines
  • Ability to organize website content and navigation that enhances usability (“Information Architecture”)
  • Experience with responsive design and ensuring that websites adapt to different devices
  • Deep understanding of design principles and best practices (Colour theory, typography, etc)
  • Understanding user needs, behaviours and preferences
  • Ability to analyze and interpret data from user testing and various research methods
  • Experience in developing and maintaining design systems

Bonus:

  • Experience with website animations, interaction design, GSAP or Lottie files
  • Familiarity with A/B testing to improve conversions and engagement

What To Expect:

  • Join a passionate and creative team that learns, collaborates, and succeeds together
  • Refuel with great coffee, lots of snacks, and on Friday's beer on tap
  • Work in a bright and spacious office space along the Vancouver Seawall
  • Bring your dog to work – We welcome all four-legged friends in our office
  • Transit-accessible location, with plenty of restaurants and cafés nearby
  • We like to have fun! Join us for fun events, lunches, and patio time

Think you have what it takes? We’d love to hear from you. Apply with your resume and link to your online portfolio.​

Please note, only qualified candidates will be contacted.

title: Webflow Developer
jobDescription:
Notable is the leading intelligent automation company for healthcare. Customers use Notable to drive patient acquisition, retention, and reimbursement, scaling growth without hiring more staff. We don’t just make software.

​

We are on a mission to fix the broken U.S. healthcare system by helping to eliminate the massive administrative burden that is placed on our nation’s healthcare staff. We hire people from diverse backgrounds and are always looking for employees who bring fresh ideas to our space. Passion is paramount, and at Notable, you will get to work with other talented people who aim to set a new standard for innovation in healthcare.

​

Role Summary

We are seeking a web developer with deep Webflow expertise (in addition to other technologies) to join our internal Brand Design team to expand, uplevel and accelerate our web and digital presence. You will be instrumental in the transformation of our current brochuresite to a multi-faceted destination to serve our growing customer base, enabling our internal teams to build and deploy dynamic content through a well-crafted, easy to use CMS. The ideal candidate is comfortable at the intersection of design and development, passionate about bringing concepts to life through innovative solutions, meticulous in craft, and committed to problem-solving and shipping with urgency.

​

What You’ll Do

Develop and manage Notable’s main Webflow instance (and additional web properties as needed: e.g. event landing pages, product or marketing microsites) ensuring robust site architecture, ABM infrastructure, analytics, and CMS ease-of-use, among other technical stewardship.

Partner closely with Marketing, Design and other functional areas to translate visual or content concepts to functional, intuitive, beautiful web experiences.

Proactively and meticulously triage priorities across new initiatives and ongoing maintenance and improvements.

Own the reliability and/or any urgent/ad-hoc requirements as may be needed by the business (as well as manage redundant coverage as needed)

Build or advise on other web/digital initiatives that may require technical knowledge (e.g. emails, webinars)

What We’re Looking For

Ideally 5+ years of experience in-house working alongside small, nimble teams with a desire to unblock and creatively problem solve.

Strong preference for a design/developer hybrid who can help push the envelope as to what’s possible, and partner alongside other brand designers to realize a shared vision. (Minimally, fluent in principles of good UX and visual design with the ability to read between the lines to execute a vision without perfect instruction/handoff).

Strong collaboration and project management skills, with the ability to work across departments and manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.

Beware of job scam fraudsters! Our recruiters use @notablehealth.com email addresses exclusively. We do not conduct interviews via text or instant message and we do not ask candidates to download software other than Zoom, to purchase equipment through us, or to provide sensitive personally identifiable information such as bank account or social security numbers. If you have been contacted by someone claiming to be me from a different domain about a job offer, please report it as potential job fraud to law enforcement and contact us here.

​

Compensation Range: $104K - $145K

title: Front-End Webflow Developer

title: Growth Marketing Manager - Age Precisely
jobDescription:
Join us as a Growth Marketing Manager at AgePrecisely™, where our mission is to empower men to regain their vitality and confidence through clinically-backed ED treatments. As part of our team, you will optimize our full-funnel digital strategy and elevate our brand's presence in the men's health and wellness sector.

​

What You’ll Do:

  • Launch Paid Campaigns: Own and optimize Google Ads, Facebook, YouTube, and Instagram campaigns targeting high-intent B2C audiences.
  • Build Funnels: Create compelling landing pages and booking funnels to maximize conversion rates.
  • Write Emotional Copy: Craft empathetic messaging that speaks directly to the psychology of men dealing with ED.
  • Measure Everything: Implement pixel tracking, GA4, and conversion dashboards; conduct structured testing to enhance performance.
  • Own Marketing Automation: Develop nurture flows (email/SMS) using tools like ActiveCampaign, HubSpot, or Klaviyo.
  • Partner with Vendors: Manage creative, development, or CRO freelancers as required.
  • Launch Local SEO Strategy: Optimize Google Business and review platforms to dominate local searches.
  • Report Results: Deliver detailed dashboards and metrics to leadership on a weekly and monthly basis.

​

You’ll Thrive If You Have:

  • 3–7 years of experience in performance marketing or growth for a DTC brand.
  • Strong track record managing Google Ads and Meta Ads campaigns.
  • Proven ability to manage advertising budgets of $30K–$100K/month.
  • Experience in building funnels tailored for high-ticket B2C healthcare or wellness products.
  • Solid knowledge of copywriting, split testing, and conversion rate optimization (CRO).
  • Proficiency in tools like GA4, Looker Studio, or Supermetrics.
  • A growth mindset characterized by grit, curiosity, and a bias toward action.

​

Bonus Points:

  • Background in men’s health, longevity, med spa, or sexual wellness.
  • Familiarity with HIPAA-compliant marketing practices.
  • Basic development skills in Webflow, Unbounce, or ClickFunnels.

​

Why Join Us?

  • Be part of a purpose-driven team building something meaningful.
  • Take ownership of a growth engine from inception.
  • Influence messaging, strategy, and product in a high-impact vertical.
  • Work alongside executive leaders who value autonomy and excellence.
  • Enjoy a performance-based culture where results matter.

​

Ready to reclaim results with us?

Submit your resume, portfolio of winning campaigns (if available), and a brief note explaining why this mission resonates with you to Michelle@HealPrecisely.com.

title: Graphic Designer
jobDescription:
Full job description

**This is a remote position. We are currently searching for office space in the GTA to move into a hybrid model, however, we will always offer fully remote, as we have employees all over Canada!

​

If you love creating social media graphics across platforms, editing and stylizing video content for Reels and TikTok, designing visually appealing and intuitive websites layouts, adjusting on-white and flat lay RAW photography asset files, developing stop motion graphics, and enjoy doing it all in sweatpants in front of the TV while Friends or The Office is on in the background and hanging out with some cool people, then we have the position for you!

​

We're hiring a full-time Graphic Designer to work under our Design & Development Lead and with our cross-functional teams in developing creative assets across digital touchpoints for various industry verticals.

​

Founded in 2015, we're a 20+ person, Shopify Expert digital marketing agency based in Thornhill (and operating remotely) with team members across Canada! We do everything related to digital marketing including, but not limited to: organic and paid social media, Google ads, email marketing and automation, website design and development, online customer service, and everything in between for clients across the world in industries verticals ranging from fashion, food and beverage, cannabis, skincare, real estate, health and wellness, restaurants, and fitness, selling products used by celebrities and featured in Vogue, GOOP, on Shark Tank, and we even have a client who hosted the White House Correspondents Dinner!

​

Work with us and you would be:

​

Designing Websites & Landing Pages: working with the development team in creating visually appealing, accessible, intuitive, and converting websites across mobile and desktop placements (using Figma) within the Shopify, Webflow, and WordPress platforms, as well we performing design review to ensure consistency between mockup and live builds (Development experience a big plus!)

Designing Social Media Graphics: working alongside our social media team to create on-brand, engaging static and animated graphics that align with corresponding copy and underlying strategy to be used across social platform

Designing Advertising Creative: working with our digital advertising team to create high converting and strategic static, motion, and video assets across Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, and other advertising ecosystems

Designing Print & Product Collateral: as requested (infrequently), designing print collateral such as business cards, banners, flyers, patterns, merchandise, or other physical objects that align with clients' existing digital brand identity in accordance with relevant printing requirements

Supporting Email Design: supporting the email team in designing email templates during a resource or deadline crunch

Providing Strategic Insight: lending your knowledge of design and design standards, as well as ideas and feedback to team members to ensure we are providing the highest quality deliverables possible

Owning Brand Identity & Quality: ensuring all assets align with client brand guidelines, as well as In Social's high standard of deliverable quality

To work with us you should:

​

Know the Adobe Suite inside and out (Photoshop, Illustrator, Premier, After Effects, Figma)

Have worked in some web design or web adjacent role

Be able to show examples of your work across both digital and print

Have a working knowledge of all digital touchpoints

Have a working knowledge of Ecommerce (being a big online shopper counts!)

Be a genuine person (sorry, no bullsh*t here!)

Understand UI/UX and accessibility best practices and principals

Be a team player, willing to help when needed to get the job done

Have exceptional comprehension skills

Pay exceptional attention to detail and include isgd2024 in your cover letter

Be a strategic thinker who knows how to problem solve

Gets along well with others and enjoys memes, gifs, and dad jokes

Appreciate a super casual work environment, but still knows how to be productive

When you work with us, you get:

​

A comfortable work environment, being remote (forever) with a potential hybrid model addition. Before COVID, we legit all worked together in sweatpants with no makeup.

A team of friends, but who push and support each other to do and be their best

Weekly stretch and game time (you won’t be able to beat our boss at Mario Kart Tour, trust us)

Events like summer retreats in Muskoka, winter holiday parties, murder mystery party nights, wood painting events, and whatever else you can think of!

Health spending account (benefits)

Free swag (we have a store and a rewards program for said store: shop.insocial.ca)

Additional opportunities for bonuses based on trackable performance

Opportunity for growth - we're one of Shopify's fastest growing agency partners. We have gone from 7 team members to 20 in 2 years, with many having been promoted 2x since then!

title: Social Media and Marketing Specialist/Admin
jobDescription:
We are looking for a dynamic and creative Social Media and Marketing Specialist to manage our digital presence and execute marketing campaigns that drive engagement and growth. The ideal candidate is passionate about storytelling, digital trends, and brand building.

Key Responsibilities

Social Media Management

aboutCompany: Fella & Delilah is the direct-to-patient clinic for busy families in Middle America to get to a healthier weight. Fella is optimized for busy bigger guys who want a no-nonsense solution to getting healthier. Delilah is built for strong women in their prime who want a sustainable solution to feel their best. We use affordable, effective weight loss medication to solve our customers’ most pressing healthcare need, then aim to remain their core healthcare provider for the rest of their life — expanding to hormonal health, mental health, longevity, and primary care. GLP-1 medications are a f*ing big deal. We’re in a remarkable & rapid period of change which will radically reshape the patient & cash flows in the healthcare system. Our mission is to accelerate the global take-up of GLP-1 medications among busy families to help solve obesity worldwide. From this, we’ll become the world’s leading consumer healthcare brand. We’ve grown >1000x since Aug 2021, >200x since Aug 2022, >20x since Aug 2023. We’ve just surpassed $70M ARR. As a company, we’ve been cashflow positive for the last 21mo. We’re fast approaching 21,000 active subscribers with high NPS. Almost all of this has so far been through Fella. Delilah is still very new and your job is to make her soar. title: Web Specialist
jobDescription:
Avenue Living Asset Management is dedicated to sourcing, analyzing, and strategizing long-term investments. Our unique approach has led to rapid growth across North America now representing one of Canada’s largest alternative asset managers. Since 2006, we have acquired over $7 billion in assets under management across a variety of sectors throughout Canada and the USA. Our various funds include assets in multifamily residential, self-storage, commercial, as well as agricultural land.

The strength of our platform lies in working with the right people. Acting with integrity and employing an entrepreneurial mindset, our employees work together to create an exciting, dynamic workplace. By employing an active hands-on approach to asset management, our skilled and innovative team operates closely alongside our investors. We’re disciplined, we focus on value, and we’re informed experts. We pride ourselves on our diverse and inclusive workplace; we are a unique workplace with unique people.

We are seeking a Web Specialist to join the team at Alberta Boot Company, one of Avenue Living's private equity companies. At Alberta Boot Company, we are driven by a singular purpose: to create superior products that honour tradition, foster innovation, and embody the spirit of progress while celebrating the Western spirit. We take pride in crafting products that pay homage to the trailblazers who have forged the path before us, while also inspiring and equipping the next generation to chart new frontiers.

About The Role:

  • Design visually intuitive, engaging user-friendly interfaces for websites and web applications.
  • Enhance user interfaces based on research into customer behavior and preferences.
  • Apply on-page SEO best practices by optimizing meta tags (title tags, meta descriptions), header tags, image alt texts, and URL structures.
  • Conduct keyword research to identify high-value keywords and incorporate them into the content strategy.
  • Oversee and implement website content related to products, promotions, and campaigns, aligning with marketing initiatives.
  • Optimize and refresh blog posts and web pages to improve SEO performance.
  • Update landing pages and product setups in line with marketing initiatives and promotions.
  • Utilize tools such as Google Analytics to track key UI/UX and SEO metrics.

Qualifications:

  • 1-2 years experience as a Web Specialist
  • Proficient in using Shopify for e-commerce management.
  • Experienced with web analytics tools, including Google Analytics.
  • Skilled in Microsoft 365 Suite.
  • Familiar with key web analytics metrics and how to interpret them.
  • Strong understanding of SEO principles and best practices.
  • Knowledge of A/B testing and other UX research methods is a plus.
  • Bachelor’s degree in Web Design, Digital Marketing, or a related field.

​

What makes us different makes us stronger. We want Avenue Living to be a great place to work for everyone; where people feel safe, included, inspired, trusted, and supported so they can be, and bring, their best. We're committed to creating a respectful and inclusive workplace where unique perspectives, experiences, backgrounds, cultures, and attributes are valued and utilized to help achieve better results. As an organization with operations across different geographies, we celebrate the fact that our workforce is made up of people with diverse demographics. We are committed to ensuring our workplace is fair, equitable, and free of discrimination and harassment. Should you require accommodation to participate fully in the recruitment process, please email peopleandculture@avenueliving.ca.

title: Webflow Designer
jobDescription:
Geek Powered Studios is an Austin-based, full-service digital marketing agency, and we’re looking for a Web Designer to join our team! We design, build, and service in-house, so you'll work on a variety of projects—from full site builds and landing pages to banner ads, newsletters, and social ad creatives.

This role starts as part-time with the potential to go full-time based on performance and business needs. You'll need to be strong in your design skills, comfortable with development, graphic design, and video editing, and an expert at Webflow with the ability to flex to WordPress and Shopify when needed.

We work collaboratively on most projects, so being a team player is a must—but you should also be able to work independently and meet deadlines without constant oversight.

What You’ll Do:

  • Design and develop custom Webflow sites with modern UX/UI best practices.
  • Work on Shopify and WordPress projects, including theme customization, site optimization, and functionality enhancements.
  • Build landing pages, website components, banner ads, and email templates.
  • Create high-quality social media ads and promotional videos for campaigns.
  • Ensure responsive design, cross-browser compatibility, and site performance optimization.
  • Collaborate with our SEO, development, and marketing teams to create high-performing websites and digital assets.
  • Stay up-to-date on design trends, UX/UI best practices, and CRO strategies.

What We’re Looking For:

  • Strong portfolio with at least 4 relevant projects (websites, ads, and video work).
  • Expert-level skill in Webflow with WordPress and Shopify capabilities
  • Experience with Figma, Adobe Creative Suite and other design tools.
  • Intermediate to advanced understanding of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
  • Experience creating social media ad graphics and short-form marketing videos.
  • A solid grasp of typography, layout, and design principles.
  • Ability to manage deadlines, work independently, and collaborate with multiple stakeholders.
  • Experience with CRO (conversion rate optimization) best practices.
  • Agency experience preferred.
  • Must be physically located in the U.S. and able to work as a W2 employee (no sponsorship available).

What’s In It For You?

  • As a part-time Geek you are eligible for:
  • 100% remote work
  • Flexible schedule after 90 days
  • Opportunities for professional development & team building
  • Medical, retirement and other benefits are available should the role become full-time.

Sound like you? Apply now and send us your portfolio with strong web design, ad, and video examples—we want to see what you can do!

title: Webflow Designer & Developer (Remote - United Kingdom)
jobDescription:
Guidebook is a simple yet powerful event management software that lets you promote your event, manage registration and create an app in four easy steps with no technical skills required.

Thousands of organizations across the globe use Guidebook to create mobile applications for events, university campus open houses, venue guides, onboarding for associations, corporate training and so much more. These apps help people stay organized, connect with their peers, and access more information - but it doesn’t stop there.

Our mission is to support the people who bring people together - event organizers. Events have never felt more important in building connections and helping shape and change minds in today’s fractured world. We are a driven team on a mission to elevate event experiences for everyone – and believe we can accomplish that while highly valuing balance, togetherness, and fun!

ABOUT THE JOB:

You’re a hybrid thinker—a digital designer with a marketer’s mindset. You know how to make websites not just look good, but perform well. With a deep understanding of Webflow and a knack for intuitive user experience, you can confidently manage and optimize a site like guidebook.com, bringing both creative flair and strategic insight to the table. You’re equally at home collaborating with marketers, salespeople, and developers, and you’re excited to see your design choices translate into measurable business results.

In this role, you’ll take ownership of Guidebook’s web presence—rebuilding and maintaining our site in Webflow, supporting our martech integrations, and contributing to brand-aligned marketing design projects. You will play a central role in executing and iterating on digital strategies that drive awareness, conversions, and engagement.

Our ideal candidate blends strong visual design skills with technical chops. You understand how SEO, user experience, and design intersect—and you’re always looking for ways to improve site performance, conversion rates, and brand consistency. Experience in a fast-paced, collaborative B2B or SaaS environment is a big plus, especially if you’ve juggled multiple marketing initiatives at once.

WHAT YOU'LL DO:

  • Own and manage the Guidebook marketing website in Webflow, initially leading on a re-design and re-development project and then making updates and improvements as needed
  • Collaborate with marketers to build landing pages, optimize user flows, and support campaign launches
  • Apply technical SEO best practices to ensure discoverability and performance across pages
  • Manage the Guidebook Blog on WordPress
  • Use your HTML, CSS and Javascript skills to help manage our WordPress blog, and support important integrations
  • Proactively suggest and implement website improvements based on data, benchmarks, and user experience trends
  • Design visually compelling digital assets and maintain brand consistency across web and marketing materials
  • Contribute to the development of social and paid campaign assets where needed
  • Help connect web performance to marketing KPIs, including traffic, conversion rates, and SEO rankings
  • Manage projects alongside the marketing team within a structured project management process using two-week sprints in Jira
  • Bring a creative and analytical lens to problem-solving—balancing great design with measurable results

THE IDEAL CANDIDATE:

  • 3+ years of digital or marketing design experience, with a strong portfolio of Webflow design and development projects.
  • Experience building and implementing design systems in Webflow.
  • Demonstrated experience managing and optimizing a B2B marketing website
  • Proficient with HTML, CSS and Javascript - supporting our WordPress blog, and integrations with marketing platforms like Marketo and Chili Piper
  • Familiar with technical SEO, including on-page optimization, structure, and site health
  • Strong design sensibility and the ability to articulate a clear point of view based on data and brand goals
  • Experience analyzing site performance and translating insights into design and UX improvements
  • Collaborative and communicative—you enjoy working cross-functionally with marketers, sales, and leadership
  • Analytical yet creative—you’re eager to A/B test and refine assets to improve performance
  • Experience producing work through project management tools such as Jira, Asana, Monday, etc
  • Is able to work effectively with a manager based in a remote office
  • Bonus: Experience in a smaller B2B and/or SaaS company where you owned multiple design/development functions
  • Bonus: Experience with Google Tag Manager

WITHIN 3 MONTHS, YOU’LL:

  • Gain a solid understanding of Guidebook’s product, brand, customers, and website structure
  • Conduct a full audit of the website with recommendations for design, UX, and SEO improvements
  • Establish relationships across marketing, sales, and product teams
  • Begin contributing to new landing pages and website updates using Webflow
  • Participate in sprint planning and align your work within the team’s 2-week cadence
  • Understand the Guidebook tone and visual identity—and begin forming ideas for evolution
  • Identify low-hanging fruit to improve conversion rates and technical performance

WITHIN 6 MONTHS, YOU’LL:

  • Take full ownership of Guidebook’s Webflow site, managing updates and improvements with autonomy - including updates to our martech integrations
  • Take full ownership of Guidebook’s blog on WordPress, managing updates and improvements with autonomy
  • Regularly analyze and report on website performance—setting clear goals and benchmarks
  • Design and launch new campaign pages and test variations to improve engagement and lead gen
  • Partner with demand gen to create high-converting landing pages and performance-focused assets
  • Contribute to improving project workflows and creating reusable design components in Webflow

WITHIN 12 MONTHS, YOU’LL:

  • Establish a clear connection between your work and core marketing KPIs (e.g. SEO, conversion rates, engagement)
  • Standardize best practices for Webflow development, website QA, and marketing design operations
  • Build scalable systems to support growth—templates, automation, atoms/molecules
  • Play a leadership role in maintaining and evolving the Guidebook brand voice and visual identity
  • Drive measurable improvements in performance through data-informed design decisions
  • Be seen as the go-to resource for high-quality, performance-driven design within the marketing team

PERKS

  • Unlimited vacation time
  • Stock options
  • Make your workstation yours: desk customization (monitor; docks/stands; keyboards; whatever else you need to be productive)
  • Awesome annual company retreats!

EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER:

At Guidebook, we're committed to cultivating an inclusive work environment for everyone – and we know that's best achieved, in part, with a diverse workforce. We're always working on increasing diversity across the company. If you're looking to join a team that is smart, highly motivated, and also values work/life balance, we can't wait to hear how you can add to our growing culture!

We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, national origin, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any other federal, state or local protected class.

COMPENSATION:

The salary range for this role is £60,000 to 75,000 annually (including variable quarterly bonuses), depending on relevant experience.

title: Webflow Developer
jobDescription:
Juice is an innovative creative agency that is passionate about creating innovative and memorable experiences online. We're on a mission to revolutionize user experiences through intuitive and responsive web interfaces. Join our talented team of developers, designers, and producers to help make wonderful things.

Position Overview:

We’re seeking a Webflow Developer (not a designer) based in United States, Latin America or Central America to join our team on a part-time contract basis. This is a technical role — you’ll be responsible for building, managing, and maintaining Webflow websites with clean structure, performance optimization, and technical best practices.

If you’re a developer who knows Webflow inside out — and you’re comfortable working independently within your time zone, we’d love to hear from you.

What You’ll Do

  • Build marketing websites in Webflow based on designs and project briefs (no design work required)
  • Manage and maintain existing Webflow websites, including content updates, improvements, and technical fixes
  • Implement Webflow CMS structures, dynamic filtering, and custom interactions
  • Optimize websites for performance, SEO, and accessibility
  • Collaborate with our team on implementation, QA testing, and site launches

Requirements

  • This is a developer role — we’re looking for a technical Webflow expert, not a designer
  • 2+ years of experience building responsive websites in Webflow
  • Strong understanding of Webflow CMS and dynamic content structures
  • Proficient in HTML, CSS, and basic JavaScript
  • Experience working with Client-First or similar Webflow styling frameworks
  • Experience with Finsweet Attributes or other advanced Webflow tools
  • Comfortable with custom code embeds and third-party integrations
  • Knowledge of technical SEO best practices (meta tags, page speed optimization, schema markup)
  • Basic understanding of Web Accessibility (WCAG standards)
  • Ability to follow Figma designs and deliver pixel-perfect builds
  • Strong attention to detail, reliable, and proactive communicator

Bonus Points

  • Familiarity with performance optimization techniques (lazy loading, minification, etc.)
  • Experience working with design systems or component libraries
  • CMS migration experience (WordPress to Webflow, etc.)

Availability

  • Part-time / Contract
  • Remote — US, Canada, LATAM candidates only
  • Estimated ~20 hours per week
  • Flexible working hours, but must be responsive and available for async communication and occasional calls

Why Work With Us

  • Competitive pay packages
  • Bonus eligibility
  • Work on exciting projects for growing brands
  • Remote-first and flexible environment
  • Join a team that values quality, clean development, and technical best practices
  • Opportunity for full-time position if it’s a good fit

Remote restrictions

  • Workday must overlap by at least 4 hours with Orlando, FL, USA
  • Must be a resident of Argentina, Brazil, Canada, Colombia, Mexico, Peru, or United States
