title: SEO Specialist
jobDescription:
At Intrepid Travel we exist to inspire, create, share and lead the best travel experiences ever – for both people and the planet. More than 34 years later, we’re proud to be a certified B Corp with hundreds of trips in over 100 countries.

​

The world needs more Intrepid people:

​

We are looking for a skilled SEO professional with a proven track record of driving organic traffic, improving search rankings, and enhancing brand visibility across search engines, including AI-driven platforms. This role offers an exciting opportunity to take the next step in an SEO career, working closely with the Head of SEO to strengthen the online presence of two growing adventure travel brands within a globally recognized responsible travel company.

​

The role will develop and execute the SEO strategy for Urban Adventures and Wildland Trekking, ensuring alignment with Intrepid Travel’s broader objectives. It will focus on improving and maintaining organic search rankings, increasing visibility, and driving traffic. Key responsibilities include monitoring, analyzing, and optimizing website performance to enhance technical SEO, on-page content, and user experience. Regular performance reporting, along with providing actionable insights and recommendations to stakeholders, will be essential. The role will also lead SEO projects that directly impact organic growth, such as site enhancements, content initiatives, and backlink strategies. Additionally, it will involve developing content strategies that prioritize user experience and optimize the customer journey across both websites.

​

This is a full-time, 8-month contract position based on-site at our Toronto, Ontario office, with the possibility of extension.

​

Some of the experience you’ll bring with you may include: 

​

  • 2+ years' experience in SEO specialist or SEO-related role/s 
  • Demonstrated expert knowledge in technical, on-page & off-page SEO elements 
  • Content writing proficiency is preferred, though not essential 
  • Experience working in-house 
  • High level proficiency in working with various CMS 
  • Strong time management skills with a proven ability to deliver on objectives, a collaborative approach to working with global teams, and strong attention to detail. 

You have extensive SEO experience and a strong grasp of current and past trends across various markets. You’re comfortable working across technical, on-page, and off-page SEO. Content writing is a key strength, and experience with multi-lingual or multi-national websites is a plus. While not essential, knowledge of paid SEM would be an advantage. You’re highly organized, manage your time effectively, and thrive in a collaborative environment.

​

Our people reflect the diversity of our customers and the communities we visit:

​

Intrepid is made up of 58 nationalities across 46 countries, and we recognise that diversity makes us a stronger business. We support a culture where people can be themselves and encourage applications from people of all backgrounds. Including First Nations People, LGBTQI+ people, those living with a disability, or from disadvantaged backgrounds, and all people from different ethnicities, races, and religions.

​

Rewarding and developing our people is important to us, some of our work perks include:

​

  •  Flexible work options, such as work-from-home options and the ability to 'work from anywhere' 4 weeks a year
  •  A minimum of 10 weeks paid parental leave for all parents, and other additional leave provisions
  •  Generous discounts on Intrepid trips for families, and close friends
  •  The opportunity to give back to causes important to you, with Volunteer leave opportunities and purpose activities
  •  Learning & Development programs, e-learning platforms, and professional development
  •  Access to an Employee Assistance Program, a 24/7 service that offers counselling for you and your family

We are Real. Together. Ambitious. Impactful. These are our values and to us they're more than just words, they are the pillars of our success and every day it's our people and the work they do that bring them to life.

​

At Intrepid ensuring diversity, equity, and inclusion is an important part of our recruitment process. If you require a reasonable accommodation to submit your application, please email careersupport@intrepidtravel.com with your name, location, and the accommodations you need, we will then be in touch to support your application.

title: Senior Content Writer
jobDescription:
Founded in 2001, York & Chapel is an international interactive marketing agency with offices in Connecticut, Philadelphia, Toronto, Vancouver, and Istanbul. We also work out of New York and San Francisco, where many of our clients are. We are designers, writers, and strategists who use today’s marketing technologies to build strong, vibrant communities around brands. As cultural technologists and marketers, we have many tools that are constantly changing and evolving. Our purpose is to offer them to each and every client in very customized and inspired ways.

Reporting to the Chief Marketing Officer, the Senior Content Writer will play a critical role in shaping brand messaging, content strategy, and editorial direction for clients. This role requires expertise in brand positioning, tone of voice development, and crafting content that aligns with strategic marketing goals.We are looking for candidates who can demonstrate confidence in presenting new ideas and help us win over clients, whether start-ups or Fortune 500 companies.

Y&C is a fast-paced environment. The candidate will be experienced in web copywriting, content audits, information architecture, social media strategy, proposal writing, and team leadership. Additionally, the Senior Content Writer will manage internal and external writers, contribute to business development, and support company-wide strategic planning.

Job Overview

As our Senior Content Writer, you will develop brand messaging, craft compelling content, and bring creative concepts to life across multiple platforms. You’ll work on everything from website copy and digital campaigns to brand storytelling, sales enablement, and advertising copy.Whether it's an attention-grabbing ad, an inspiring brand manifesto, or a persuasive case study, your words will build brand equity, engage audiences, and support business growth. If you love the challenge of writing for diverse brands and industries, have a knack for storytelling that stirs emotion and drives action, and can turn creative ideas into powerful messaging, this is the role for you.

Key Responsibilities Creative

Concepting & Brand Messaging

  • Develop creative concepts, key messaging, and brand storytelling that capture audience attention.
  • Craft brand positioning statements, taglines, and tone of voice guidelines for clients.
  • Write engaging campaign messaging, ad copy, and creative narratives across multiple channels.
  • Collaborate with designers, strategists, and creative teams to develop impactful marketing campaigns.
  • Ensure all brand messaging aligns with client objectives, audience insights, and business goals.

Content Strategy & Development

  • Develop and implement brand and content strategies that strengthen positioning and engagement.
  • Translate complex ideas into compelling, easy-to-digest content for different industries and audiences. This includes both B2C and B2B audiences.
  • Write content for websites, blogs, print, and broadcast media, email campaigns, marketing materials, and sales enablement tools.
  • Conduct content audits, information architecture planning, and web copywriting to enhance clarity and impact.
  • Optimize web content for SEO, readability, and engagement, including keyword research and meta descriptions.

Social Media & Editorial Content

  • Develop and execute content strategies for social media and digital platforms that drive engagement.
  • Write engaging, on-brand social media copy that aligns with audience preferences and industry trends.
  • Manage editorial content calendars, ensuring brand consistency and strategic messaging across platforms.

Sales Enablement

  • Write sales and marketing collateral that supports business development efforts and drives conversions.
  • Craft persuasive proposals, RFPs, and presentations that showcase agency expertise and success stories.
  • Develop case studies and thought leadership content that highlight the impact of our work.

Additional Responsibilities

  • Stay updated on industry trends, content marketing innovations, and SEO best practices.
  • Edit and proofread content to ensure accuracy, consistency, and brand alignment.
  • Manage multiple projects and meet deadlines in a fast-paced, agency-driven environment.

Requirements

  • 5+ years of experience in content writing, brand strategy, and content marketing (agency experience preferred).
  • Strong expertise in brand messaging, positioning, and tone of voice development.
  • Experience developing creative campaign messaging, ad copy, and high-impact storytelling.
  • Proven experience with website content strategy, content audits, and web copywriting.
  • Solid understanding of SEO best practices, content structure, and information architecture.
  • Experience developing social media strategies and editorial content.
  • Strong background in B2B sales enablement, proposal writing, RFPs, and case study development.
  • Ability to adapt writing style and messaging for different industries, audiences, and platforms.
  • Excellent storytelling, research, and editing skills with a strong portfolio of impactful work.
  • Highly organized, detail-oriented, and able to manage multiple projects in a fast-paced setting.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Possesses exceptional communication skills, written, verbal, and listening skills
  • Solid understanding of creative and production processes
  • Demonstrates confidence communicating with clients on day-to-day activities, presenting new ideas, or addressing objections
  • Must be a proactive problem solver with the ability to anticipate the needs of the client
  • Self-motivated and always take initiative
  • Must have great attention to detail, be organized and flexible to manage in an ever-changing environment
  • Able to speak about and address issues surrounding technologies the company is developing
  • Proficiency in Google Workspace and other project management and productivity tools
  • A relevant degree, diploma, or equivalent experience

About York & Chapel: How does technology live happily with big-brain brand strategy and dynamic shareable social content? How do one-of-a-kind experiences play nice with meticulous measurable results? At Y&C, we elegantly solve the Rubik’s cube of marketing challenges for some of the world’s most notable companies. That’s Technology Beautifully Articulated. York & Chapel is an agency with 23+ years of experience looking at the world through an ever-evolving technology filter – going beyond traditional website development, mobile applications, and microsites to a place few agencies tread. Join a team of talented designers, developers, writers, strategists, and account managers who are just as passionate as you are about bringing innovative and cutting-edge ideas to life. Some of our current clients include Fortune 500 companies in technology, entertainment, telecommunications, and luxury goods.

This is York & Chapel.

Specialties: Program and campaign strategy and development, enterprise UI/UX, branding, creative development, marketing, mobile and emerging platforms, social media, website design and implementation.

Job Types: Full-time, Permanent

Pay: $60,000.00-$75,000.00 per year

Benefits:

  • Company events
  • Dental care
  • Extended health care
  • Flexible schedule
  • Paid time off
  • RRSP match
  • Tuition reimbursement
  • Vision care
  • Wellness program
  • Work from home

Flexible language requirement:

  • French not required

Schedule:

  • Monday to Friday
  • Weekends as needed

Education:

  • Bachelor's Degree (required)

Experience:

  • Copywriting: 5 years (required)
  • Agency work: 5 years (required)

Language:

  • English (required)

Location:

  • Vancouver, BC V5X 3A2 (required)

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Vancouver, BC V5X 3A2

Application deadline: 2025-04-18

jobDescription:

title: Digital Marketeer and SEO Specialist
jobDescription:
Sigma Capital Partners (“SCP”) is a real-asset focus developer, financier, and operator active in the Canadian seniors housing, infrastructure, and project finance sectors. The company includes three main entities: Sencap Partners, Sigma Asset Management, and Arcavia.

Arcavia Care Services (“ACS”) is the growing home care services division of Arcavia (a developer and owner of long-term care homes). ACS operates across the GTA, providing compassionate and professional in-home care services, including nursing and personal support, to enhance the quality of life for seniors, enabling them to continue to live independently in their homes while they age – arcavia.ca.

Sencap Partners Inc. (“Sencap”) is an established but quickly growing boutique project finance firm specializing in financial advisory and capital raising for infrastructure, power, and seniors housing projects across Canada – sencappartners.ca.

The Digital Marketeer and SEO Specialist will play a key role in ensuring that marketing and web content is optimized for reach and engagement for both ACS and Sencap. The end goal is to set both SCP companies up for success with effective and timely communications and SEO best practices that allow for the delivery of high-impact and high-performing marketing content.

Roles and Responsibilities: SEO Optimization

  • Identifying relevant keywords and phrases to optimize websites for better search engine rankings.
  • Creating a strategy that includes identifying target audiences, understanding their search behaviors, and setting goals.
  • Improving Sencap and ACS websites’ content, including meta tags, headers, and images, to rank higher in search results.
  • Managing all SEO operations to increase a company's search rankings.
  • Analyzing competitors' SEO strategies and identifying opportunities for improvement.
  • Using web analytics dashboards to gain insights.

Roles and Responsibilities: Digital Marketing

· Conceptualizing, planning, and executing comprehensive digital marketing campaigns across various channels such as social media, email, search engines, and websites.

· Analyzing campaign performance metrics and key performance indicators (KPIs) such as website traffic, conversion rates, engagement metrics, and ROI, enabling data-driven decision-making and continuous optimization of marketing strategies.

· Leverage various digital channels to disseminate brand messaging, showcase product offerings, transaction and team announcements, and engage target audiences in meaningful ways, ultimately enhancing brand visibility and recall.

The ideal candidate will possess the following:

  • Experience in developing branded digital and social media content for platforms including but not limited to Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, and X.
  • Demonstrated ability to create compelling social media content, including excellent copy, that converts users on objectives related to campaign and organizational growth actions like acquisition of email leads.
  • Knowledge and experience with social media strategy, digital content creation, and community management.
  • Experience with content creation applications including Adobe Creative Cloud.
  • Proficiency in SEO tools such as Google Search Console and Google Tag Manager.

· Familiarity with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript is preferred for effective website analysis.

  • Proficiency in English is essential.

· Job Type: Freelance/ Part-time (10 – 12 hours per week). The majority of time will be spent on ACS related work (i.e. ~ 80% per week).

· Schedule / Hours: Flexible

· Pay: $20 - $25 / hr.

· Applications can be submitted via email to homecare@arcavia.ca

SCP is committed to promoting an inclusive, accessible environment, where all employees and customers feel valued, respected, and supported. We are dedicated to employing a workforce that reflects the diversity of our communities and members in which we live and serve.

Qualified applicants will receive consideration without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, or disability.

We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation.

Please note that due to the volume of applications, only those under consideration will be contacted for an interview.

Job Types: Part-time, Freelance

Pay: $20.00-$25.00 per hour

Expected hours: 10 per week

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Oakville, ON L6J 7L9

Application deadline: 2025-05-16

title: Digital Marketing Specialist
jobDescription:
Department: Marketing

​

Employment Type: Permanent - Full Time

​

Location: Canada (Remote)

​

Description

​

At OnePlan, we specialize in creating AI-enabled solutions that make strategic portfolio, financial, resource, and work management seamless. We help businesses bridge the gap between strategy and execution by offering solutions that boost business agility, streamline project management, and optimize resources.

​

What Makes us Unique?

​

What truly makes OnePlan stand out is our commitment to delivering powerful solutions and fostering a culture of collaboration. We combine robust analytics with a platform that integrates seamlessly into the tools businesses already know and trust. Our high-trust, team-focused environment allows us to innovate quickly and deliver solutions that drive meaningful results for our clients. We're passionate about exceeding expectations, working together to empower organizations to succeed in a rapidly changing business landscape.

​

About The Role

​

As a Digital Marketing Manager, you’ll take the lead on growing our digital presence through strategic SEO and PPC initiatives. This role is all about driving qualified traffic, improving visibility in search, and continuously optimizing to turn clicks into conversions. You’ll collaborate closely within our marketing team to ensure every digital touchpoint delivers measurable results.

​

What You’ll Do at OnePlan

​

  • Own and manage PPC campaigns across platforms like Google and LinkedIn - constantly testing, refining, and looking for ways to improve performance 
  • Write compelling ad copy and collaborate on landing pages that actually convert 
  • Keep a close eye on trends, performance data, and customer behavior to uncover what’s working and what’s not 
  • Brainstorm and test new digital growth strategies, from campaign ideas to creative experiments 
  • Run A/B tests and conversion experiments to optimize user experience and drive better results 
  • Work closely with internal teams to build and refine landing pages that support SEO and paid campaign goals 
  • Conduct regular technical SEO audits and implement best practices to keep us ranking 
  • Perform keyword research and optimize web content to make sure we’re showing up for the right searches 
  • Track campaign performance across channels—clicks, conversions, ROI—and turn that data into clear, actionable insights 
  • Analyze the full customer journey across touchpoints to identify drop-off points and improve the overall conversion funnel 

Our Ideal Fit

​

  • Experience working in a SaaS or B2B tech environment 
  • Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, or a related field 
  • Proven experience in technical SEO and paid advertising (Google Ads, Bing, LinkedIn) and managing successful PPC campaigns 
  • Deep understanding of search engine algorithms and digital performance marketing 
  • Strong analytical skills with experience using tools like Google Analytics, SEMrush, and Ahrefs 
  • Strong communication skills with the ability to present campaign results and strategy recommendations 
  • A growth mindset—curious, iterative, and always testing 
  • Familiarity with HTML/CSS, CMS platforms (like WordPress), and data analysis using Excel

Bonus Points

​

  • Knowledge of Microsoft ecosystem tools and how they integrate with marketing platforms 

More Reasons Why You Should Apply!

​

  • We’re a remote-first company with team members across the USA, Canada, UK, and India!
  • OnePlan has been recognized as the Global Microsoft Partner of the Year in Project and Portfolio Management in 2019, 2020, 2021, and 2022.
  • We’ve been named a "Strong Performer" in the latest Forrester Strategic Portfolio Management WAVE report.
  • OnePlan was recognized as Gartner’s Voice of Customer for Strategic Portfolio Management Report for 2024 and 2023. 
  • We offer comprehensive health, dental, and vision benefits, with additional insurance options.
  • Employer RRSP and 401K matching programs.
  • A fun, collaborative, and diverse environment with regular health and team challenges to keep things light and enjoyable!

At OnePlan, we are committed to equal employment opportunities regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or expression, or veteran status. We are proud to be an equal-opportunity workplace.

​

Upon receipt of an offer letter, candidates will be subject to a standard background check process.

​

Disclaimer: We’ll only contact candidates who have applied directly through our official channels. Any communication about job offers will always come from an email address linked to OnePlan Solutions, and we’ll follow our standard hiring process every time. You’ll never be asked for money or personal information during the interview process. If something feels off, don’t hesitate to reach out to us to confirm.

​

Ready to Apply?

​

Check out what it’s like to work at OnePlan and learn more about us at https://oneplan.ai/

title: Director, Content Strategy
jobDescription:
At Qualtrics, we create software the world’s best brands use to deliver exceptional frontline experiences, build high-performing teams, and design products people love. But we are more than a platform—we are the creators and stewards of the Experience Management category serving over 18K clients globally. Building a category takes grit, determination, and a disdain for convention—but most of all it requires close-knit, high-functioning teams with an unwavering dedication to serving our customers.

When you join one of our teams, you’ll be part of a nimble group that’s empowered to set aggressive goals and move fast to achieve them. Strategic risks are encouraged and complex problems are solved together, by passing the microphone and iterating until the best solution comes to light. You won’t have to look to find growth opportunities—ready or not, they’ll find you. From retail to government to healthcare, we’re on a mission to bring humanity, connection, and empathy back to business. Join over 5,000 people across the globe who think that’s work worth doing.

Director of Content Strategy

Why We Have This Role

As our business grows, so does the importance of how we tell our story. We are looking for a visionary Director of Content Strategy to define and evolve our content strategy across the entire customer journey. This role sits at the center of our brand, product marketing, and digital experience teams, driving a unified approach to storytelling that resonates with our audiences and accelerates business growth. By enhancing our content initiatives, you will help shape how we connect with our customers and reinforce our brand’s value in the marketplace.

​

How You’ll Find Success

  • As Director of Content Strategy, you will lead a high-performing team of strategists, writers, and producers, transforming content into a strategic lever for brand equity, customer engagement, and revenue.
  • You’ll bring a modern, audience-first mindset fueled by data, insights, and emerging technologies like AI to build content experiences that are timely, relevant, and high-impact.
  • This role is about more than writing great copy; it’s about building a system that consistently delivers meaningful content experiences that scale with the business and deepen relationships with our customers.
  • You'll foster innovation and creativity within the team, ensuring that content strategies evolve to meet changing market demands.
  • You will prioritize alignment with business objectives, collaborating across departments to maximize the effectiveness of all content initiatives.

How You’ll Grow

  • In this role, you will have the opportunity to mentor and develop your team, driving a culture of high performance and collaboration.
  • You’ll engage with various departments, enhancing your cross-functional leadership skills and understanding of content strategy at a global scale.

​

Things You’ll Do

  • Define and lead a global content strategy that drives brand affinity, customer education, and full-funnel engagement.
  • Manage and mentor a team of content strategists, writers, editors, and producers, fostering a high-performance and collaborative culture.
  • Own and evolve the company’s editorial voice and storytelling framework to ensure clarity, consistency, and resonance across all touchpoints.
  • Collaborate cross-functionally with brand, product marketing, demand generation, communications, events, and digital teams to align content with business objectives.
  • Guide the creation of diverse content formats including long-form articles, video, podcasts, social media, interactive tools, and in-product content.
  • Leverage AI and emerging technologies to scale content production, optimize personalization, and enhance performance.
  • Build systems and processes to support efficient, scalable, and high-quality content operations.
  • Use data and customer insights to drive content decisions, optimize performance, and demonstrate impact.
  • Ensure content is strategically aligned across regions, buyer personas, and customer lifecycle stages.
  • Advocate for audience-first thinking, positioning content as a growth engine within the broader marketing organization.

What We’re Looking For On Your Resume

  • 9+ years of proven experience in content strategy, preferably in a leadership role.
  • Demonstrated ability to manage and inspire teams, driving high performance and collaboration.
  • Strong understanding of brand storytelling and content marketing best practices.
  • Experience with data-driven decision-making and leveraging analytics to optimize content performance.
  • Familiarity with emerging technologies, including AI applications in content creation and distribution.

What You Should Know About This Team

  • Our team is passionate about storytelling and dedicated to creating impactful content that resonates with our audience.
  • We value collaboration, creativity, and innovation, and we believe in fostering an inclusive environment where every team member can thrive and contribute to our mission.
  • We can create campaigns that inspire our stakeholders, and ultimately, our desired audiences.
  • We think local and scale globally. We build plans and create campaigns that will be effective across geographies.

Our Team’s Favorite Perks and Benefits

  • Culture: A vibrant work environment that encourages collaboration, creativity, and work-life integration.
  • Team Engagement: Opportunities for team-building activities, company-wide celebrations, and a supportive community.
  • Health and Wellness: Competitive health coverage, wellness stipends, and an annual experience bonus to enhance your personal and professional life.

The Qualtrics Hybrid Work Model: Our hybrid work model is elegantly simple: we all gather in the office three days a week; Mondays and Thursdays, plus one day selected by your organizational leader. These purposeful in-person days in thoughtfully designed offices help us do our best work and harness the power of collaboration and innovation. For the rest of the week, work where you want, owning the integration of work and life.

Qualtrics is an equal opportunity employer meaning that all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, status as a protected veteran, or any other protected characteristic.

Applicants in the United States of America have rights under Federal Employment Laws:Family & Medical Leave Act,Equal Opportunity Employment,Employee Polygraph Protection Act

Qualtrics is committed to the inclusion of all qualified individuals. As part of this commitment, Qualtrics will ensure that persons with disabilities are provided with reasonable accommodations. If reasonable accommodation is needed to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and/or to receive other benefits and privileges of employment, please let your Qualtrics contact/recruiter know.

Not finding a role that’s the right fit for now? Qualtrics Insiders is the one-stop shop for all things Qualtrics Life. Sign up for exclusive access to content created with you in mind and get the scoop on what we have going on at Qualtrics - upcoming events, behind-the-scenes stories from the team, interview tips, hot jobs, and more. No spam - we promise! You'll hear from us two times a month max with fresh, totally tailored info - so be sure to stay connected as you explore your best role and company fit.

For full-time positions, this pay range is for base per year; however, base pay offered may vary depending on location, job-related knowledge, education, skills, and experience. A sign-on bonus and restricted stock units may be included in an employment offer, in addition to a range of medical, financial, and other benefits, based on eligibility criteria.

Washington State Annual Pay Transparency Range

$190,500—$318,000 USD

title: Graphic Design & Digital Marketing Specialist
jobDescription:
Full job description

4 Level Coach

Graphic Design and Digital Marketing Specialist

Candidates and Recruiters DO NOT contact 4 Level Coach directly. Please follow the steps to apply. All applications must go through Contractor Staffing Source.

​

Are you a creative, data-driven marketer with a passion for innovation? 4 Level Coach is on the hunt for a Digital Marketing Specialist who's ready to make a real impact! If you're excited by the idea of driving targeted marketing campaigns and working in a dynamic environment that values growth and fun, this is the role for you.

​

At 4 Level Coach, we're not just about business; we're about transforming lives and creating meaningful impact. Our mission is to revolutionize the home building and remodeling industry by helping businesses grow, streamline, and succeed. We foster a relaxed, non-corporate environment where innovation meets passion. If you're looking to join a team that's committed to excellence and growth, this might be the perfect fit for you. Learn more about us at: https://www.4levelcoach.com/

​

Why You'll Love Working Here:

​

We're passionate about transforming the industry and having fun while doing it!

​

Relaxed, supportive work environment

​

Opportunities for professional growth in a dynamic team

​

Great starting pay and potential for future advancement

​

As our Graphic Design & Digital Marketing Specialist, you'll be at the forefront of our marketing efforts. You'll create and manage campaigns across various platforms, from social media to email marketing, optimizing our digital presence and helping builders and remodelers streamline their strategies for success.

​

Key Responsibilities:

​

Develop and execute multichannel campaigns (email, social media, webinars, paid ads) to boost brand awareness and drive results.

Design engaging materials using tools like Adobe Creative Suite or Canva.

Use CRM platforms to manage client journeys, ensuring high engagement and retention.

Manage and optimize our Webflow-based website, build landing pages, and enhance the user experience.

Leverage SEO best practices to boost organic traffic and manage paid ad campaigns for maximum impact.

Create compelling content, plan webinars, and promote our Building Freedom podcast.

Cultivate relationships with industry influencers and collaborators to expand our reach.

Track campaign metrics, manage the marketing budget, and report on campaign effectiveness.

Qualifications:

​

4+ years in digital marketing, preferably in a B2B setting.

Proficiency in CRM systems (e.g., Go High Level, Active Campaign), SEO strategies, and design tools such as Adobe Creative Suite and Canva.

Knowledge of Webflow, with a strong grasp of user experience design, landing page development, and integration with CRM and marketing tools.

Ability to craft engaging, on-brand content across channels and apply strong storytelling skills to resonate with target audiences.

Demonstrated ability to analyze metrics, make data-informed decisions, and optimize campaigns based on performance insights.

Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple campaigns and projects effectively under tight deadlines.

Proven track record of adapting to fast-paced environments and responding to new challenges with creativity and initiative.

Experience working cross-functionally to align marketing initiatives with business objectives and support other team members.

Compensation:

​

Full-Time Position

Competitive salary at $75,000 - $85,000 per year, based on experience.

Hybrid position - Collaborate with us in person (Toronto/Burlington area) and remotely.

We're committed to your professional development.

3 weeks paid vacation, 5 sick days, and your birthday off!

Be part of a team dedicated to transforming lives and reshaping an industry.

If you're a driven marketer ready to make a difference, we'd love to hear from you! Please submit your resume, cover letter, and portfolio to join us on this exciting journey.

​

4 Level Coach is an equal-opportunity employer that celebrates diversity and inclusivity in our workplace.

title: Marketing Coordinator
jobDescription:
Are you motivated by using your talents to support others? Come work for one of BC’s top regulated colleges!

Established in 1903, Sprott Shaw College is a regulated post-secondary institution with 16 government-designated campuses across British Columbia. We take great pride in educating and preparing our students for rewarding and relevant careers in Administration, Business, Trades & Design, Nursing & Healthcare, and Child, Family & Community Support.

Sprott Shaw College is one of the oldest colleges in British Columbia with over 120 years of storied history. As an organization, we cultivate professional growth and positive working environments for our employees, which fosters our values of Community, Diversity, Integrity, Quality, Respect, and Results-Oriented.

Reporting to the Director of Marketing, the Marketing Coordinator is responsible for the development and coordination of marketing content, events, and other College-related initiatives ensuring the College’s successful marketing presence.

Responsibilities

  • Understand Sprott Shaw College’s corporate culture, programs, campuses, and target demographics

  • Coordinate with suppliers and vendors and manage stock of promotional items and other marketing-related products and services

  • Support the Director of Marketing in updating the website and CRM as required
  • Generate marketing reports as needed

​Performs other related duties as required

  • Writing experience is required

Skills

title: E-Commerce / Marketing Coordinator
jobDescription:
Hillsound Equipment Inc. (www.hillsound.ca) is a Vancouver-based manufacturer of hiking and outdoor equipment. We truly believe that our outdoor experiences continue to inspire personal growth, fulfillment, and strength in our community. For this reason, we strive to design products that will enhance the experience and create accessibility for anyone who desires to be outside no matter the season.

The Role

We are seeking a detail-oriented and innovative E-commerce and Marketing Coordinator to join our team and take charge of our digital marketing efforts. In this role, you will be responsible for managing our e-commerce platforms, executing targeted advertising campaigns, and collaborating with external agencies to drive brand growth.

You'll play a key part in ensuring our Shopify and Amazon storefronts are optimized for performance, while also identifying new opportunities to enhance customer engagement and increase sales.

This is a unique opportunity for an individual who is also interested in joining the outdoor industry. You are passionate about all things digital, e-commerce, and connected devices.

Primary Responsibilities

  • Assist in the planning and execution of customer journeys, including web and Amazon Project briefing and the coordination of production assets for various digital channels
  • Ensure campaigns and customer journeys are consistent across channels and touchpoints
  • Oversee the creation of discounts and promotions
  • Assist with the launch of campaigns and enhancements to the customer experience by inputting content into digital systems, such as Shopify and Amazon Seller Central Support reporting and analytics on an ongoing as well as identify actionable opportunities to improve e-commerce and digital marketing performance
  • Analyze sales and inventory to generate the sales trend and manage demand planning
  • Report on paid and organic search campaigns and initiatives
  • Manage product listings including listing strategy and product description
  • Setup and maintain products across Amazon, and other e-commerce channels
  • Manage all SEO efforts to maximize ROI, drive organic traffic and revenue
  • Ensure all elements on the Hillsound website are optimized to drive rankings and attract relevant traffic relevant traffic
  • Recommend changes to website architecture, content, linking, and other factors to improve SEO positions for target keywords
  • Drive traffic to our brand website via external sources
  • Monitor and manage active subscriptions for marketing tools and services
  • Ensure our website meets all technical requirements to drive rankings
  • Conduct research to inform digital strategy and operations
  • Ad-hoc troubleshooting and liaising with stakeholders to solve e-commerce issues
  • Generate, organize, and translate sales data to support the team
  • Other duties as assigned

Required Skills and Qualifications

  • 1+ years of in-depth e-commerce and marketing experience
  • Minimum 1 year of experience with Shopify or any other e-commerce platform
  • Minimum 1 year of experience with Amazon FBA Knowledge of e-commerce backend systems
  • A fundamental understanding of marketing channels and the role they play in a customer’s journey
  • Detail-oriented - you are able to execute content creation and campaign builds and make sure assets are delivered to specifications
  • Ability to work independently and take initiative – You should be capable of applying your own knowledge and experience to take on new challenges and solve problems without constant supervision.
  • You are self-motivated, proactive, and driven to tackle new tasks with a positive attitude, even in unfamiliar situations
  • Analytical, experience processing and analyzing data, creating reporting, and making data-driven recommendations
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills
  • Passion for the outdoors

The Benefits:

  • 4-day work week (office is closed on Wednesdays)
  • Opportunity to enjoy the outdoors through team hiking trips & excursions
  • Company product & industry discounts
  • Relaxed and dog-friendly office setting

Anyone who has a passion for the great outdoors and possesses the requirements above is welcome to apply! We thank all applicants; however, only those candidates under consideration will be contacted.

title: Paid Search Coordinator
jobDescription:
At Wonderist Agency, we guarantee you’ll have problems to solve. You’ll be dragged into projects in the 11th hour. And you’ll be expected to create and contribute to our ever-evolving processes. The work isn’t easy. It’s dental. And there’s plenty to go around.

We need a skilled PPC Coordinator with the flexibility to turn on your heels when priorities change, the thoughtfulness to do what’s in the best interest of the team, and the desire to always produce your best work. We do what’s right for the client, not just what’s easy. And that’s what makes our jobs gratifying. Along with unlimited PTO and a dog-friendly office, these are some of the reasons Wonderist Agency has been voted one of San Diego's Best Places to Work.

We are a fast-growing startup of over 40, and that means we’re agile. We’re able to try new things and experiment. It also means we make time to celebrate the work, each other when we hit our quarterly goals, or every Friday at 5 pm. Our Favorite dive bar, Desi’s, is just down the street and right across from Liberty Station, so we’ve got our location going for us.

Basically, we need someone who’s willing to work really hard and doesn’t get discouraged when a client or project is going off the rails. So if you’ve got a lot of grit and an equal amount of gumption, keep reading...

Job Description:

The PPC Coordinator will work with other members of the Paid Search Team to build, manage, optimize our PPC and Social Ads accounts. Driving quality conversions for our clients through Google Ads and Social Media Ads is at the heart of this role. Daily tasks will include but not be limited to managing request tickets, monitoring the launches in the pipeline for the department, providing QAs on ad accounts, completing tickets from Specialists and Client Success Managers, auditing existing campaigns and providing campaign analysis suggested optimizations.

Essential Functions:

The PPC Coordinator will be expected to know the ins and outs of PPC and Social Ads campaigns and how to best execute them. 

  • Understanding of PPC and Social Ads Strategy: Understanding of PPC and Social Ads strategy and digital marketing principles. Communicating campaign strategy to other members of the Paid Search team is an essential task for this role.
  • Landing Page Optimization: Optimization of new and existing web pages and stand-alone landing pages, using a variety of content management systems including HighLevel, Unbounce and Webflow.
  • Understanding of Google Suite: Comfortable with the full range of Google products from Google Ads, Google Analytics, Search Console, and Google Tag Manager, to Gmail and Google Drive.
  • Writing ad copy: Write compelling, conversion-centric, authentic ad copy for Google Ads and Facebook Ads. Using best practices and working within limitations depending on the platform. 
  • Campaign Reporting: You must be comfortable with learning our business intelligence dashboards, measuring the status and success of campaigns, and giving updates to the Paid Search Team.
  • Campaign Maintenance: Proactively research campaigns and highlight ongoing opportunities. Notifying PPC Specialists if something is not performing.

Core Competencies:

  • Intuitive Communication and Emotional Intelligence: Our office is constantly collaborating with clients and other departments. Being able to clearly communicate abstract concepts to other members of the paid search team is key in this position.
  • Deadline-driven: Overseeing project timelines and regularly reporting the status of projects is essential.
  • Organization: Timely communication inside our project management system inside and outside the Paid Search Team is essential.
  • Strategic: You will help identify opportunities and goals for clients and match those opportunities with the best strategies to meet their needs. In addition to coming up with your own marketing ideas, and working within the department on feasibility and execution.
  • Attention to Detail: We don’t allow details to fall through the cracks, so an advanced level of organization is crucial. Clients depend on us to deliver and maintain marketing campaigns that deliver consistent results. Fully-functioning, fully-optimized ad campaigns cannot exist without a dedication to detail.
  • Analytical: We measure everything we do by its ROI for our clients. You will need to learn not only building ad campaigns, but analyzing their performance and explaining the results in a straightforward and simple way.
  • Client-Focused Approach: What’s best for the client is at the forefront of what you do. Our main goal is to provide a marketing outlet for clients to increase their ROI. All decisions that are made on client accounts are for the betterment of the client’s overall practice goals.
  • Desire to Learn: The digital marketing landscape is always changing - our team stays up-to-date on the latest trends and updates, so we can deliver the best for our clients.
  • Task Prioritization: Ability to prioritize a large volume of client accounts, effectively managing resources to triage their needs for optimal client satisfaction. You will need to make decisions on which tasks and accounts take priority in certain situations.

Personality Musts:

Our perfect fit is someone who is a problem solver who understands the difference between “yes, and…” and “yes, but…”. You should have a hungry, scrappy personality with a positive attitude and aims to deliver. Working in the client service industry can mean going the extra mile, and we need someone who is always willing to rise to the occasion for our clients and puts forward beautiful work that exceeds client expectations. Someone who is detailed and organized, driven and displays vigor and comes ready to play every day. Confidence, self-discipline, and a congenial, curious mind are things we love and admire.

Minimum Qualifications

  • 1+ years of Google Ads experience
  • Up-to-date knowledge of current challenges facing the digital marketing industry
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Excel or Google Sheets

Bonus Points for Applicants With…

  • Agency experience
  • Experience in the healthcare industry
  • Social Ads experience
  • Basic understanding of HTML/CSS
  • Basic understanding of JavaScript
  • Bachelor’s degree in a related field (Marketing, Website Development, Digital Media, etc.)

Pay DOE

This job description is intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by persons assigned to this classification. It is not intended to be an exhaustive list of all job responsibilities, duties and skills required for this position. Wonderist Agency reserves the right to make alterations or exceptions to job descriptions, with or without notice.

title: Senior Content Marketing Specialist
jobDescription:
As a Senior Content Marketing Specialist, you’ll lead end-to-end content strategy and execution for a range of client brands—turning insights into content that ranks, nurtures, and converts. From SEO blog posts and case studies to email sequences and landing pages, you’ll create high-impact assets that support every stage of the funnel. Using AI-powered tools, you’ll analyse metrics, streamline workflows, and scale production without sacrificing quality.

‍

Designity is a Creative-as-a-Service (CaaS) platform that empowers businesses with a flexible, high-quality, and scalable alternative to traditional agencies and in-house teams.

Our unique model connects marketing and creative leaders with the top 3% of US-based creative and marketing talent, all led by experienced Creative Directors, Project Managers, and Marketing Strategists. Through our collaborative platform, we streamline the creative process, ensuring fast, high-impact execution without the inefficiencies of hiring in-house or the high costs of traditional agencies.

‍

Brands like Calendly, Kung Fu Tea, and Marriott Hotels trust Designity to handle their design and marketing needs—proving that there's a better way to scale creativity. Unlike in-house hiring, which is expensive and skill-limiting, or marketplaces like Upwork and Fiverr, which lack reliability and creative oversight, Designity delivers a seamless, results-driven approach that makes creative collaboration effortless.

‍

PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES:

  • Develop and execute content strategies and manage the content calendar across different channels.
  • Identify target audiences and buyer personas and craft engaging content that drives brand awareness, lead generation, and customer engagement.
  • Use AI tools to speed up research, optimize production, and boost ranking potential.
  • Write and edit conversion-focused content that addresses audience pain points and aligns with client positioning.
  • Collaborate with creative teams to align on brand voice and style guidelines.
  • Analyze performance metrics like traffic, engagement, and conversions and optimize accordingly.
  • Stay up to date on SEO, content, and algorithm trends, and proactively adjust tactics to keep client content competitive.

REQUIREMENTS:

  • 5+ years of experience in content marketing, backed by a portfolio of succesfull content that has ranked, driven traffic, and contributed to pipeline or conversions.
  • Deep understanding of content marketing strategy across the funnel.
  • Skilled in SEO-driven content creation, and with exceptional writing, editing, and messaging skills.
  • Experienced in analytics platforms (e.g. Google Analytics), content management systems (e.g. WordPress, Drupal), and SEO tools (e.g. SEMrush)
  • Proficient in using AI to improve quality, consistency, and performance of produced content.
  • Organized and confident managing multiple projects and editorial calendars.

BENEFITS:

  • Join a company at the perfect moment! Career making opportunity.
  • Work fully remotely full-time, from wherever you are.
  • Unwind during the flexible PTO, and company holidays.
  • Learn and grow with the best by becoming part of our exclusive Design Community.
  • Enjoy dedicated focus times designed to boost your productivity and creativity.
  • ‍

In order to be considered for this position, you need to complete the application process at www.designity.com/careers/senior-content-marketing-specialist

‍

Please do NOT follow up on your application. We will be in touch within 2-3 weeks if you're qualified.

At Designity, employment decisions are based on business needs, qualifications, and merit. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer, valuing diversity in culture, perspectives, skills, and experiences, which strengthens our team, clients, and products.

title: Webflow Designer & Developer (Remote - Raleigh, NC)
jobDescription:
Guidebook is a simple yet powerful event management software that lets you promote your event, manage registration and create an app in four easy steps with no technical skills required.

Thousands of organizations across the globe use Guidebook to create mobile applications for events, university campus open houses, venue guides, onboarding for associations, corporate training and so much more. These apps help people stay organized, connect with their peers, and access more information - but it doesn’t stop there.

Our mission is to support the people who bring people together - event organizers. Events have never felt more important in building connections and helping shape and change minds in today’s fractured world. We are a driven team on a mission to elevate event experiences for everyone – and believe we can accomplish that while highly valuing balance, togetherness, and fun!

ABOUT THE JOB:

You’re a hybrid thinker—a digital designer with a marketer’s mindset. You know how to make websites not just look good, but perform well. With a deep understanding of Webflow and a knack for intuitive user experience, you can confidently manage and optimize a site like guidebook.com, bringing both creative flair and strategic insight to the table. You’re equally at home collaborating with marketers, salespeople, and developers, and you’re excited to see your design choices translate into measurable business results.

In this role, you’ll take ownership of Guidebook’s web presence—rebuilding and maintaining our site in Webflow, supporting our martech integrations, and contributing to brand-aligned marketing design projects. You will play a central role in executing and iterating on digital strategies that drive awareness, conversions, and engagement.

Our ideal candidate blends strong visual design skills with technical chops. You understand how SEO, user experience, and design intersect—and you’re always looking for ways to improve site performance, conversion rates, and brand consistency. Experience in a fast-paced, collaborative B2B or SaaS environment is a big plus, especially if you’ve juggled multiple marketing initiatives at once.

WHAT YOU'LL DO:

  • Own and manage the Guidebook marketing website in Webflow, initially leading on a re-design and re-development project and then making updates and improvements as needed
  • Collaborate with marketers to build landing pages, optimize user flows, and support campaign launches
  • Apply technical SEO best practices to ensure discoverability and performance across pages
  • Manage the Guidebook Blog on WordPress
  • Use your HTML, CSS and Javascript skills to help manage our WordPress blog, and support important integrations
  • Proactively suggest and implement website improvements based on data, benchmarks, and user experience trends
  • Design visually compelling digital assets and maintain brand consistency across web and marketing materials
  • Contribute to the development of social and paid campaign assets where needed
  • Help connect web performance to marketing KPIs, including traffic, conversion rates, and SEO rankings
  • Manage projects alongside the marketing team within a structured project management process using two-week sprints in Jira
  • Bring a creative and analytical lens to problem-solving—balancing great design with measurable results

THE IDEAL CANDIDATE:

  • 3+ years of digital or marketing design experience, with a strong portfolio of Webflow design and development projects.
  • Experience building and implementing design systems in Webflow.
  • Demonstrated experience managing and optimizing a B2B marketing website
  • Proficient with HTML, CSS and Javascript - supporting our WordPress blog, and integrations with marketing platforms like Marketo and Chili Piper
  • Familiar with technical SEO, including on-page optimization, structure, and site health
  • Strong design sensibility and the ability to articulate a clear point of view based on data and brand goals
  • Experience analyzing site performance and translating insights into design and UX improvements
  • Collaborative and communicative—you enjoy working cross-functionally with marketers, sales, and leadership
  • Analytical yet creative—you’re eager to A/B test and refine assets to improve performance
  • Experience producing work through project management tools such as Jira, Asana, Monday, etc
  • Is able to work effectively with a manager based in a remote office
  • Bonus: Experience in a smaller B2B and/or SaaS company where you owned multiple design/development functions
  • Bonus: Experience with Google Tag Manager

WITHIN 3 MONTHS, YOU’LL:

  • Gain a solid understanding of Guidebook’s product, brand, customers, and website structure
  • Conduct a full audit of the website with recommendations for design, UX, and SEO improvements
  • Establish relationships across marketing, sales, and product teams
  • Begin contributing to new landing pages and website updates using Webflow
  • Participate in sprint planning and align your work within the team’s 2-week cadence
  • Understand the Guidebook tone and visual identity—and begin forming ideas for evolution
  • Identify low-hanging fruit to improve conversion rates and technical performance

WITHIN 6 MONTHS, YOU’LL:

  • Take full ownership of Guidebook’s Webflow site, managing updates and improvements with autonomy - including updates to our martech integrations
  • Take full ownership of Guidebook’s blog on WordPress, managing updates and improvements with autonomy
  • Regularly analyze and report on website performance—setting clear goals and benchmarks
  • Design and launch new campaign pages and test variations to improve engagement and lead gen
  • Partner with demand gen to create high-converting landing pages and performance-focused assets
  • Contribute to improving project workflows and creating reusable design components in Webflow

WITHIN 12 MONTHS, YOU’LL:

  • Establish a clear connection between your work and core marketing KPIs (e.g. SEO, conversion rates, engagement)
  • Standardize best practices for Webflow development, website QA, and marketing design operations
  • Build scalable systems to support growth—templates, automation, atoms/molecules
  • Play a leadership role in maintaining and evolving the Guidebook brand voice and visual identity
  • Drive measurable improvements in performance through data-informed design decisions
  • Be seen as the go-to resource for high-quality, performance-driven design within the marketing team

PERKS

  • 100% paid benefits: medical, dental, and vision
  • Short term and long term disability benefits
  • Unlimited vacation time
  • 401k program with matching benefit
  • Stock options
  • Make your workstation yours: desk customization (monitor; docks/stands; keyboards; whatever else you need to be productive)
  • Awesome annual company retreats!

EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER:

At Guidebook, we're committed to cultivating an inclusive work environment for everyone – and we know that's best achieved, in part, with a diverse workforce. We're always working on increasing diversity across the company. If you're looking to join a team that is smart, highly motivated, and also values work/life balance, we can't wait to hear how you can add to our growing culture!

We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, national origin, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any other federal, state or local protected class.

COMPENSATION:

The salary range for this role is $75,000-100,000 annually (including variable quarterly bonuses), depending on relevant experience.

​
